## How the GBC changed Deity worship in ISKCON | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### Deity worship, ISKCON history, GBC resolutions
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/k6TE3K_pRk4?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** historical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This system was given a stamp of approval and finality by the GBC in the year 1995."
**Summary:** The speaker outlines the GBC's resolutions in 1989 regarding Deity worship, the formation of a research committee, and the publication of the Pancharatra Pradipa in 1995, detailing the procedures for Aarati as described in the manual.

On March 11, 1989, ISKCON Mumbai Group's GBC passed two resolutions regarding Deity worship in ISKCON, Resolutions No. 65 and 66. Resolution No. 65 reads that the GBC body hereby requests the following devotees to research on the basis of Guru, Sadhu and Shastra to help establish ISKCON policies regarding Deity worship – Bhanuswami, Bhakti Vidyapurna Swami, Atma-Tattva, Jananivas, Ashtaratha and Krishna Kshetra. Jagadeesha Goswami is appointed to coordinate the research work. This resolution was followed by Resolution No. 66 which read that the Deity worship research group and the full GBC body must approve the contents of the ISKCON Deity worship handbook before publication. So, in 1989, the GBC felt that Deity worship policies need to be set and for that they need the help of Shastra. A committee was made as per this decision, which in the following years, underwent some modifications to the constituent members of the committee. This committee did its research and submitted its report to the GBC. The GBC finally published the Deity worship manual in the year 1995 under the ISKCON GBC Press calling it the Pancharatra Pradipa. In this Deity worship manual, the procedure for Aarati is described as follows. In addition to the worship of Srila Prabhupada in his Guru Puja, one should also honour him when performing Aarati by presenting the Aarati items to Srila Prabhupada after presenting them to one's own spiritual master, then with the consciousness that you are offering it on behalf of your spiritual master and with the blessings of Srila Prabhupada and Lord Chaitanya, offer it with the full number of circles listed below to the main Deity. After offering the incense to the main Deity, offer it as Prasada to the Lord's associates in descending order and to the Guru Parampara Sr. to Jr. This may be done with seven or three circles for each personality depending on time allowance. Then distribute it with one or three circles to the main Deity. This may be done with 7 or 3 circles for each personality depending on time allowance. Then distribute it with one or three circles to the main Deity. the assembled Vaishnavas as the Prasada of the Lord and his associates. So, as per the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai group, Aarati has to be offered in descending order. And how to offer the Aarati to the Deity? That is also described in the Pancharathra Pradipa, which says under the heading, how to offer each item as follows. Offer all the items, except the chamara and fan, by moving them in clockwise circles while ringing a bell with your left hand above waist level, fixing your attention on the Deities. Lamp. How to offer a lamp? That is described here. Lamp. Offer four circles to the lotus feet, two circles to the navel, and three circles to the Lord's face. Then offer seven circles to the Lord's whole body. Then the second one. Argya in a conch. Offer three circles to the Lord's head and seven circles to the whole body of the Lord. Then pour a small amount of the offered Argya into the Visarjaniya Patra, small throat pot, before proceeding to offer Argya to the next personality. Aarati Argya, plain or scented water. Then comes the cloth. Offer seven times around the Lord's body. Flowers. Offer seven times around the Lord's body. Chamara. Wave before the Lord a suitable number of times. Fan. Wave before the Lord a suitable number of times. So in 1995, the GBC approved the Manual for Deity Worship where when a devotee offers Aarati, he presents the Aarati to his Guru, then to Srila Prabhupad as a matter of honoring them and taking them home. blessings from them. Then the arathi actually starts by offering it to Krishna first. 4, 2, 3, 7 formula for lamparathi, 3, 7 formula for water, etc. The key point is that the arathi offering begins with offering to Krishna on behalf of your spiritual master and then the prasadam is offered to Radharani, then to Mahaprabhu, then to Nithyananda Prabhu and then to Srila Prabhupada and then to your Guru. This system was given a stamp of approval and finality by the GBC in the year 1995.

---
### Deity worship, Srila Prabhupada's instructions, Deviation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/k6TE3K_pRk4?t=317
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, you see, no 4-2-3-7 formula. Straight seven circles. That's it. That means, the 4-2-3-7 aarti introduced by the GBC in the 90s is an outright deviation in deity worship."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the GBC's 1995 manual with Srila Prabhupada's original instructions on Aarati, citing letters where Prabhupada prescribed offering everything first to the Guru and using seven circles for each offering, arguing that the GBC's changes constitute a significant deviation.

Now, we must note that Srila Prabhupada had already established a systematic and elaborate system of Deity worship in ISKCON. Srila Prabhupada not only established the standards of Deity worship, Srila Prabhupada also ran it under his personal supervision in all the temples in ISKCON from 1967 till 1977. That is 10 straight years, if not longer. And this same system that Srila Prabhupada established continued for another 18 years or so till 1995 when the Pancharatrapadeepa came out. So, what was the system that Srila Prabhupada established for offering aarti to the deities? What was the original system? Was it the same as that instructed by the GBC in the Pancharatrapadeepa? Let us look at a few of Srila Prabhupada's directions in this regard. In a letter to Aniruddha dated 14th of November 1968, Srila Prabhupada instructed, aarti is performed at one and a half hour before sunrise to awaken the deities. Each offering is made by moving it in seven big circles, starting at the lotus feet of the Lord and going clockwise round. So, you see, no 4-2-3-7 formula. Straight seven circles. That's it. That means, the 4-2-3-7 aarti introduced by the GBC in the 90s is an outright deviation in deity worship. Are you stunned? Hold your breath because the worst is yet to come. Next comes the sequence of offering the aarti. In another letter to Giriraj, dated 28th of May 1972, Srila Prabhupada instructed, so far your question regarding deity worship during aarti, everything should be offered first to the Guru. So, Srila Prabhupada instructed to offer first to Guru, not to Krishna. And in his letter to Hemavathy dat1st april 1970, Srila Prabhupada gave even greater clarity on the sequence or order of doing the aarti. Srila Prabhupada wrote, You have asked some questions. The order of worshipping is 1st spiritual master 2. then Lord Chaitanya, then Radha Krishna. As in the mantras or Bendeyam prayer. So, here, very clearly, Srila Prabhupada gave an even greater clarity on the sequence and the position of doing the aarti. Very clearly, Srila Prabhupada said that we have to follow the sequence of Vandeyam prayers. And what is the sequence of Vandeyam prayers? First Guru, which is Srila Prabhupada, then Savadhootham or Sri Nithyananda Prabhu, then Lord Chaitanya, then Radha and then finally Krishna. It is the ascending order, not the descending order which the GBC has now implemented in ISKCON. Descending way is the procedure which was established by Srila Prabhupada for Deity worship in ISKCON from 1967 till 1977. And ISKCON continued with that system till in the late 80s or 90s, the GBC decided to change it entirely. Both the sequence of offering Aarati by completely reversing the order to begin the Aarati with Krishna. And also the number of times from just 7 straight times to 4, 2, 3, 7 etc. This is an outright outrageous deviation by the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai group. What audacity they must be having to change the Deity worship system. Is there no one in ISKCON to question such pernicious deviations by the GBC? Don't the GBC understand they are playing with something? Are they playing with something more dangerous than dynamite with the Deities? What is everyone doing in ISKCON? Are they all sleeping? As if this itself were not enough.

---
### Bhoga offering, Guru Parampara, Deviation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/k6TE3K_pRk4?t=575
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Srila Prabhupada instructed that we cannot offer anything directly to Krishna. We must first offer to Guru."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to critique the Pancharatra Pradipa, focusing on the Bhoga offering procedure. He contrasts the manual's instruction to offer Bhoga directly to the deities with Srila Prabhupada's instructions to offer through the spiritual master, citing letters where Prabhupada emphasizes the Guru's role as the intermediary.

Brace yourself for even worse deviation by the GBC. Look at what they did with the Bhoga offering procedure to the Deities. When it comes to Bhoga offering, the Pancharatara Pradipa of 1995, published by the ISKCON GBC Press states. Dates. section called Offering the Bhoga. Offer the Bhoga and drinking water with a hand gesture to each deity, showing the plate and saying, My dear Lordships, Gaur Nithay, Jagannath Baldev Subhadra, etc., please accept this offering of nice food. My dear Lordships, Gaur Nithay, Jagannath Baldev Subhadra, etc., please accept this beverage. Then the Pancharatra Pratipa states, While ringing a bell, chant the Pranam prayers to your spiritual master three times, begging permission to assist him in serving the deities. After offering everything to the deities like this, the Pancharatra Pratipa instructs the devotee as follows, Leave the deity room and chant the Gayatri mantras. You may chant all the Guru-given mantras at this time. Meditate on the Lord Yudhisthira. After the offering is completed, the bhoga is offered to the deities and then permission from the guru is sought to assist him in serving the deities. When removing the prasadam plates, the Pancharatra Pratipa instructs, Offer the lord s prasada remnants to the spiritual master and the Lord s associates. After this the plates are to be removed. So, the system here too. to offer the bhoga first to the deities and then offer their prasadam to Shaila Prabhupada and to the Diksha Guru. Descending process once more. Now again, what was the system Shaila Prabhupada set up and ran for 10 years or more in ISKCON and which continued in ISKCON till about 1995? Let us look at what Shaila Prabhupada instructed about offering bhoga to the deities. In this letter to Arundhati dated June 16, 1969, Shaila Prabhupada instructed, regarding your question about offering prasadam, whatever is offered to the deity, actually it goes through the spiritual master. The spiritual master offers to Lord Chaitanya and Lord Chaitanya offers it to Krishna. Then Radha Krishna eats or Jagannath eats. Then Chaitanya Mahaprabhu eats. Then the spiritual master eats and it becomes Mahaprasadam. So, when you offer something, you think like that and chant the Gayathri mantra and then everything is complete. At last, ring the bell, take out the plate and wipe the place where the plate was kept. The GBC in the Pancharatra Pradipa instructs the devotee to meditate that Krishna is eating the bhoga. Whereas, Shaila Prabhupada instructed to meditate on offering to the guru and the guru will offer to Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Chaitanya Mahaprabhu will offer to Krishna. The GBC has completely changed the way of offering. This is a shocking deviation by the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai group. In another letter to Jathurani, dated 15 February, 1968, Shaila Prabhupada wrote, regarding offering food. Shaila Prabhupada says the custom is to offer the foodstuff first to the spirit. Show of hands is the custom of offering foodstuff. This is also a custom. spiritual master. We cannot do anything directly. The spiritual master accepts the offering on behalf of his disciple and offers the same to Krishna. After Krishna's eating, the spiritual master eats it and then the devotees take it as Mahaprasadam. This is the system. Everything is offered to the spiritual master first with the prayer Nama Om Vishnupadaya. So, you see, Srila Prabhupada instructed that we cannot offer anything directly to Krishna. We must first offer to Guru.

---
### Guru, ISKCON, Deviation, Hrithik initiations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/k6TE3K_pRk4?t=944
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Srila Prabhupāda gave such crystal clear instructions that we cannot offer anything directly to Kṛṣṇa, but we must offer to Guru first."
**Summary:** The speaker expresses shock at the GBC's deviation, questioning their motives and suggesting it might be related to the issue of fallen Gurus. He connects this deviation to the disobedience of Srila Prabhupada's July 9th order regarding Hrithik initiations, emphasizing that his group strictly follows Prabhupada's instructions and offers everything to him first.

This is beyond shocking. This is mind-boggling. This is mind-numbing. Doesn't the GBC have any fear of Kṛṣṇa and Srila Prabhupāda's anger? Doesn't the GBC have any fear of Guru aparādhā? Doesn't the GBC have any fear in behaving as if they are better than their Guru Mahārāj, Srila Prabhupāda? Why on earth did the GBC have to make such a catastrophic change? Why did they deviate so monstrously? Was it because they knew? That their Dīkṣa Gurus are not bona fide and offering through them would be outrightly rejected by Kṛṣṇa and so they reversed the whole procedure, offering directly to Kṛṣṇa? In most Guru fall-downs in ISKCON, the GBC tries to suppress the news, brushing it under the carpet. When the news finally breaks, several months of the Guru's deviation and fall-down would have already passed. So, naturally, questions will be raised by all the disciples of the fallen Guru as to what happened to all the bhoga and ārati that was being offered in the temples by His disciples from the time the Guru fell-down. Was it because the GBC had no answer for such questions that they found a quick-fix solution? Offer to Kṛṣṇa directly instead of offering to the Guru. And offer the Guru only prasādam. The Guru is the last person in the descending order in the line. So, even if Guru has fallen down, no problem. Is that the reason why the GBC made such an unbelievable change in the process of Deity worship? Look at what one deviation of the GBC, one disobedience of the GBC in 1978 to Śrīla Prabhupāda's July 9th order has led to. They are playing with the spiritual lives of the devotees without any qualms. Downright disgusting and appalling. How can the GBC even walk with their heads held high after such preposterous deviations? This deviation of the GBC is beyond atrocious. It is actually criminal. But it serves as a stark reminder of even just one deviation. How even just one disobedience of Śrīla Prabhupāda's one instruction, the July 9th, 1977 order to implement the Hrithik system of initiations, just that one deviation can cause so much havoc and destruction of the spiritual lives of thousands of devotees. Devotees of our group, Śrīla Prabhupāda's ISKCON group, can rest assured that we strictly follow the standard of offering ārati and bhoga exactly as Śrīla Prabhupāda instructed. Everything is offered to Śrīla Prabhupāda first because we follow the July 9th order of Śrīla Prabhupāda to implement Hrithik initiations. Hence, we happily and blissfully offer every ārati, every bhoga directly first to Śrīla Prabhupāda because Śrīla Prabhupāda is, Śrīla Prabhupāda was and Śrīla Prabhupāda will always, eternally be the sole dikṣa guru for every single devotee in our group, Śrīla Prabhupāda's ISKCON group. Hare Kṛṣṇa, Viśva Guru, Śrīla Prabhupāda ki Jai!

---

## Impact of Srila Prabhupada’s Books | Sri Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### detachment, sense gratification, Prabhupada's mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nN4HR0hGBq0?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "All simply vanished into thin air."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the pursuit of material pleasures through education and career, and how that passion vanishes upon encountering Prabhupada's teachings.

We spend our whole life trying to acquire material pleasures. We go to college, study very hard. Why? Because I want to get good marks. So we work hard, two years, three years, four years, doing our engineering or whatever, five years in case of doctors, study hard, write all the exams, day and night, burn the midnight oil and get good marks. Finally, at the end of it, we get a degree. And finally, when we come to the final year, we start preparing for the campus interviews. And then when you get a job, you start working hard. I should work in such a way that at the end of the year, my annual appraisals are good and I get either an increment or a promotion. But suddenly, when we read Prabhupada's books, what happened? All that passion we had for studying, getting good marks, getting a good job, what happened to all that? All simply vanished into thin air. No more you are interested. How? Because when we come in touch, we come in touch with the instructions of Prabhupada. Prabhupada is directly bestowing the mercy of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu upon us. In our heart, we lose all the consideration, all the attraction, whatever value we had for sense gratification in this material world.

---

## Important Advice by Rishabhadeva | Sri Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa | ISKCON Bangalore
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### sense gratification, karma, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/x1puAzc6nNk?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "My actual factual position is to be a servant of the servant of the servant of Krishna."
**Summary:** Rishabhadeva advises his sons about the futility of sense gratification and the importance of understanding one's true position as a servant of Krishna.

Rishabhadeva tells his sons that in this material world, people are engaged in all kinds of sinful activities because they want to satisfy their senses. In my opinion, this is not at all a good thing to do, because this body for which the people are toiling so hard is asat. It is temporary. At the point of death, one has to give up all the results of one's hard work and one has to go. And in the midst of this, we engage in so much enmity with other people. They don't think about the repercussions of their action. It can never satisfy them. After a certain point, they become frustrated, become very renounced, and they start saying, "I am the supreme Brahman." After some time, they will again come back to the material conception. We have to understand that my actual factual position is to be a servant of the servant of the servant of Krishna.

---

## Is July 9th letter Tamala Krishna Goswami’s? | Sri Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### ISKCON history, Ritvik system, July 9th directive
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gFqRGmuQp_4?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Srila Prabhupada, on 9th of July, 1977, issued a written directive to the whole of ISKCON, detailing a Rithvik system of initiations to be followed in ISKCON."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the July 9th directive, its context, and Tamalkrishna Goswami's role in its creation and dissemination.

Hare Krishna! Vishwaguru Srila Prabhupada ki Jai! Srila Prabhupada, on 9th of July, 1977, issued a written directive to the whole of ISKCON, detailing a Rithvik system of initiations to be followed in ISKCON. In this directive, Srila Prabhupada appointed 11 devotees to act as his representatives, also called Ritviks, to initiate disciples on his behalf. The newly initiated disciples would be Srila Prabhupada's disciples. This directive, July 9th directive, was actually the outcome of a GBC meeting with Srila Prabhupada on 28th of May, 1977, where they asked Srila Prabhupada what would be the system of conducting initiations in ISKCON, particularly at the time when Prabhupada was no longer with us. Tamalkrishna Goswami, who was Srila Prabhupada's secretary at that time, typed out the directive on July 9th, 1977, and he took Prabhupada's signature on it, and he sent it out to all the GBCs and temple presidents at that time.

---
### Guru system, ISKCON controversy, Tamalkrishna Goswami
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gFqRGmuQp_4?t=71
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Several years later, Tamalkrishna Goswami claimed that the July 9th letter was his letter, not Srila Prabhupada's."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how the 11 Ritviks were elevated to Diksha Gurus after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance and Tamalkrishna Goswami's later claim that the July 9th letter was his own, not dictated by Srila Prabhupada.

However, after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance in November of 1977, in the year 1978, all the 11 people appointed as Ritviks were elevated by the GBC to become Diksha Gurus. They claimed at that time that Srila Prabhupada had appointed or selected these 11 people as Diksha Gurus. Several years later, Tamalkrishna Goswami claimed that the July 9th letter was his letter, not Srila Prabhupada's. He said that Srila Prabhupada did not dictate or write the letter, but he did. So, it was his letter, not Srila Prabhupada's. So, he gave this explanation to justify his and others. He said that he was a Guru. Then, there is no other order by Srila Prabhupada for anyone to become a Guru in ISKCON. The letter was written by me. It was not dictated by Prabhupada. It was signed by me, and it's my letter. It's not Prabhupada's letter. So, it's just astounding to me that somehow my letter turns out to be the final order. And it was my suggestion that Prabhupada approved it, because I thought it would add weight to it, so people would know it was authorized. And it's just astounding how this becomes the basis of a new understanding of the Sampradaya. This video of Tamal Krishna Goswami is cited by the supporters of the self-appointed Guru system in ISKCON, saying that July 9th directive is not the final order, since it didn't come from Srila Prabhupada at all. It was Tamal Krishna Goswami's letter. So, it appears like ISKCON is now disowning the July 9th Directive from Srila Prabhupada.

---
### Ritvik system, Authority, ISKCON leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gFqRGmuQp_4?t=178
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only Srila Prabhupada had such powers. Therefore, July 9 directive is undeniably from Srila Prabhupada only. It cannot be from Tamal Krishna Goswami."
**Summary:** The speaker argues that even if the July 9th directive was written by Tamalkrishna Goswami, he lacked the authority to appoint Ritviks or direct the GBC and temple presidents, thus reinforcing the directive's origin from Srila Prabhupada.

Actually, with this argument, the self-appointed Gurus are honestly shooting themselves in the foot. The devotees of ISKCON Bangalore, of course, we are not buying this argument. However, for the sake of argument, let us hypothetically assume that the July 9th Directive was not Srila Prabhupada's letter, but it was Tamalkrishna Goswami's. Well, if this was Tamalkrishna Goswami's letter, then the question comes, who appointed the 11 people as Ritwiks? Was it Tamalkrishna Goswami? Did Tamalkrishna Goswami have the powers to appoint Ritwiks in ISKCON? Did he really have that kind of power? The answer is obviously a resounding no, because what was Tamalkrishna Goswami? Tamalkrishna Goswami, at that time, was just a mere secretary to Srila Prabhupada, nothing more. He had no power to appoint anyone in ISKCON. Forget about Ritwiks. The second question that crops up is that the July 9th Directive was addressed to all the GBCs and temple precedence. If this was not Prabhupada's letter, but Tamalkrishna Goswami's letter, did Tamal Krishna Goswami, just as a simple secretary, did he have the powers to direct the entire GBCs and temple presidents, which means the entire movement? Is there any order from Srila Prabhupada that said that he was conferring such powers on Tamal Krishna Goswami? In reality, none. Only Srila Prabhupada had such powers. Therefore, July 9 directive is undeniably from Srila Prabhupada only. It cannot be from Tamal Krishna Goswami.

---
### GBC resolution, Guru selection, ISKCON history
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gFqRGmuQp_4?t=293
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, in this resolution by the GBC, and mind you, Tamalkrishna Goswami was one of the GBC members. So, he has also been part of this resolution. So, in this resolution, it is clearly mentioned by the GBC, in which Tamalkrishna Goswami was also a member, that the 11 people were selected by Shela Prabhupada."
**Summary:** The speaker analyzes GBC resolution number 16 from 1978, highlighting that it stated the 11 Ritviks were selected by Srila Prabhupada, and questions why Tamalkrishna Goswami didn't object at the time if he believed the July 9th directive was his own.

Moreover, the 11 people named in the July 9 directive became diksha gurus in 1978. How? By claiming that Srila Prabhupada appointed them, selected them. The GBC resolution number 16 of 1978 stated, the GBC will consider each year at Gaurapurnima the appointment of new spiritual masters to be approved by a three-fourth vote. However, for 1978, no new spiritual master shall be appointed other than the 11 selected by Shela Prabhupada. So, in this resolution by the GBC, and mind you, Tamalkrishna Goswami was one of the GBC members. So, he has also been part of this resolution. So, in this resolution, it is clearly mentioned by the GBC, in which Tamalkrishna Goswami was also a member, that the 11 people were selected by Shela Prabhupada. At that time, why did Tamalkrishna Goswami not tell them? Why did he not tell them? That these are not selected. They are selected by Prabhupada. They are selected by me, because this is my letter, not Prabhupada's letter. Why did he not tell at that time? But these 11 names, because you have to understand that these 11 names, which are mentioned here in this resolution, appeared only in the July 9th, 1977 directive, and that too as Ritwiks, not as Gurus. And there is no other document from Shela Prabhupada where these 11 names are mentioned.

**Summary:** The speaker points out the contradictions between the GBC's 1978 resolution and their later confession in 1999, where they admitted to allowing the unauthorized zonal acharya system to evolve.

All that they are achieving is to raise serious questions on their own unauthorized, self-appointed Guru system. ISKCON, GBC, in fact, contradicted their resolution number 16 of 1978 later on. And they confessed that Shela Prabhupada, as they claimed in resolution number 16 of 1978, was a Guru. So, Tamalkrishna Goswami is following Tamalkrishna Goswami in his confession. As they had claimed in that, it was not true. Shela Prabhupada did not select or appoint any Gurus in ISKCON in 1978. So, this confession came in 1999 from the GBC when they passed this resolution number 403, which reads, whereas Shela Prabhupada established ISKCON in 1966 and prior to his disappearance in November 1977, he stated in his will that there was no GBC is the ultimate managerial authority for the society. Unfortunately, shortly after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance, the GBC body, not Prabhupada, the GBC body allowed for an unauthorized zonal acharya system to evolve in ISKCON, elevating 11 exclusive successor diksha gurus.

In 1999, the GBC is saying the GBC body allowed for elevating 11 diksha gurus. So, in this resolution, the GBC is clearly saying that they elevated 11 successor diksha gurus. ### Ritvik system, July 9th directive, Srila Prabhupada's order
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gFqRGmuQp_4?t=661
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So, please do not be swayed by such illegitimate claims by Tamal Krishna Goswami or the self-appointed Gurus of ISKCON. Please remain firm in following Srila Prabhupada's July 9th directive for Diksha in ISKCON by Rittviks or representatives of Srila Prabhupada."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reaffirming the validity of the July 9th directive as Srila Prabhupada's order, urging devotees to follow it and reject claims by Tamalkrishna Goswami and self-appointed gurus, advocating for the Ritvik system.

The reality, what is the reality? The reality is that there was a discussion with Srila Prabhupada on 7th of July 1977, regarding who all could be appointed as his deputies or as his representatives. Srila Prabhupada mentioned all the 11 names in that conversation. And as Srila Prabhupada's secretary, and the Chairman of the GBC Entertainment Company, those names are all mentioned. It was not as that in the session 12 years prior, and it was not as that in the session 13 years before. Tamal Krishna Goswami's duty to put it in writing and inform everyone about Srila Prabhupada's order. So, based on this order of Srila Prabhupada and based on the May 28th, 1977 meeting of the GBC with Srila Prabhupada, the July 9th directive was typed out by Tamal Krishna Goswami. Prabhupada saw that, Prabhupada signed it and then it was sent out to all the GBCs and TPs. Since Srila Prabhupada had signed it, all the GBCs and TPs who received the directive accepted it as Srila Prabhupada's order. Nobody thought it was Tamal Krishna Goswami's order. Actually, we devotees of ISKCON Bangalore also even today accept that it is Srila Prabhupada's order, it is not Tamal Krishna Goswami's order. So, please do not be swayed by such illegitimate claims by Tamal Krishna Goswami or the self-appointed Gurus of ISKCON. Please remain firm in following Srila Prabhupada's July 9th directive for Diksha in ISKCON by Rittviks or representatives of Srila Prabhupada. Everyone in ISKCON is a disciple of Srila Prabhupada. No one is authorized to be Diksha Guru in ISKCON. Diksha Guru Srila Prabhupada Ki Jai! Vishwa Guru Srila Prabhupada Ki Jai! Hare Krishna! Vishwa Guru Srila Prabhupada Ki Jai!

## Is the Ritvik System Just a Reaction to Gurus Falling Down? | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### Rithvik system, Guru fall downs, ISKCON history
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9OU9FT_qOfg?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This argument, in essence, maintains that the Rithvik system was a new invention developed by some devotees who were victims of some unsavory dealings by leaders or gurus in ISKCON."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the argument that the Rithvik system arose as a reaction to guru fall downs and mistreatment of devotees in ISKCON, and states that this argument attempts to discredit the Rithvik system.

One argument against the Rithvik system of initiations is that it came out as a reaction to some gurus falling down in ISKCON. This argument in its various versions attempts to discredit the Rithvik system. To get to the bottom of this argument, we will have to go back in history. The GBC banned all of Srila Prabhupada's letters. Within one year, since 1978, devotees started developing doubts. The devotees in ISKCON had been deceived. Only the GBCs at that time knew of the July 9th directive. The guru fall downs are a direct reaction of disobeying Srila Prabhupada's July 9th directive to conduct initiations in ISKCON via Rithviks on behalf of Srila Prabhupada. Hare Krishna Vishwaguru Srila Prabhupada Ki Jai. One argument against the Rithvik system of initiations is that it came out as a reaction to some gurus falling down in ISKCON. Another version of this argument is that the reaction came due to the problems arising out of the zonal acharya system. Yet another version of this argument is that the reaction came due to the misbehavior of some leaders of ISKCON with rank and file devotees, mistreating them. This argument, in essence, maintains that the Rithvik system was a new invention developed by some devotees who were victims of some unsavory dealings by leaders or gurus in ISKCON. This is a very patronizing approach. Either the victims or at least those who sympathized with the victims came up with this new theory of Rithvikism. To put it bluntly, it was the invention of some disgruntled devotees of ISKCON. Gurus or leaders fell from grace or dealt harshly with some devotees and as a result, the devotees came up with the idea of Rithvik initiations. This argument in its various versions attempts to discredit the Rithvik system as a post-1978 invention by some disgruntled devotees who wished well for the organization, but the solution they came up with was not correct. So, this was a patronizing approach, as if we sympathize with these devotees, but they were incorrect in the institution. Therefore, the Rithvik solution that they came out with. So, if you are sympathizing with the devotees, the victims of all these mistreatment within ISKCON, then why are you supporting the expulsion of such devotees which is encapsulated by the GBC as a law in ISKCON? If you are really sympathetic towards the devotees, expulsion should not be the solution. Anyway, to get to the bottom of this argument, we will have to go back in history to the year 1978.

---
### GBC, Srila Prabhupada, Rameswar Swami
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9OU9FT_qOfg?t=177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But after Jadurani Mataji heard the tape, it was clear to her that she and the rest of the devotees in ISKCON had been deceived."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts the events of 1978, including the GBC's claim about 11 selected Diksha Gurus, the banning of Srila Prabhupada's letters, and the growing doubts among devotees, culminating in Jadurani Mataji's investigation and discovery of the deception regarding the appointment of gurus.

In March of 1978, the GBC in their Resolution No. 16 lied to all the Vaishnavas of ISKCON that Srila Prabhupada selected 11 devotees to become Diksha Gurus. The GBC themselves have admitted to this in their apology to the institution in 1999 in Resolution No. 403. But in 1978, when they lied that 11 devotees had been selected as Diksha Gurus, all the devotees naively believed them. A few, whoever asked where Srila Prabhupada had selected them, were met with a standard response, there is an appointment tape. That tape, however, was never released. So, devotees left it at that. Moreover, we have to understand, in the aftermath of Srila Prabhupada leaving the world, all the devotees must have been in a state of shock and immense grief. Naturally, in that state of anguish, they couldn't be expected to look at things too critically. To add to this, the GBC banned all of Srila Prabhupada's letters in ISKCON. No devotee in ISKCON could read any of Srila Prabhupada's letters. Can you imagine that? It was forbidden. So, there was no way any devotee could lay his hands on the July 9, 1977 Directive. They simply did not know that such an order existed. However, within one year since 1978, as the dust started settling in the horizon, devotees started developing doubts. In just two years, by 1980, Jadurani Mataji was convinced that these Gurus were not what they claimed to be, to be pure devotees. There were some mutually disrespectful dealings between two of the 11 Diksha Gurus, which Jadurani Mataji happened to witness. That made her draw this conclusion that they could not be pure devotees. At that time Jadurani Mataji was serving as one of the top book distributors in Los Angeles temple and Los Angeles temple was also the headquarters of BBT at that time. Rameswar Swami, who was one of the 11 Diksha Gurus, was heading the Los Angeles temple and the BBT at that time. So, Jadurani Mataji went to Rameswar Swami and from Rameswar Swami, she demanded proof about the appointment of the 11 Diksha Gurus by Srila Prabhupada. She wanted to hear the appointment tape. Rameswar Swami was unable to evade her and since he was in charge of BBT, he had access to all the tapes. And on her determinedly strong persuasion, he reluctantly gave Jadurani Mataji the recording of the May 28th GBC meeting with everyone. This is what they were calling as the appointment tape at that time.

---
### Rithvik initiation, GBC pressure, Tamal Krishna Goswami
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9OU9FT_qOfg?t=378
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, we can see that the first incident of Rithvik initiations was conducted in ISKCON in the year 1980."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Jadurani Mataji's confrontation with Rameswar Swami, Rameswar Swami's subsequent Rithvik initiation ceremony, the GBC's reaction and pressure on Rameswar Swami, and Tamal Krishna Goswami's declaration about Srila Prabhupada appointing only Rithviks.

But after Jadurani Mataji heard the tape, it was clear to her that she and the rest of the devotees in ISKCON had been deceived. They had been duped. She confronted Rameswar Swami, who had no answers to her indignant questions. Backed with guilt, a highly repentant Rameswar Swami, what did he do? He removed his Vyasasana from the temple and he stopped this Guru Puja in the Los Angeles temple. A few days later, Rameswar Swami oversaw an initiation ceremony in the Los Angeles temple for a few devotees. During that initiation ceremony, in the class, this was in the year 1980, he declared that he was only a devotee. He was only a representative of Srila Prabhupada and all of the devotees who were getting initiated, they were disciples of Srila Prabhupada. Some of those devotees who were initiated in that ceremony till today, consider themselves disciples of Srila Prabhupada only, not of Rameswar Swami. Anyway, the moment the GBC came to know of this, a team of them landed up in Los Angeles. And they berated Rameswar Swami. They threatened to remove him from BBT and to expel him from ISKCON, unless he withdrew the Rithvik initiation. Because this initiation what he conducted was actually a Rithvik initiation. Poor Rameswar Swami, under duress, called the devotees and announced that he was withdrawing his earlier statement and that the initiates were his disciples. And Jadurani Mataji, she was physically expelled from the temple. So, we can see that the first incident of Rithvik initiations was conducted in ISKCON in the year 1980. Coincidentally, in the same year, in a separate and unrelated incident, Tamal Krishna Goswami declared in the Topango Canyon discussions, the famous Topango Canyon discussions, he declared that, Shaila Prabhupada appointed only Rithviks and not Diksha Gurus. So, in both these cases, we see that Rameswar Swami, who conducted the first Rithvik initiation in ISKCON after 1978, as well as Tamal Krishna Goswami, who declared that Shaila Prabhupada appointed only Rithviks and not Diksha Gurus. We see that both of them were not victims of any sort of improper behavior, nor were they at the receiving end of the zonal acharya system or any guru fall downs. In fact, they were themselves zonal acharyas. They were not some nondescript disgruntled devotees. They were Diksha Gurus and zonal acharyas. They were GBCs and top leaders in ISKCON.

---
### July 9 directive, Rithvik system, ISKCON authorization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9OU9FT_qOfg?t=576
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Rithvik system has nothing to do with the guru fall downs. Whether gurus fell or not, irrespective of that, the Rithvik system is the only system authorized by Shaila Prabhupada for carrying on initiations in ISKCON after his Thiro Bhava."
**Summary:** The speaker argues that Rameswar Swami and Tamal Krishna Goswami's support for the Rithvik system was not due to personal grievances but because they knew it was Srila Prabhupada's order, as outlined in the July 9 directive, and concludes that the Rithvik system is the only authorized system for initiations in ISKCON after Srila Prabhupada's departure.

They obviously cannot be victims of themselves and their own system. Then, why did they come up with the Rithvik system? In reality, both of them independently raised the Rithvik initiation system because neither of them invented or came up with the Rithvik system. But instead, they both knew that this was what Shaila Prabhupada had ordered on July 9, 1977. The Rithvik system of initiations was Shaila Prabhupada's order. In fact, only the GBCs at that time knew of the Rithvik system and the July 9 directive. No one else in ISKCON knew about it because the letters were banned until much later after 1987. So, historical evidence does not support the theory that the Rithvik system of initiations was invented as a reaction to the problems of the zonal acharya system or the guru fall downs or due to the mistreatment of some devotees by some ISKCON leaders. Historical evidence clearly favors the fact that the Rithvik system of initiations was specifically ordered by Shaila Prabhupada on July 9, 1977, particularly for that time when Shaila Prabhupada is no longer with us, as it is clear from the May 28 GBC meeting with Shaila Prabhupada which is the precursor to the July 9 directive. For a detailed analysis of the May 28 meeting and the July 9 directive, please see the link below in the description. So, to conclude, from the history of ISKCON, we can see that the claim that the Rithvik system was a reaction to the guru fall downs and excesses of the zonal acharya system falls flat on its face. The Rithvik system has nothing to do with the guru fall downs. Whether gurus fell or not, irrespective of that, the Rithvik system is the only system authorized by Shaila Prabhupada for carrying on initiations in ISKCON after his Thiro Bhava. Even if no gurus ever fell down in ISKCON, our stance will not change. In fact, rather than the Rithvik system coming up as a reaction to the guru fall downs or excesses of the zonal acharya system, the fact is the reverse. The guru fall downs and excesses of the zonal acharya system are a direct reaction of disobeying Shaila Prabhupada's July 9 directive to conduct initiations in ISKCON via Rithviks on behalf of Shaila Prabhupada. So, the claim that Rithvik system of initiations is a reaction to guru fall downs is simply a desperate figment of imagination in order to discredit Shaila Prabhupada's July 9 order and it is nothing but a feeble attempt to give credence to the unauthorized guru system in ISKCON. In reality, the July 9 directive alone stands as the sole, unrivaled authorization of the system of Rithvik initiations in ISKCON. Hare Krishna! Vishwaguru Shaila Prabhupada ki Jai!

---

## Living in Harmony with Nature | Sri Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### harmony with nature, sense gratification, exploitation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NZ949aKmV84?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The earth produces enough to satisfy the needs of a man, but not the greed of a man."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Vedic civilization's focus on spiritual advancement with the modern world's exploitation of nature for sense gratification, emphasizing the consequences of greed and the importance of living in harmony with natural laws.

Even though big, big cities were there in the estuaries in the Vedic times, still the whole city would be very beautiful and very pleasing to walk around. The breeze, the plants, the fruits, the trees, everything would be very pleasing. Actually, human society is supposed to live in this manner, in harmony with material nature. And in the Vedic times, the consciousness of the civilization was how to utilize this human form of life, advance in spiritual life. Whereas in the current day world, the whole consciousness of the civilization is how to exploit material nature and maximize my sense gratification. If we live according to the laws of material nature, there is ample opportunity for sense gratification, but not for unlimited, unrestricted sense enjoyment. In a limited, restricted way, in the manner it is mentioned in the Vedas, sense enjoyment is there, not that it is not there. But then, as it is said, the earth produces enough to satisfy the needs of a man, but not the greed of a man. Now, the whole human civilization has become so greedy for sense gratification that all the laws of material nature are discarded. And we try to exploit and exploit and exploit material nature. Because of what happens? Material nature also starts reacting. Material nature also starts reacting.

## Lord Krishna's love for Vidura | Sri Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### love, devotion, acceptance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yfv1wo37TbU?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This is the love of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Krishna visiting Vidura and accepting a banana peel offered with love, contrasting it with Duryodhana's opulent feast, to illustrate Krishna's unconditional love and acceptance of devotion.

When Krishna came to Hastinapura, he did not go to Duryodhana's palace. Instead, he went to Vidura's house. And what did Vidura offer him? Vidura made him sit in one place. Then he brought some bananas. He did not bring any dry fruits. He did not bring badam and walnut and all. He just brought some simple bananas. He peeled the bananas, threw away the banana fruit, and gave the banana skin to Krishna because Vidura was absorbed in looking at Krishna. He forgot which is fruit, which is skin, and unknowingly, he gave the peel to Krishna. Krishna took that banana peel and ate it. Look at Krishna's love and affection. Can you or I do it? If somebody whom we love, suppose somebody has a girlfriend, loves her very much. He goes to her house. She takes a banana peel and gives it. Will you eat? But look at Krishna. He is thinking, you know, he is offering me with so much love. How can I refuse? Let me eat it. And Krishna is eating. Krishna refused the luxuries of the palace, went to the simple household of Vidura. Krishna refused the big feast that Duryodhana had kept. He took the banana peel from Vidura's hands. This is the love of Krishna.

## Lord Krishna's sweetness attract even Lord Vishnu | HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### responsibility, dharma, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xSJJHtg7D9A?t=47
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Don't worry, I am there. Please don't think that kshatriyas are not there to protect the brahmanas. I am there. I will give you all protection. I'll see that your next child doesn't die."
**Summary:** A brahmana repeatedly laments the death of his newborns in Dwaraka, blaming the Yadu kings. Arjuna, present during the ninth instance, vows to protect the brahmana's next child.

A child, but unfortunately, as soon as the child was delivered, it died. So the brahmana came to the Dwaraka Palace, and he condemned all the Yadu Dynasty Kings, saying that in the presence of the father, how is the sun dying? Because in the olden times, the king used to be responsible to ensure that there were no untimely deaths within the kingdom. People could die only of old age, out of natural causes. If any of the citizens dies in his youth when his father is still living, that means the king has become adharmic. So then the king is no longer fit to rule. So the brahmana came here and accused all the Yadu Kings in front of Krishna, Balaram, and Ugrasena. He condemned, he criticized all of them. What is this? You are all big, big people sitting here, and in front of you, my son is dying. How is it possible? That means you are doing some adharma. Krishna smiled and kept quiet. Then, in due course of time, the brahmana's wife gave birth to a second child, and even that child died upon birth. Again, he came to the court of Dwaraka, and he criticized all the Kings of the Yadu Dynasty, and he went back. This happened again and again and again. Like this, it happened eight times. And then the ninth time also, the child died upon birth. So the brahmana came and very bitterly criticized all the Yadava Kings. Krishna, Balarama, Pradyumna, everybody he criticized. So that ninth time, Arjuna happened to be there, and when Arjuna heard this, he got shocked. All the Yadu Kings, they had become used to it because this is not the first time it is happening again and again it is happening, and Krishna is keeping quiet, so they are also keeping quiet. But Arjuna got shocked. What is this? He's coming and criticizing all the Kings. So he got up and told, he told the Brahmana, 'Don't worry, I am there. Please don't think that kshatriyas are not there to protect the brahmanas. I am there. I will give you all protection. I'll see that your next child doesn't die.' So in due course of time, the Brahmana said okay.

### vow, pride, failure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xSJJHtg7D9A?t=177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "After you know keeping aside all Krishna Balarama all those people I believed you I am a fool like this the brahmana said"
**Summary:** Arjuna boasts of his abilities, even claiming to have defeated Lord Shiva, and vows to protect the brahmana's tenth child. Despite Arjuna's protective measures, the child vanishes upon birth, along with Arjuna's arrows.

All become personalities. Pradyumna, Samba, then Krishna, Balarama himself, Krishna himself are sitting here. They are not saying anything. You are saying you will protect my child. How is it possible? Arjuna said, 'No, no, no, no. I've got pashupatastra from Lord Shiva. I have defeated even Lord Shiva. I am this, I am that,' and all those kind of things he started telling about himself. So he said, 'Don't worry, I will protect, and if I am not able to protect your child, then I'll give up my life.' So Arjuna took this vow. So then Brahmana said, 'Okay, if you are ready to protect, I am fine.' So then, in due course of time, the Brahmana's wife, the time came for the Brahmana's wife to deliver the 10th child. So immediately, the Brahmana called Arjuna. Arjuna till then he was staying in Dwaraka only. He did not go back to Indraprastha, why? Because of his promise that he would protect this Brahmana's child. So immediately, Arjuna took up all his bow and arrow and all and went to the Brahmana's house, and then he started shooting arrows for protection. He invoked the pashupatastra, everything, so that any enemy comes, the arrow would kill all of them. All of them. And then the child was born, and as soon as the child was born, what happened? The child and all of Arjuna's arrows, everything disappeared, and the brahmana came and said, 'I came and believed you.'

### vow, divine plan, Krishna's intervention

**Summary:** Arjuna, bound by his vow, searches the universe for the brahmana's lost child, failing in his quest. He prepares to immolate himself, prompting Krishna to intervene and reveal a deeper purpose behind the events.

After you know keeping aside all Krishna Balarama all those people I believed you I am a fool like this the brahmana said. So then Arjuna thought what has happened. So Arjuna got very you know he has given a vow to the brahmana he cannot break his vow a kshatriya cannot break his vow so he said no no no no no I'll find out where your son is and I'll get him back and immediately he took his chariot and then he started traveling. He went to Indraloka, he went to Brahma Loka, he went to Yama Loka, and he went up to Brahma's planet, up to Brahma Loka. There also he could not find the sons of the Brahmana. And then he went to rasatala, patala, everywhere, all the planets he traveled, but he could not find the brahmana son. Finally, Arjuna was frustrated. He came back, and he said, 'I could not find your son, so as per my vow, I am going to kill myself.' So he started organizing to sit in yoga asana and immolate himself. He started preparing all this while Krishna was silently watching what was happening. Let us see the fun. Then when Krishna saw that Arjuna is going to kill himself, immediately Krishna, 'Hey, wait, wait, wait, wait. I am there now. Why are you doing all these things?' So this is what happens, you know, devotees of Krishna, they will go and take some vow or some kind of endeavor, they will big endeavor they will take up and finally they will not be able to do it then Krishna will be forced to come and fulfill that vow. So Krishna said, 'Why did you take up this vow like this?'
