### yadava lineage, vasudeva, devaki, lord krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E0_4ECBJB48?t=700
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "And this Devaki married Vasudeva. That is how they became the parents of the real hero of the Mahabharata, which is Lord Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker returns to the Yadava dynasty, tracing the lineage from Yadu to Satvata, Andhaka, Vrishni, Ahuka, Ugrasena, Shurasena, Anakadundubi (Vasudeva), Devaka, and Devaki, ultimately leading to Devaki marrying Vasudeva and becoming the parents of Lord Krishna.

Now we come back to the Yadava dynasty. After a few generations after the king Yadu came a king called Satvata. This Satvata was also a great devotee of the Supreme Lord. And Satvata had two sons, Andhaka and Vrishni. This Andhaka, after a few generations after him came a king called Ahuka. And this Ahuka had two sons. The first son was Ugrasena. And this Ugrasena had nine sons and five daughters. And Ugrasena's first son was Kamsa. And on the other side, Ahuka's other son, Ugrasena's brother had seven daughters. Now Satvata's another son was Vrishni. A few generations after Vrishni came Shurasena. And this king Shurasena, he had ten sons and five daughters. His ten sons were headed by Anakadundubi. Anakadundubi was his eldest son. Now Ugrasena's daughters, Kamsa's sisters, they married the younger brothers of Anakadundubi. And Ugrasena's brother, his daughters married Anakadundubi. And this Anakadundubi was none other than the same Vasudeva as whose son Vishnu had informed Brahmaji that the Supreme Lord himself is going to appear as his son. And the brother of Ugrasena was Devaka. And Devaka's daughters were headed by Devaki. And this Devaki married Vasudeva. That is how they became the parents of the real hero of the Mahabharata, which is Lord Krishna.

### swayam bhagavan, lord krishna, incarnations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E0_4ECBJB48?t=828
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is not an incarnation. Krishna is the Supreme Personality of God and Himself."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that Krishna is not an incarnation but Swayam Bhagavan, the Supreme Personality of Godhead himself, the source of all other incarnations, referencing the Srimad Bhagavatam's description of various incarnations.

In fact, in the description in the Srimad Bhagavatam of the various kinds of incarnations, like we have discussed earlier, Leelavataras, Yugavataras, Gunavataras, Purushavataras, there is a list of almost twenty-two incarnations which is given in the Srimad Bhagavatam. And it mentions that this is just a broad list of the incarnations of the Supreme Lord. The actual number of incarnations of the Supreme Lord are uncountable, are innumerable. And in the list of the incarnations, the Purushavataras, the various Vishnu incarnations, the Leelavataras like Narasimha, Meena Avatara, Varaha Avatara, all these things are described. Lord Krishna is also described there. And then, after describing all the incarnations, the Bhagavatam says, All these twenty-two incarnations that have been described, they are all expansions or plenary portions or portions of the plenary portions of the Supreme Lord. But then, Krishna is not an incarnation. Krishna is the Supreme Personality of God and Himself. Just like Vishnu had informed Brahmaji, Sakshad Bhagavan Vasudeva Grihe. The Supreme Lord Himself will appear in the house of Vasudeva, as the son of Vasudeva. Therefore, Krishna is not an incarnation. He is the Supreme Personality of God and Himself. He is Swayam Bhagavan. This is what the scriptures have informed us, have revealed to us. And all incarnations, whether it is the Purushavataras, Leelavataras, Lord Vishnu, Lord Ramachandra, Narasimha, Vamana, all the incarnations are coming from Lord Krishna ultimately. Lord Krishna is the fountainhead of all incarnations.

**Summary:** The speaker introduces another character from the Yadava dynasty, a daughter of Shurasena who was given to his childless cousin brother and raised by him, hinting that she will play a central role in the Mahabharata story, setting up the next episode.

Now, this is a description of the entire Yadava dynasty. Now, in the Yadava dynasty, the father of Vasudeva, Shurasena, he had a cousin brother. And this cousin brother of Shurasena did not have any children. He was issue-less. Therefore, he requested Shurasena that you have got so many sons, your next child who is there, you please give it to me. I would like to rear that child as my own. And so, to Shurasena was born one girl child. And this girl child, Shurasena handed over to his cousin brother saying that you take care of this child. And that cousin brother very happily took that child and started rearing her as his own daughter. And this daughter of Shurasena who was being taken care of by her father, who was the cousin brother of Shurasena, she grew up to become one of the central characters of Mahabharata. Who was this daughter of Shurasena and what was the role she had to play in Mahabharata? Please join us in our next episode to know about this.

The burning question that must be running in all of your minds, what happened with Suryumna, is what we will be learning about today. We will start today's episode with an incident related to the Yadava dynasty. In the beginning, Lord Brahma, when he was creating this material world, he created many sages. Once, when Brahmaji was conducting a fire sacrifice for Varuna, from the fire sacrifice he begot a son by name Bhrigu. From this Bhrigumani, after a few generations, after Bhrigumani came Shukracharya. Shukracharya became the priest of the Asuras, and Shukracharya had two sons, Shanda and Amarka, and he had one daughter, Devayani. Shukracharya was serving as a chief priest of a king by name Vrishaparva, and this Vrishaparva had a daughter by name Sharmishta. Now, because of the death of Devayani, Shukracharya often used to come to the palace of Vrishaparva and conduct all his sacrifices, poojas, rituals, etc. The family of the king, as well as of the chief priest, were quite close, and so Sharmishta and Devayani, they became close friends. Once it so happened that Sharmishta, because she was a princess, she had many, many friends. Along with them, she was on a leisure stroll around the palace gardens. Accompanying her, Devayani was also there. While they were strolling around the palace garden, they came upon a nice pond, and so they all thought, let us take a dip in the pond, and all of them took off their clothes and jumped into the water, and they were frolicking around in the water, splashing water against each other. In the midi, by mistake, Sharmishta picked up one of the clothes of Devi Ani, and she was about to wear them when Devi Ani saw this, she became wild. Devi Ani's nature was that she was quite an innocent girl, but she had a short temper, and she flew into a rage and started the using uncharitable words against Sharmishta. She said, Devi Ani said, hey, what is this? You are trying to wear my clothes. This is exactly like a dog coming into a sacrificial arena and trying to steal away the ghee meant for the sacrifice. And so she also retorted. She said, I don't tell me all these things. My father is whatever Dakshina he is giving. You and your family are subsisting based on that. Your father is a beggar in my father's court. And like this, Sharmishta started retorting. Now this became a big ego battle between the two of them, and they started bitterly fighting with each other. And finally, Sharmishta pushed Devayani into a well, and she called all her friends and walked away from there. Devayani got trapped like this, and there is nobody else in the garden there, and she is without clothes, and she has fallen into the well, and she did not know what to do, and so she was stuck like this. ### transformation, attraction, devotional service

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "One month we are having a king, next month the same king has become a queen."
**Summary:** The speaker shifts back to the story of Sudhyamna, who turns into a woman, marries Buddha, and has a son named Pururava. Vasishtamuni arranges for Sudhyamna to alternate between male and female forms monthly, which displeases the citizens. Eventually, Sudhyamna hands over the kingdom to Pururava and goes to the forest for devotional service.

We will come to that shortly. We come back now to what happened with Sudhyamana. Sudhyamana and his ministers, the moment they entered the forest, all of them turned into women as per whatever Lord Shiva had said. And they started looking at each other. Hey, what happened to you? Your whole body has changed and they are looking at themselves, my god, I myself have also changed and all of them realized that they have all become women. Now they could, they could not understand why this has happened and what is the solution for this, what to do, they were in a quandary. Now, not knowing what to do, they all returned and sudhimna, because now he had turned into a girl, he became a beautiful young girl. It so happened that Somadeva's son, Buddha, happened to once see sudhimna who was in a girl's body, and he got attracted by her beauty and he proposed to her, why don't you marry me? Sudhimna, now because he was in a girl's body, his mind was also like that and he also got attracted to Buddha and so she accepted his proposal and both of them got married and they eventually they had a son and the name of this son was Pururava. After this, now Vasishtamuni, who was the one who had prayed initially and converted Ila into a boy for Viva, so Vasishtamuni, when he heard that s Perquè had again become a girl, he felt very unhappy and so he conducted a sacrifice and by the fire sacrifice he could make an arrangement where sudhimna would get back his masculinity once a month. That means every alternate month sudhimna would become a girl and the next month he would become a man and like this he spent the rest of his life alternating between a man and a woman. And like this, he was ruling over the kingdom. But the citizens were not very happy. One month we are having a king, next month the same king has become a queen. So, the way they address the king, the way they approach, everything changes every month. They have to adjust themselves in their mind also. Now, this was not very, this was not something which all the citizens were happy with. Eventually, when Puru Rava had grown up sufficiently, Sudhyamna handed over the kingdom to Puru Rava and he went to the forest to execute devotional service.

Now, Puru Rava became the king and this Puru Rava, he eventually had his sons and one of his sons was Yayati. And Yayati succeeded Puru Rava to the throne. And this Yayati made his power, his capital on the banks of the Vaitharani river in what is present day known as Odisha. And this place came to be known as Yayatipura and then later it became Yayapura and finally now today it is known as Yajapur. It is on the banks of river which is known as Vaitharani and this is in Odisha. Now Yayati, once from his kingdom, he was going out on an excursion and he was strolling around and going through various gardens and all that. He came across a well in one garden and he was thirsty, he thought let me drink some water and he approached the well. And when he looked into the well, he found a beautiful young girl caught naked inside the well. No prizes for guessing who this is. This was Devayani. Having looked at her, having understood that she has somehow fallen into the well, Yayati took compassion upon her and he removed his upper clothes. He gave those garments to Devayani to wear and he pulled her out of the well. Okay. Now, when Devayani was rescued like this, naturally you would have seen in all Bollywood movies that is the entry for the hero, right? The heroine is being harassed by some goons and the hero makes a dashing entry and he saves the heroine and the heroine falls in love with him. ### caste, marriage, hesitation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6uU6VkZSVtE?t=695
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If already by being born in a Brahmana family or automatically a Brahmana, then what is the need for all this list of twelve qualities?"
**Summary:** Yayati is hesitant to marry Devayani because of caste considerations, specifically the concept of Pratiloma. The speaker then questions the rigid caste system based on birth, pointing out that the Shastras emphasize qualities and qualifications over birth.

But then when she proposed to Yayati that, oh hero, you have rescued me and I am very much obliged to you and I would like to marry you, Yayati was a little hesitant. Why was Yayati hesitant? Because he, according to the Vedas, the person of a man of a lower caste marrying a girl of a higher caste is called Pratiloma. It is generally not preferred. Okay. A man of one caste carrying, marrying a woman of a lower caste is, is not, it is not incompatible, it is not undesirable and so that is generally accepted. Generally the man from a lower caste would not marry a woman of a higher caste. Not that it was forbidden or that if they would marry like this, if there was an inter-caste marriage, there would be some honor killing. Those are, those were definitely not accepted. Definitely not the considerations. The consideration was something else. What was Yayati's consideration based on the, so why was he considering the caste? It appears from this as if the caste system as we know it today based on the birth was prevalent those days. Actually if you go by the Shastras, if you look at the Mahabharata, in the Mahabharata there is a description of the twelve qualities of a Brahmana. Now if the caste is by birth, then why is the list of qualities required? Why is qualification needed? If already by being born in a Brahmana family or automatically a Brahmana, then what is the need for all this list of twelve qualities? The fact is the list of twelve qualities itself indicates that it is, caste is not by birth. You have to qualify yourself to be called a Brahmana.

---
### caste, qualifications, Garbhadana Samskara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6uU6VkZSVtE?t=811
**Verse References:** BG 4.13
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The four varnas or castes of Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya, Shudra are created according to the guna and karma, qualities and propensities of work for the people."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes the Bhagavad Gita and Srimad Bhagavatam to support the idea that caste is determined by qualities and work (guna and karma), not birth. He explains that during Vedic times, people ensured that children were born with the qualities of their caste through marriage within the same caste and the practice of Garbhadana Samskara.

Lord Krishna also says in the Bhagavad Gita, that caste is not by birth. You have to qualify yourself to be called a Brahmana. Lord Krishna also says in the Bhagavad Gita, which is again part of the Mahabharata, that chatur varnyam maya srishtam guna karma vibhagasaha. The four varnas or castes of Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya, Shudra are created according to the guna and karma, qualities and propensities of work for the people. In the Srimad Bhagavatam also it is stated, yasyayallakshanam proktam pumso varnabhi venjakam yad anyatra api drishyetah. Tattena yada anyatra api drishyetah. Meaning that if a person is born in a particular family, let us say he is a born in a Brahmana family, but he exhibits Kshatriya qualities then he has to be called A Kshatriya, not a Brahmana. There are examples like this Vishwamitra Muni, Vishwamitra Muni was born as a Kshatriya. He was, he was a king and as long as he was executing the responsibilities, activities of a thing, he was called a Kshatriya. The moment he gave up the activities of a Kshatriya, the activities of a sage he became a brahmana within the same lifetime he changed from kshatriya to brahmana like that many instances are there sons of kshatriyas being born and they become brahmanas now these kind of instances are also there in the shastras so then why if the vedas are clear that a brahmana kshatriya vaisya shudra is not as per birth but by qualification then why this consideration of birth like we are seeing about the ayati thinking like this the the the reply is very simple it is during the vedic times the people would ensure that in a brahmana family brahmana will be born the kshatriya family kshatriya would be born vaisya family vaisya would be born by following two simple things the first thing is by marrying within the same caste they would ensure that the characteristics of that particular caste could be passed on to the next generation just like you see even in if we take a crude example that is not of course compare human beings with animals but even if you look at animals there are various breeds like for example in cows you take concrete you have whole stream you have cybal etc as long as you don't crossbreed the characteristics will be maintained in a similar fashion in a similar fashion to maintain the the characteristics of that caste to the qualifications whatever are there to pass it on to the next generation the people would marry within the same caste but that alone would not ensure that the next generation would have all the qualities of that particular caste the second most important criterion for this was the second most important practice that used to ensure that the qualities would go on would get passed on uncontaminated from generation to generation was was the practice of Garbhadana Samskara.

---
### Varna Sankara, devotion, spiritual elevation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6uU6VkZSVtE?t=990
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The whole purpose of the Varnashrama system was to please Lord Vishnu."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that if Garbhadana Samskara is not performed, the children are considered Varna Sankara or Shudra. He emphasizes that the purpose of the Varnashrama system is to please Lord Vishnu and that devotion to Vishnu is more important than birth in a high caste.

By these two practices, they would ensure that the next generation would have the characteristics of the parents. If the Garbhadana Samskara is not performed, then the children who are born are considered Varna Sankara or Shudra. For example, we have the great example of Kashyapamuni, he was a Brahmana. He and his wife Diti begot two sons, Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashyapu. But because the sons were begotten without undergoing the process of Garbhadana Samskara, the two sons were not considered Brahmanas, they were not even considered part of the Varnashrama system, they were not even considered Shudras, they were called Asuras. In fact, this is another thing which we have to understand. The whole caste system, what we currently see of as giving certain privileges to one caste and depriving others of the lower castes of it, was not actually the purpose of the Varnashrama system or the caste system. The whole Varnashrama system, as it is explained in the Vishnu Purana, Varnashmacharavata Purushena Parappuman Vishnurarabhyate Nanya Tattosakarana, the whole purpose of the Varnashrama system was to please Lord Vishnu. And therefore, in the Puranas, it is stated that Shudra Bhagavad Bhakka Kepi Bhagavato Tamaha. Sarva Varneshu Teshu Varneshu. Beshudra Yena Bhakta Janardhanye. Those who are part of a particular caste, if they are born as a Brahmana or a Kshatriya or as a Vaisya, but they are not devotees of Janardhana, Vishnu or Krishna, then they are to be considered Shudras. On the other hand, those who are, who may be born in a Shudra family, but if they are devotees of Lord Vishnu, then they are to be considered higher than even Brahmana. This is being said. This is what the Shastras are saying. That means, the whole purpose of the caste system, maintaining the Brahmanas, Vaishyas, Kshatriyas, Shudras, all these things, was meant to, ultimately, it was meant to elevate a person spiritually and bring him to the point of becoming a devotee of Vishnu. And if that purpose was not served, being born in a Brahmana family or a Kshatriya family had no meaning. Anyway, so, this, now,

---
### responsibility, curse, resolution
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6uU6VkZSVtE?t=1128
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Sharmishta should become my maidservant for life along with all her friends because they are the ones who put me into such an embarrassing condition."
**Summary:** The speaker returns to Yayati's hesitation, explaining that he felt marrying Devayani would prevent her from having Brahmana children who could guide society spiritually. Devayani goes to her father, Sukracharya, who becomes angry. Vrishaparva pacifies Sukracharya, and Devayani demands that Sharmishta become her maidservant. The episode ends with the question of whether Devayani will be able to marry Yayati.

Yayati's consideration was that he was a Kshatriya and now if he married Devayani who was a Brahmana, then what would happen? The children who would be born would be Kshatriyas only. His thinking was, now, if Devayani were to marry another Brahmana, then they would have Brahmana children who are Brahmanas with all those 12 qualifications, can guide the society spiritually and they can become great spiritual masters. So, that is a far greater responsibility in the society than what a Kshatriya can play. If he married Devayani, then that opportunity would be lost. That was his consideration, Yayati's consideration. Now, when Yayati refused like this, Devayani went to her father, Sukracharya. When she went to her father, Sukracharya, he became very angry. And so, he immediately took his daughter and went to her father. He went to Vrishaparva. Vrishaparva, he had heard what had happened with Sharmishta and Devayani and he also, his people came and told him, now, Devayani is coming with her father and Sukracharya is in a livid mood. And Vrishaparva understood, now, in this mood, if Sukracharya comes, he is going to give some curts and we are all going to suffer. The moment he came across Sukracharya, he immediately fell at his feet and he supplicated him with apologies and told him, and tried to pacify him. And Vrishaparva told Sukracharya, by mistake, something has happened. Whatever punishment you are going to give to my daughter, we are willing to accept. Kindly, please be merciful unto us. So, then Sukracharya also calmed down and he told, okay, I am not going to curse you or anything like that. Let Devayani tell what kind of amends she wants Sharmishta to make and you take care of that. I will be satisfied with that. Then Devayani told, Sharmishta should become my maidservant for life along with all her friends because they are the ones who put me into such an embarrassing condition. Vrishaparva immediately agreed. And now, so that particular issue was resolved. Now, the second issue was not yet resolved. Yayati had refused Devayani and Devayani had fallen in love with Yayati. She wanted to marry him only. Now, what to do? How would Sukracharya solve this? Was Devayani, able to marry Yayati or did she have to marry someone else? Join us in our next episode to know about this. Thank you for joining us today. Hare Krishna Jagat Guru Shri Ila Prabhupada Ki Jai Nitaayegor Premanande Hari Haribol

### Rama-rajya, Ideal Society, Absence of Misery
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TFLSGROR_jg?t=114
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The pangs of old age and disease were unknown to the citizens."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the ideal conditions during Lord Ramachandra's reign, highlighting the absence of miseries, natural calamities, and premature death.

The entire earth was ruled over by Lord Ramachandra. He was rid of all kinds of miseries. There were no natural calamities. The weather was always pleasant, with the summers and winters appearing like springtime, without excessive heat or excessive cold. The earth always produced grains, fruits, and vegetables abundantly. No one ever went hungry. The pangs of old age and disease were unknown to the citizens. No son died in the present of his father, meaning everyone lived long lives and no one died young. Not only that, there was no death for anyone who did not want it. So these are all the descriptions of how Lord Ramachandra ruled over.

### Materialism, Ravana, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TFLSGROR_jg?t=273
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Despite all this economic and military and personal power, what happened? Ravana was quickly destroyed along with his life breath."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Rama-rajya with Ravana's Lanka, highlighting that economic and military power are insufficient for happiness and that Ravana was destroyed despite his wealth and power because he abducted Sita Devi.

The secret is not economic development; it is not military or political power. Ravana, in fact, had all these. His Lanka was called Svarna Lanka, why? Because every house in Lanka was cladded with gold. The richest of the people in the world today clad their houses with Italian marble or granite or some expensive stuff like that. But during Ravana's rule, every house was clad with actual gold. Can you imagine that? That was, we can imagine the economic powerhouse Lanka had become under Ravana. And moreover, the military power of Ravana was unrivaled in this whole universe. But despite all this economic and military and personal power, what happened? Ravana was quickly destroyed along with his life breath. Why? Because Dara means wife. Because he stole the wife of Lord Rama. Because Ravana abducted Sita Devi, his lifespan was cut short. And how was it cut short? Vispurjita Dhanusha. Simply by the twanging of the bow of Lord Rama, Ravana instantly lost his life. Lord Ramachandra did not have to take any big endeavor, simply by the twanging of his bow. So we can see from this that although Ravana had immense economic and military power, his citizens are not described to be free from anxiety and filled with bliss like the citizens of Lanka. And so, Ravana was cut short. And how was it cut short? Vispurjita Dhanusha.

### Kali Yuga, Chanting, Liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TFLSGROR_jg?t=436
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The speaker quotes the Srimad Bhagavatam, stating that despite the faults of Kali Yuga, the chanting of the Hare Krishna Mahamantra can liberate one from material attachments and allow one to return to the spiritual world.

So the Srimad Bhagavatam says that even though Kali Yuga is filled with so many faults, so many flaws, so many problems, full of distresses, still there is one Lord. One great, good quality, auspicious quality in Kali Yuga. What is that? Keertanadeva Krishnasya. Simply by performing this Keertana or singing and chanting of the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, what will happen? Keertanadeva Krishnasya Muktasangaha. One will become free from all attachments in this material world and Parambarajet, we will be able to go back to the spiritual world, which is Vaikuntha, free from all distresses, free from the problems of birth, death, disease, and old age which pervades this entire material world. Nobody is free from these four problems. If we want real freedom from these four problems, then we have to take shelter of the holy name of Krishna and Rama, we have to chant Hare Krishna Mahamantra, we have to remember Lord Ramachandra's pastimes, Lord Krishna's pastimes, and then our lives can become completely blissful, and the effects of birth, death, disease, and old age will become diminished in our life.

## Rtvik system ordered by Krishna Himself | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### Srila Prabhupada's instructions, Continuity, ISKCON history
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sheila Prabhupada wanted that whatever he had set up should continue without changes and then the movement would flourish unhindered."
**Summary:** The speaker outlines Srila Prabhupada's concerns about the ISKCON movement continuing after his departure and the three primary things he established: deity worship, disciplic conclusion, and initiations.

When Srila Prabhupada disappeared in 1977, he left behind him a glorious moment and a priceless legacy. In the days leading up to his disappearance, Srila Prabhupada was concerned specifically that the movement should not flounder in his absence. On April 10, 1977, Srila Prabhupada said, "Don't spoil it. We are very good, prestigious people. Don't spoil it. It's a hard day. It started with a very emotional moment. I am in humble condition, I am just comfortable, such exalted position. Don't spoil it. That is my request. Increase. That will depend on your character, behavior, position." Srila Prabhupada repeated the same request on April 22, 1977, saying, "Keep to our principle and see our GVP. It's very a lot. Then everything is gone. I am not there. Do that. That is my request. Whatever I left to you, I have taught you. Follow that. Nobody will be able to do it. No māyā will tell you. Now Kṛṣṇa is giving us, and there is no scarcity of money. To cut both, I'm sorry. Everything is there. You have got good sages all over the world. You have got, in the street you have got… Even if I die suddenly, you'll be able to manage. That I want. Manage nicely, don't go back." That was Prabhupada's concern. Again, Sheila Prabhupada reiterated the same concern on 29th April 1977 where he said, "The only request is don't spoil it." Once more, Sheila Prabhupada on 24th of May 1977 said the same thing. Sheila Prabhupada wanted that whatever he had set up should continue without changes and then the movement would flourish unhindered. Out of the many things that Sheila Prabhupada set up in ISKCON, three things were primary. One was the system of deity worship. Everything that was offered to the deities, whether the arathis or the boga, would have to be first offered to the guru Sheila Prabhupada and then to Nithyananda Prabhu. After that, then the arathi or the boga would be offered to Goranga Maha Prabhu, then it would be offered to Radharani, and finally it would get offered to Krishna. This was the system of deity worship Sheila Prabhupada set up. The second thing was the disciplic conclusion, which was all the deities were offered to the guru and then there in Prabhupada's books in their pure and unadulterated form. And the third thing was the system of deeksha or initiations Sheila Prabhupada set up and ran the dithích system of initiations in ISKCON from 1970 to 1977 and on July 9th 1977 Sheila Prabhupada ordered the dithích system to continue even in his absence. All that was twelve years and the existence of riti groups or in口ic scoring groups and their duasas all that was first mentioned and his mothers brothers and sisters linked gifts all that was all that was reduced to satan as per tradition means he was requesting a nonpublical organization then on 3rd he went to India in 1971 under carbohydrates heComputed a memorialitta needed was that nothing be changed in ISKCON after Srila Prabhupada's departure, especially these three things, dictatorship, books and initiations.

The GBC started changing everything. They changed the system of Deity worship to offering everything to Krishna directly first, whether it was Bhoga or it was Arati. This was a complete tectonic shift in the method of Deity worship Prabhupada taught. Not only that, they changed the books and they changed the system of initiations. They changed all the rules of the GBC. They changed all the three primary things in ISKCON. Srila Prabhupada had warned against this many times, as Srila Prabhupada said in this letter to Bhakta Dasa written on 24th of November 1974. Srila Prabhupada wrote, "Don't change from this to that. That is your American disease. This is very serious that you always want to change everything." Despite Srila Prabhupada's warnings and concerns, he did not change anything. He did not change anything. He did not change anything. After Srila Prabhupada's departure, the GBC went ahead and changed all the three primary things set up by Srila Prabhupada.

### GBC, Zonal Acharya System, Contradiction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=370
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be appointed as the first Diksha Gurus."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the GBC's resolutions regarding the appointment of spiritual masters, highlighting a contradiction between their 1978 declaration that Srila Prabhupada appointed eleven Diksha Gurus and their 1999 acceptance that they themselves created the zonal acharya system.

As far as initiations go, on March 19, 1978, in Resolution No. 16, the GBC resolved. The GBC will consider each year at Gaurapurnima, the appointment of new spiritual masters to be approved by a three-fourth vote. However, for 1978, the GBC will consider each year at Gaurapurnima, the appointment of new spiritual masters to be approved by a three-fourth vote. However, for 1978, no new spiritual masters shall be appointed other than the eleven selected by Srila Prabhupada. So, the GBC declared in 1978 that Srila Prabhupada appointed eleven Diksha Gurus. However, later in 1999, the GBC resolved in Resolution No. 403 that, unfortunately, shortly after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance, the GBC body allowed for an unauthorized zonal acharya system to evolve in ISKCON, elevating eleven exclusive successor Diksha Gurus. So, in 1999, 21 years after their resolution declaring that Srila Prabhupada selected eleven Diksha Gurus, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be appointed as the first Diksha Gurus. So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be the first Diksha Gurus. So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be appointed as the first Diksha Gurus. So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be appointed as the first Diksha Gurus. So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be appointed as the first Diksha Gurus. So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be appointed as the first Diksha Gurus. So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila follows that all other Diksha Gurus as well as the system of regulating Diksha Gurus in ISKCON by the GBC indicating no objection are all invented by the GBC.

### GBC, Diksha Guru System, Lack of Precedent
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=460
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Satswaroop Prabhu openly accepted that they, the GBC, did not know what they were doing in instituting the Diksha Guru system in ISKCON in 1978."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Satswaroop Prabhu, one of the original 11 Diksha Gurus, admitting that the GBC did not know what they were doing when instituting the Diksha Guru system and that there was no scriptural precedent for it.

Till date, the GBC has not given any explanation for the source of their system of regulating Diksha Gurus in ISKCON. In fact, Satswaroop Prabhu, one of the original 11 Diksha Gurus from 1978, said in the year 1986, in an article which appeared in the September-October edition of Hinduism Today, in the article titled, Murder quickens wider crisis in Krishna sect. In this article, Satswaroop Prabhu said, "If the different worship systems we instituted were accepted, then the system of regulating Diksha Gurus in ISKCON would not have been excessive. My answer is that we did not know what to do because there was no precedent scriptural rule which told us what to do." Satswaroop Prabhu openly accepted that they, the GBC, did not know what they were doing in instituting the Diksha Guru system in ISKCON in 1978. Shaila Prabhupad did not order them to institute a Diksha Guru system in ISKCON nor did he give any instructions on what to do in such a system. Nor did the GBC model their Diksha Guru system on any precedent or tradition or scripture or shastra. It appears that they did what appeared right as per their collective intelligence and discrimination. Their intelligence and discrimination decreased when the JVC was instilled into the Diksha discrimination dictated that the Ritvik system that Srila Prabhupada ordered for implementation in ISKCON on July 9, 1977, had to be discarded after Srila Prabhupada disappeared.

---

### GBC, Diksha Guru System, Realization

Rather, the GBC indicates that it has no objection to his initiating disciples within ISKCON." This is the GBC's resolution. Unless one is a pure devotee, one cannot talk to Krishna directly. So, as per the GBC's own resolution, they have declared that none of the devotees in ISKCON, not even the Gurus in ISKCON are pure devotees or that they have not even achieved any specific state of realization too. Hence, naturally, we must assume that they are not consulting Krishna in allowing devotees to act as Diksha Guru in ISKCON. They must be doing it on the basis of their intelligence and discrimination.

### Srila Prabhupada, Krishna, Leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=715
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Basis, just to see whether he is qualified. That's all."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces an exchange between Srila Prabhupada and Mr. Kevin Lehart about how Srila Prabhupada chose leaders, temple presidents, and devotees for BBT positions.

Now, interestingly, there is an exchange between Srila Prabhupada and one Mr. Kevin Lehart, which was recorded on 14th of July 1976. Listen to this carefully as Srila Prabhupada explains how he appointed temple presidents, how he appointed devotees to BBT positions, etc. How do you choose? You are responsible for the organization. Yes. Are you the one who chooses, who runs each center, who is responsible in each temple? Yes. Our program is to open centers in every village, every town to Prabhupada and Krishnapada. And you choose the leaders? Yes. What Krishna does, he tells you, how does that work? No. I have been chosen by my spiritual master. And you in turn choose others? Yes. That is our succession. On what basis? Basis on the order of Krishna. Originally, the order is from Krishna. How does that come? It comes by disciplic succession. Just like Krishna, Krishna said this knowledge to Arjuna, and Arjuna said to others, and it is open. Everyone can take it. There is no secrecy. We have to accept order. That's all. I missed that. I am sorry. The order is already there. It is open, open secret. There is no secrecy. Anyone can take it. All right. But in terms of specific, say, choosing specific things, specific details. Yes. In all details. How to become Krishna Karsayas, there is all details in this book. Bhagavad Gita as it is. So he is asking what is a man's qualification to be chosen? In other words, Prabhupada will decide who should be leader if he is qualified. And there is a process for making him qualified. And there is a process for testing to see if he is qualified. So in that way, it all comes from Krishna. You chose, for example, these fellows here to run the publishing house and be responsible for... Yes. Like he is in charge of publication. He is in charge of doing his research. On what basis, sir? Can you tell me some of the things? Basis, just to see whether he is qualified. That's all. Just like ordinarily, when manager is appointed, by the superior authority, on the merit, on his qualification, that's all.

### Krishna, Spiritual Master, Direct Communication
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=885

Okay. It's not... It's a mediated choice, or is it a direct communication from Krishna? That's my question. No. Oh, he is asking whether we claim that God speaks to us directly. Yes. God speaks to you when you are qualified. You cannot expect God as order supplier. Yes. When He speaks to you, you are qualified. You cannot expect God as order supplier. You cannot see that you are qualified. He is strict with you. But if God, the spiritual master, is the representative of God to disciple, because he is in direct contact with God. Yes, yes. My spiritual master appointed me, then you do. Similarly, I shall appoint somebody else. This is your choice. It's difficult for people to understand that God can speak to a man. That is… They question, how can God… how can God speak to some man? That is… Anything that the radio message is coming, a foolish man cannot understand how it is coming. He'll think how it is there, speaking. So any foolish man will be astonished how things are happening. That is foolishness. But God says, yes, please, I know this. What? Author? Saivayam Teja? No, no, no. buddhi-yogaṁ dadāmi tam yena mām abhyayanti teṣyāṁ sattva-yogaṁ tam yena mām abhyayanti dadāmi buddhi-yogaṁ tam yena mām abhyayanti Yes. To those who are constantly devoted and worship me with love, I give them the understanding by which they can come to me. God is situated in everyone's… As soon as he sees that here is a qualified person, then he gives him instruction. But in the same way that Kṛṣṇa says he will provide for all your needs, you still must work to achieve whatever Kṛṣṇa is giving you. You work for Kṛṣṇa. You have to work to get your necessities. In the same way, I am curious with respect to the way Kṛṣṇa communicates with you, whether it's in a similar kind of way that gives you your necessities. In other words, when you decide that someone is to be in charge of a particular temple, does Kṛṣṇa tell you that this person should be in charge? You, by judging him, say this person is qualified. Yes. Because a devotee always concerns Kṛṣṇa, and he gives order. So that's a more direct communication. Yes. And he gives order.

### Krishna, Intelligence, Devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=1082
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee always consults Kṛṣṇa and Kṛṣṇa tells him, do like this, not figuratively, practically."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada clarifies that a devotee always consults Krishna directly, and Krishna gives practical instructions, correcting Ramesvara Prabhu's interpretation that intelligence alone is sufficient.

Because intelligence… Our philosophy is that intelligence comes from Kṛṣṇa. So if I have something… Your philosophy is that your daily necessities come from Kṛṣṇa. Yes. I understand. Suppose my intelligence sees that this person is qualified, that means Kṛṣṇa has told me. No, not necessarily. Kṛṣṇa will tell directly. A devotee always concerns Kṛṣṇa, and Kṛṣṇa tells him. Two levels. Not figuratively. Practically. Does that apply then to other kinds of decisions and other kinds of activities as well? Everything. Everything. Because a devotee… A devotee does not do anything without concerning Kṛṣṇa. But that applies to a very greatly elevated soul. That is not an ordinary person. And that is… Therefore the minor devotees, they concern the spiritual master. That is our process. Yasya prasādād bhagavata-prasādād. I see. I was trying to explain the minor devotees. No, I was talking about… You were talking about the topmost level. Yes. That's a good phrase for the service. That's it. That will do it for me. I thank you kindly. Now, in this exchange, Śrīla Prabhupāda, on being questioned by Mr. Kevin Lehart, about how he appointed temple presidents and devotees to BBT positions, Rāmēśvara Prabhu interpreted Śrīla Prabhupāda's answer by saying, suppose my intelligence sees that this person is qualified, that means Kṛṣṇa has told me. This was Rāmēśvara Prabhu's interpretation. In effect, Rāmēśvara Prabhu said that the decision was made by using one's intelligence and that meant that Kṛṣṇa had ordered it. In other words, he said that there is no direct consultation with Kṛṣṇa. But Śrīla Prabhupāda immediately corrected him saying, no, not necessarily. He said that Kṛṣṇa will tell directly. A devotee always consults Kṛṣṇa and Kṛṣṇa tells him, do like this, not figuratively, practically. That means even to appoint temple president or any devotee to a BBT post, Śrīla Prabhupāda would always consult Kṛṣṇa and Kṛṣṇa was telling directly to Śrīla Prabhupāda, Śrīla Prabhupāda would always consult Kṛṣṇa and Kṛṣṇa was telling directly to Śrīla Prabhupāda, do like this, practically, not figuratively. Meaning, Kṛṣṇa was telling Śrīla Prabhupāda directly whom to appoint and Śrīla Prabhupāda would appoint such a person as a temple president or to any official post in the BBT.

### Diksha Guru, Krishna, Ritviks
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=1213
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In other words, all appointments in ISKCON came form Krishna and Śrīla Prabhupāda being the Vāyā media relayed about it."
**Summary:** The speaker argues that if Srila Prabhupada consulted Krishna for even minor appointments, it is even more applicable to the appointment of Diksha Gurus, and that all appointments in ISKCON came from Krishna.

Remember the verse Śrīla Prabhupāda so often quoted? Āchāryam mām vijāniyām nāvamanyetakarhi chit. Āchāryam mām vijāniyām nāvamanyetakarhi chit. Āchāryam mām vijāniyām nāvamanyetakarhi chit. These are Krishna's words. Whatever is said by the spiritual master is to be taken as directly the order of Krishna. No one must disrespect or disobey the spiritual master's order because the spiritual master's order is the order of Krishna. Now imagine, if even for BBT posts and temple president posts, Śrīla Prabhupāda was consulting Krishna, directly and Krishna was ordering whom to appoint, then is it not all the more applicable to appointing Diksha Gurus in ISKCON, particularly for the time after Śrīla Prabhupāda's departure? Diksha Guru position is far higher than that of a temple president or even a GVC. So it is natural that if Śrīla Prabhupāda was always consulting Krishna directly for appointing a devotee to any post, then naturally, Śrīla Prabhupāda wouldn't appoint anyone to Diksha Guru post, without consulting Krishna directly. In other words, all appointments in ISKCON came form Krishna and Śrīla Prabhupāda being the Vāyā media relayed about it. that order from Krishna. Now comes the question. If this is the case, then how do you think Srila Prabhupada appointed the Ritwiks to carry on initiations in ISKCON after his departure? How does the GBC think Srila Prabhupada sent out to all the temple presidents and the GBCs the July 9, 1977 order for implementing the Ritwiks system of initiations in ISKCON? How does the GBC think Srila Prabhupada decided on this order? Do you think Srila Prabhupada simply used this intelligence and discrimination like the GBC did in either inventing the no-objection guru system or the GBC even today is doing in selecting diksha gurus? Do you think Srila Prabhupada did not consult Krishna?

### diksha, initiation, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When we are taking initiation from Srila Prabhupada, we are accepting Srila Prabhupada as our Diksha Guru, initiating spiritual master, and Prabhupada is also accepting you as his disciple."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the concept of Diksha initiation, emphasizing Srila Prabhupada's role as the Diksha Guru and referencing relevant verses from the Chaitanya Charitamrita.

Namah Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswati Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Sunyavadi Paschatya Deshatarine. Jaya Sri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Sri Advaita Gadhadhara Sri Vasadi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. So today is a very significant day in the lives of the devotees who are taking Diksha initiation. When we are taking initiation from Srila Prabhupada, we are accepting Srila Prabhupada as our Diksha Guru, initiating spiritual master, and Prabhupada is also accepting you as his disciple. It is important to understand what Diksha is, what is initiation. In the Chaitanya Charitamrita, Madhyalila, Chapter 4, Chapter 24, verse 333, purport, Srila Prabhupada says that, as far as Diksha is concerned, initiation is concerned, one must refer the Chaitanya Charitamrita, Madhyalila, Chapter 15, verse 108. So in that purport to verse 108 of Chapter 15 of the Chaitanya Charitamrita, Madhyalila, Srila Prabhupada elaborately explains about Diksha or initiation.

---
### diksha, divya jnana, spiritual progress
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=136
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So the process of divya jnana, of Diksha started the day when you started hearing from Srila Prabhupada."
**Summary:** This segment defines Diksha as the revealing of transcendental knowledge and the destruction of sinful reactions, explaining how this process begins with hearing from Srila Prabhupada and following his instructions, leading to gradual purification.

Srila Prabhupada quotes Srila Rupa Goswami and he says that, divyam jnanam yathodadyat kuryat papasya sankshayam, diksheti sa chokta deshikais tatva kovidaihi. What is the definition of Diksha? The definition of Diksha, the word Diksha is taken from two words, di is taken from divya jnana, and ksha is taken from kuryat papasya sankshayam, kshaya. So divyam jnanam yathodadyat, the process by which transcendental knowledge is revealed within the heart, divyam jnanam yathodadyat. Kuryat papasya sankshayam, and by the process by which sinful reactions are destroyed in one's lives. These two things constitute the process of Diksha. So all of you who are taking Diksha today, you have already received divya jnana in your heart to some extent. The day you came in touch with devotees and somebody gave you a book of Prabhupada and you started reading Prabhupada's books, divya jnana started revealing within your hearts, isn't it? So the process of divya jnana, of Diksha started the day when you started hearing from Srila Prabhupada. And when you started hearing from Srila Prabhupada and you decided to follow his instructions, you might have begun with chanting Hare Krishna Maha Mantra a few times, one mala, then slowly, slowly when you read, you increase your number of malas, then you come to four malas, you are still reading Prabhupada's books, so many things you are understanding. In the beginning, you may not have the understanding that Krishna is Supreme Personality of Godhead, but anyway, I am told to chant Hare Krishna, I am chanting. Some mantra, we chant like that. But as we go on reading Prabhupada's books, we understand, oh yes, Krishna is Supreme Personality of Godhead. Krishna is not like other devatas. So divya jnana has started getting revealed within the heart. And also you understand, I am not the body, I am a spirit soul, I am Atma, I am not the Deha. Divya jnana is getting revealed within the heart. And slowly what happens, you go on reading Prabhupada's books, you keep chanting, by the time you come to chant four rounds, you feel like giving up coffee and tea. And then continue reading Prabhupada's books, continue chanting, by the time you come to eight rounds, onion and garlic goes out of your life. And then slowly, slowly, you come to the stage of chanting sixteen rounds, you start wearing Kanti Mala, you start wearing Tilak, you start fasting for Ekadashi and other festival days. Isn't it?

---
### fasting, self-control, kuryat papasya sankshayam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=318
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "But then after reading Prabhupada's books, I realized, no, we have to fast."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a personal anecdote about his initial experience with fasting, highlighting the struggle between bodily desires and the determination to follow Prabhupada's instructions, illustrating the principle of Kuryat Papasya Sankshayam.

Before coming in touch with Prabhupada and Prabhupada's instructions, some of us might have been fasting, but many of us would not have fasted in our life. I, for that matter, never fasted in my life. Whole life, whenever I wanted to eat anything, I was eating. But then after reading Prabhupada's books, I realized, no, we have to fast. So, first time I fasted on Gaurapurnima day. And I didn't realize, I didn't know what is fasting. Fasting karna hai, fasting karo. By that time, it was afternoon, my stomach was growling. And by evening, I was like, you know, I am going to fall dead now. And I was working in office. Then I had to leave the office. Somehow, I drove the scooter all the way to the temple. I don't know how I drove. And then when I reached the temple, Abhishekam, all those things happened. And then finally reached the Prasadam hall, sat down with great, ah, now relief. Now I am going to eat Prasadam. Half my life is already gone by that time. And I am sitting in front of the plate, waiting when will Prasadam come and fall in the plate. Suddenly, one devotee comes and tells me, what are you sitting there for? Get up, come and serve. Another 25% of my life went out. Serve ah, what? Serve. Anyway, he is calling, I could not say no. I got up and served somehow. And then finally, he sat and had Prasadam. And I was like, oh my God. First time I was, what is this fasting? Never had, had any idea. But you see, when we, when we start following Prabhupada's instructions, we start fasting. Even though the body is demanding, body is craving, I want food, I am hungry. Body is all feeling so tired, so fatigued. No, determination has come. No, I must fast. Why? Because I am not the body. I am not given to the body's demands. So you see, Divya Jnana has started getting revealed within the heart. Meat eating goes out of the life. The coffee and tea goes out. All intoxicants go out. So, this is Kuryat Papasya Sankshayam. Papasya Sankshaya also has started happening.

---
### karma, sinful reactions, prarabdha, aprarabdha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=451
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Papa or the sinful reactions in our life are of two kinds. One is the propensity to perform sinful activities which comes by our, because of our ignorance."
**Summary:** This segment delves into the nature of sinful reactions, categorizing them into the propensity to commit sins and the suffering that results, further dividing the latter into Prarabdha Karma (manifested results) and Aprarabdha Karma (unmanifested results in seed, sprouting, and unripe fruit forms).

So why? Because, Papa or the sinful reactions in our life are of two kinds. One is the propensity to perform sinful activities which comes by our, because of our ignorance. And because of our ignorance, that thinking that I am the body, I am not a spirit soul, we have committed so many sinful activities in our past life. So we get the propensity to perform sinful activities in our current life also, which is the result of sinful activities in our past lives. And second, the way the sinful reactions manifest is in the form of suffering. So there is suffering and when we speak of suffering on account of our accumulated sinful activities, reactions in our past lives, it again manifests in two types. One is Prarabdha Karma and another is Aprarabdha Karma. Prarabdha Karma means the results which are already manifested. This body which I have got is the result of my past activities. It is Prarabdha Karma. I have got a certain mind and intelligence that is the result of Prarabdha Karma. I might be suffering from some disease that is the result of my Prarabdha Karma. That means it is already manifest. I am already, I am already either enjoying or suffering the fruit of my past activity. The second type of sinful reaction is Aprarabdha Karma. Aprarabdha Karmam Kutam Bijam Falon Mukam. This Aprarabdha Karma is of three kinds. One is, it is in seed form. Another is sprouting. Another is the fruit which is not yet ripe. So the, when the sinful reactions in our life are accumulated like a mountain, they are all like the bija, like the seeds which you have accumulated within a jar of seeds. So when you keep the seeds in the jar, they will remain as it is. Now you take out the seed and put it into the soil, put a little water, put a little manure. What will happen? After a few days, you will see it sprouting. So that is called Kuta. Bijam Kutam Falon Mukam. Kuta, bija is in the seed form. When the situations in your life become favorable for that seed to start sprouting, it will become like a, it will be, it will start sprouting.

---
### karma, diksha, spiritual master, commitment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=602
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So real initiation begins from the day you start hearing from the spiritual master, from Srila Prabhupada."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of engineering college admission, the speaker explains Fala Unmukha, and emphasizes that the spiritual master gives Divya Jnana and removes sinful propensities, leading to real initiation when one is determined to follow instructions.

That sprouted plant is called Kuta. And then, the plant will grow. And after a little while, it will start giving fruits. That fruit, when it is still unripened, it is called Fala Unmukha. That means, the result is going to come. So just like suppose, I go and admit myself in engineering college. So I have just admitted myself in the college. That means in four years, the fruit of my admitting in the engineering college is going to come in the form of the degree. So, the moment I admit myself into the engineering college, my engineering degree is Fala Unmukha. It is going to come. And then finally, there is the ripened fruit, which is Prarabdha Karma. So, when we, when, so the spiritual master in your life until now, he has been giving you Divya Jnana. And he is also Kuryat Papasya Sankshayam. He is taking away the sinful reactions in your life by removing your propensities, to perform sinful activities. That is why you have started following the four regulated principles. No meat eating, no intoxication, no gambling, no illicit sex. Before you came in touch with Prabhupada's books, Prabhupada's instructions, Prabhupada's teachings, we might have been engaging in all those sinful activities. But after reading Prabhupada's books, we feel, no, I should not do that. That means the propensity was there in you, but that has been removed by Srila Prabhupada. So therefore, he has already given, put you on the, into the process of Diksha or initiation. So real initiation begins from the day you start hearing from the spiritual master, from Srila Prabhupada. And when you take the determination, when you are determined, when you read Prabhupada's books, you have to chant 16 malas, you have to follow the four regulated principles, you have to fast on Ekadashi, what happens? You start, you feel, yes, I should follow that. That determination actually constitutes Diksha or initiation. And what ceremony we are undergoing right now, where there is a fire sacrifice, you will receive a new spiritual name, you will receive beads chanted on by the Ritvik on behalf of Prabhupada, this is the formal part or the official part of Diksha.

---
### diksha, commitment, spiritual master, eternal bond
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=747
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Your commitment to Srila Prabhupada goes a step higher."
**Summary:** This segment addresses the question of why formal Diksha is necessary if one is already following Prabhupada's instructions, explaining that the ceremony strengthens commitment, creating an eternal bond with Prabhupada as the sole Diksha Guru, even in the spiritual world.

So somebody may say, then I can undergo real initiation, I will read Prabhupada's books, I will follow all his instructions, why do I have to undergo formal Diksha? Why do I need to undergo the ceremony? I will keep following whole my life. Prabhupada says that if you, as long as you are following Prabhupada's instructions and in your heart you have accepted Prabhupada as your spiritual master, as your Diksha Guru, you are as good as his disciple, but you are not yet his disciple. So when you undergo Diksha, undergo the official ceremony, what happens? Your commitment to Srila Prabhupada goes a step higher. When you are sitting in the formal ceremony, what are you committing? You are committing that, I am going to accept Prabhupada as my Diksha Guru, life after life, eternally. Eternally, Prabhupada is my only sole Diksha Guru. This is the commitment you are making from your side. There is no going back from here. There is no point of no return. You cannot go back tomorrow and say that, no, I made a mistake, I want to take Diksha from someone else. No. Not in this life, not in a hundred life henceforth, not even after you go back to the spiritual world. Now from this point onwards, once you have taken formal official Diksha, even when you go to the spiritual world, there also Srila Prabhupada will be guiding you as your Diksha Guru. That is the meaning of eternally you are accepting him as your Diksha Guru.

---
### diksha, karma, punishment, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=837
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada takes away all your sinful reactions."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Prabhupada takes away all sinful reactions (prarabdha and aprarabdha karma) during formal Diksha, but this also makes the disciple liable to punishment for future sinful activities, unlike before initiation when only sukruti was gained.

And from Prabhupada's side, he is acting as your Diksha Guru. What he does, the moment you are sitting for this formal Diksha, Prabhupada says, when you take the first initiation, Prabhupada takes away all your sinful reactions. How does Prabhupada take away all your sinful reactions? Already he has taken away all your propensity to perform sinful activities. No. Kuryat Papasanksham has already done to one extent. But all your accumulated sinful reactions in all the past lives, aprarabdha karma, sitting as Bija, Kuta, Phalamukha, where will that go? And your prarabdha karma, you have got a body and a mind and intelligence, where will that go? That also has to go, when you take formal initiation from Prabhupada, at that time, Prabhupada will take away all the prarabdha and aprarabdha karma also. Not only the propensity to perform sinful activity, Prabhupada will also take away all your accumulated sinful reactions. So therefore, a disciple, once he has taken formal diksha from Prabhupada, once you have formally sat in the ceremony and you have taken diksha from Prabhupada, what happens? One is the eternal bond is created. Second is Prabhupada takes away all your accumulated sinful reactions. Third, what happens is, therefore, you become liable. You become liable to be punished if you commit sinful activity again. See, until you undergo formal initiation, until you undergo formal diksha from Prabhupada, suppose you followed all the four regulative principles, you were chanting sixteen malas for one year, after that, somehow, you fell down. You gave up. You started, you stopped following the four regulative principles, you stopped chanting sixteen malas. What will happen? Whatever you have done for that one year is to your eternal credit. You will get some sukruti. That's all. And loss of karma will take over. Whatever activity, punya karma, papa karma you do, go on. That much only will happen. You will get an eternal credit. You will get sukruti for whatever you have followed. But once you take formal diksha, after that, if you don't chant your rounds or you break the regulative principles, what happens? You are liable to be punished. If you don't follow, if you break, you will be punished by your diksha guru, Prabhupada and by Krishna. Until that point of time, no punishment. You are only getting sukruti. You are only getting blessing. Now blessing will be hitting also.

---
### diksha, holy name, consciousness, parampara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=990
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The holy name is officially getting delivered to you from the parampara."
**Summary:** This segment explains the significance of the Ritvik chanting on the beads, emphasizing the delivery of the holy name from the parampara, creating an eternal bond between the disciple and Prabhupada, and resulting in a shift from material to spiritual consciousness.

If you disobey, if you break your vow, because in a formal ceremony what you are doing, you are sitting in front of Agni, you are sitting in front of all Vaishnavas, you are sitting in front of the Deity, and you are taking a vow that you will follow Prabhupada's instructions without fail. So therefore, you become liable to be punished. And Prabhupada also says, when the Rithvik has chanted on your beads, until now you are chanting, you are chanting on beads only. But what is the significance of the Rithvik chanting on the bead and that bead is being given to you? There are two significances. One is, the holy name is being delivered to you from the parampara. Until now you are chanting the holy name. Okay, no problem. The holy name was acting in your life. But now, the holy name is officially getting delivered to you. From the parampara. Therefore, the holy name will act more powerfully in your life. Now, from now on when you chant Hare Krishna Mantra, that holy name will have a much more powerful effect in purifying you and taking you back to Godhead. Second thing is, that when the Rithvik has chanted on the beads and that bead is given to you, what actually it creates is, it creates an eternal bind between you and Prabhupada. As Prabhupada is your spiritual master and you as his disciple. This is the significance of the Rithvik chanting on the beads on behalf of Prabhupada and giving it to you. And as a… Now that all your prarabdha and aprarabdha karma is taken away from you, you are actually getting a new birth. You may not feel it. You may think, I am feeling the same. It is not there. It is not a matter of feeling. It is a matter of consciousness. Because you have to understand, until now, before we took initiation from Prabhupada, our consciousness was different. What was that? Sense gratification is the goal of my life. Now, when you are taking diksha from Prabhupada, what is your consciousness? Your life and substance is Prabhupada's vani, Prabhupada's instructions. Whatever Prabhupada has ordered, that is my life. And just like life cannot be separated from the body and if the life is separated from the body, then the body becomes useless. It becomes a dead body. Like that, my soul existence, I exist now only for carrying out Prabhupada's instructions because he is my diksha guru now. So my Guru Maharaj, Prabhupada's instructions are my life and soul now. For me, material sense gratification doesn't matter, material name, fame, wealth, these things are immaterial for me now. For me, what is now soul thing in my life is carrying out the instructions of my spiritual master, my Guru Maharaj, Srila Prabhupada. So your consciousness has changed, you see. Your consciousness has gone from material consciousness to spiritual consciousness.

---
### subtle body, brahmana, diksha, second birth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=1175
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada has taken that away and given you a subtle brahmana body."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Diksha changes the subtle body from material to spiritual, giving a brahmana body and a new spiritual name, signifying a second birth where the goal is to go back to Godhead.

How is this possible? Until and unless your subtle body is changed. Your subtle body until now was materially working. Now your subtle body is working spiritually. Goals have changed. Your purpose of life has changed. Your outlook towards life has changed. The way you perceive the material world and other people around you has changed. How this is possible? That's because your material subtle body which you have got because of your prarabdha karma is now removed and you are given a new spiritual body. You are given a brahmana body. Actually you are given a brahmana body now. So you are able to think like a brahmana because now you think in terms of shastra, right? You see, when you have a when there is a in sanatana dharma when there is a shudra, vaishya, kshatriya they all think in terms of the body. They think in terms of sense gratification. They think in terms of fame. Shudra, simply sense gratification. Vaishya is more into hoarding money. He is interested in wealth. Kshatriya is interested in name and fame. So all revolving around the body. What does brahmana think? Brahmana's thinking, his consciousness is I have to attain moksha. I have to attain liberation. I have to know who that brahman is. So his thinking is on the spiritual platform. So now you are also thinking like that. What is your thinking? Your thinking now is my goal in life is I have to go back to Godhead. So this is how a brahmana thinks. A brahmana is not interested in sense gratification. Brahmana is not interested in doing any sinful activity. He very rigidly follows the shastra. For him, shastra is the most important. Vani of his guru is most important. So now your life is like that. So that means Prabhupada has taken away your praarabdha karma also. Praarabdha karma, Prabhupada is anyway taking away. Kootam, bijam, phalaunmukam. Prabhupada is also taking your praarabdha karma away. Because externally, the gross body that we have got, that's not the real thing. The real thing is the subtle body. How the subtle body works. So the subtle body has been your current subtle body. In Kali Yuga, everyone is born as Shudra. Even if somebody is born in a brahmana family, he is also a Shudra only. Kalau Shudra Sambhava. So you were born in Kali Yuga with a Shudra body. Now Prabhupada has taken that away and given you a subtle brahmana body. So this is your second birth. And so therefore, to signify the second birth, you are given a spiritual name. You are given a new name. You got this praarabdha karma of this body, with its subtle body, gross and subtle body, by taking birth from your mother and father. So you got a name from them. Now, Prabhupada is giving you a new birth by taking away all your past praarabdha and aprarabdha karma. And so therefore, you are getting a new spiritual name. So this is a second birth for all of you who are taking initiation.

### diksha, commitment, spiritual master, obedience
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=1356
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada's order is your life and substance."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of college admission, the speaker emphasizes that formal initiation is like enrolling in college, requiring adherence to the spiritual master's instructions without question, making Prabhupada's orders the disciple's life and soul.

And Prabhupada says that this formal initiation ceremony is like, it is like getting, filling up the admission form when you go to school or college. So when you go to school or college, you fill in an admission form. And after filling in the admission form, like most of you would have done, I also did, start bunking classes. And tell one of our friends, go and put proxy attendance. Then, although you have got admitted in the college, you really are not a student. If you are really a student, you should go and sit in the classes. You have to be attentive to the classes. You should be absorbing the lessons. You should be learning. That mood of yours, that determination of yours, I have come to the college to learn, not to bunk and go out and party with my friends. That's not my goal. I have come to the college, I have filled in the admission because I want to sit and learn something. Then you are a real student. Similarly, formal initiation also, it is a formality. It is like getting admitted into the college. Now, don't bunk. Right? Don't bunk and go and party with friends. You should follow all the rules and regulations. You should follow the instructions of the spiritual master. Order of Guru Maharaj, Srila Prabhupada is your life and soul now. Alright? So, whatever Prabhupada has ordered, you have to follow that implicitly. No question. No question. There is no question of asking. Until now, you could have asked. Prabhupada says this. So, let me find out whether it is asked for Shastra or not. Until now, you could have asked that question. From now onwards, you cannot ask that question. Prabhupada's order, end of it. You cannot ask for Shastric evidence and all that. Where is Shastric evidence for Prabhupada's order? Where is tradition in Prabhupada's order? Now, you cannot ask that question. Prabhupada's order is your life and substance. Shastra, tradition, your understanding, not understanding, all your logic, your arguments, you want to understand how it is correct, incorrect, all that is secondary. Whether you understand or not, whether you are going to be delivered or not, are you going back to Godhead or not, all those considerations are thrown out of the window. No more consideration. Your only consideration now is, I have to follow Prabhupada's orders in my life. Period. This is the meaning of Diksha.

---
### diksha, loyalty, guru, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=1509
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This unquestioning paraabhakti towards Guru, is what pleases Guru."
**Summary:** This segment concludes by emphasizing that true initiation involves unquestioning loyalty to Prabhupada's instructions, pleasing the Guru and ensuring Krishna's pleasure, leading to effortless progress back to Godhead.

If you are determined like this, then, you are not only formally initiated, you are also actually initiated. Otherwise, if you get, if you sit in the formal Diksha ceremony, got initiated, after initiation, if you say, Prabhupada is saying like this, but Shastra is saying something else, science is saying something else, who knows whether it is right or not, then, you are only filled in the admission form, but you are bunking the classes. So, you are not, you are not really eligible to sit in the exam to go back to Godhead. You understand? So, therefore, you have, you have to understand the significance of formal Diksha. It involves many things. It involves your commitment to Prabhupada and Prabhupada's commitment to you to take you back to Godhead, provided you fulfill all the conditions from your side. It is a two-way contract, eternally binding you to Prabhupada. So, remain determined to follow Prabhupada's warning, Prabhupada's instructions, Prabhupada's orders and that is your life and soul from this point onwards and Prabhupada is your whole, you know, is your soul Diksha Guru, life after life and you will definitely progress on the path back to Godhead. You don't have to worry about going back to Godhead, whether I will go back to Godhead or not. None of those considerations have to be there. Simply, right now, from now on, your only thinking and duty is, how to execute the orders of Srila Prabhupada. That's all. How to purify you, when to take you back to Godhead, how many lifetimes after which to take you back to Godhead, will I take three lifetimes like Jaya and Vijaya or will I take two lifetimes like Bharat Maharaj or will I go back within six months like Dhruva Maharaj, you don't have to consider all that. Your own, no matter, even if I have to take one thousand lifetimes or one million lifetimes again and again, I have to take birth within the material world, no problem. Even in every single of those million lifetimes also, I'll keep following Prabhupada's instructions, my Guru Maharaj's instructions. This should be your determination. This is the, this is what really makes you a disciple and this is what pleases Prabhupada as your Diksha Guru. This attitude of a devotee, unquestioning loyalty, this unquestioning paraabhakti towards Guru, is what pleases Guru. And if Prabhupada, your Diksha Guru, is pleased with this, yasya prasadad bhagavad prasadad, then Krishna will be pleased with you. And if Krishna is pleased with you, your going back to Godhead is not an issue at all. Comfortably, without your putting any endeavor, you'll go back to Godhead. So please keep all these things in mind and firmly remain determined in following the Prabhupada's instructions, His orders, and you will, you'll remain firmly situated as a true disciple of Prabhupada and you will be able to access the true meaning of Diksha and you'll find yourself going back to Godhead very quickly. So, Vishwaguru Sheela Prabhupada ki jai, Shishi Gauranitha ki jai, Shishi Krishna Rukmini Satyabhama ki jai, Netai Gaur, Premanande Hari Haribol.
