## Srimad Bhagavatam 3.17.5 | HG Suvyakta Narsimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### omens, demons, material nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=389
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When the breeze was blowing, it was very uninviting to the touch."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the omens that manifested during the descent of Jaya and Vijaya as Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu, focusing on the unpleasant changes in the natural elements, particularly the wind.

So here in this chapter, we are seeing the descent of Jaya and Vijaya into this material world, and when they descended into the material world, they descended as enemies of Vishnu. So when such enemies of Vishnu or demons take their birth, what happens? What kind of portents one can see in this world? What kind of symptoms you can see? Those are being described here. There are many omens that were manifest when Jaya and Vijaya came down as Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu. So it is described here, Vavavvayu. Vayu, the air. A breeze which blows. Winds which blow. Normally in Chennai what happens, it's very humid. So when we stand outside in the tree shade, there is a breeze that blows. It feels very pleasing. It feels very nice, cooling, comforting. But the kind of breezes that started blowing when Hiranyakashipu and Hiranyakashipu appeared, they were Suddhusvarsha and they were Phutkaaraan Eerein. When the wind used to blow, when Hiranyakashipu and Hiranyakashipu were going to take birth, the breeze used to give a hissing sound. And just like you would see in if any of you before joining you have seen these horror movies, sometimes in the night they will show one window is closing, one door is closing and there is a kind of sound that comes which is very fearful. Those kind of sounds are not pleasing to the ear. And Suddhusvarsha, to the touch also, the breeze was not pleasing. So, especially when you are in Chennai, you don't have experience of breeze which is unpleasant. Isn't it? Any time breeze blows, it's welcome. But when Karthik Maas comes, when Chaitanya Maas comes, that time, the breeze is very pleasing. So, but then, when Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu were taking birth, the entire climate itself became so bad, that the breeze became distrustful, became uninviting to the touch.

And the sound that they were giving was also not pleasing to the ears. And what was happening, Vathani Ko, Vathya means, there was, you know, these cyclones and hurricanes had become very common. So, which is what we are experiencing now. Every other day, there is a cyclone. Just recently, you know, Chennai has gone through one cyclone. Still not got over the, still the cyclone hangover is still going on. Still continuously raining. And a few years back, we had the big tsunami. And I think a couple of years back, we had a big flood also here. So, these kind of natural disturbances are actually, if you see, they are not meant to be in this world. It is not that this world is like this. No, this world is not like this. Not supposed to be like this. This world, Krishna has created in such a way, that it is supposed to be very pleasing and giving lot of peace and comfort to us in our minds, as long as we are executing spiritual activities. The moment we go against the will of Krishna, we go against the laws of nature, we start trying to exploit material nature. Material nature as well as other living entities. When that becomes prominent in this material world, what happens is, all the entire system around which this whole material universe is designed gets disrupted. And when that gets disrupted, all these kind of natural disturbances become very prominent.

### omens, Krishna's appearance, pure environment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=878
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A pure breeze began to blow, pleasing the sense of touch, and bearing the aroma of flowers."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the omens at the time of Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu's birth with those at the time of Krishna's appearance, highlighting the pure and pleasing environment that manifested when Krishna was about to appear.

So, it is described here, when the breeze was blowing, it was very uninviting to the touch. That's how it is described when Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu were going to take birth. Because they were great demons. They were enemies of Vishnu. Now, on the other hand, in the tenth canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam, there is a description of what was the prevailing situation when Krishna was going to appear. So, the same words, Vavavvayuhu is used even when Krishna appeared. But when Krishna appeared, I'll just read that verse. When Krishna appeared, the Srimad Bhagavatam, it says, Vavavvayuhu suhasparsha. You see, when Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu came, Vavavvayuhu sudhasparsha. And when Krishna was going to appear, Vavavvayuhu suhasparsha. And then, Punyagandhavahasuchihi, Agnayastha, Dvijatina, Chandastatra samindhata. So, what it means is, I'll read the translation. A pure breeze began to blow, pleasing the sense of touch, and bearing the aroma of flowers. Can you imagine that? It was bearing the aroma of flowers. And when the brahmanas, engaging in ritualistic ceremonies, ignited their fires, according to Vedic principles, the fires burned steadily.

### Vedic civilization, sense gratification, exploitation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1050
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The earth produces enough to satisfy the needs of a man, but not the greed of a man."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the Vedic civilization's focus on spiritual advancement with the modern world's emphasis on exploiting material nature for sense gratification, leading to ecological imbalance and natural disasters.

Actually, human society is supposed to live in this manner, in harmony with material nature. And in the Vedic times, the whole civilization was, the consciousness of the civilization was how to utilize this human form of life to advance in spiritual life. So, whereas in the current day world, the whole consciousness of the civilization is how to exploit material nature and maximize my sense gratification. So, material nature, if we live according to the laws of material nature, there is ample opportunity for sense gratification, but not for unlimited, unrestricted sense enjoyment. In a limited, restricted way, in the manner it is mentioned in the Vedas, sense enjoyment is there, not that it is not there. But then, as it is said, the earth produces enough to satisfy the needs of a man, but not the greed of a man. Now, the whole civilization, human civilization has become so greedy for sense gratification that all the laws of material nature are discarded. And we try to exploit and exploit and exploit material nature because of what happens? Material nature also starts reacting.

### sinful activities, reactions, Kali Yuga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1134
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We are accountable for our actions. Whatever I do, there will be some reaction involved."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the accountability for actions, both individually and as a civilization, explaining that sinful activities lead to negative reactions from material nature, making the world an unbearable place to live, especially in Kali Yuga.

So, as Prabhupada is mentioning, the demoniac people who are accustomed to all kinds of forbidden sinful activities, they will create such a situation that the material world becomes an unbearable place to live in. So, we have to understand that we are accountable for our actions. Whatever I do, there will be some reaction involved. If the civilization is engaged in sinful activities, then material nature will give suitable reaction. We will not be able to enjoy. We will not be able to enjoy very happily. Just like if there is a criminal, and if he thinks, I will engage in all sorts of anti-social activities, and I will steal, I will rob people, and with that money, I will enjoy. It is not possible. Why? Because the police department will start running after him. And they will not allow him to live peacefully. They will go behind him until he is behind the bars. So, in a similar way, if the whole civilization is trying to go against the orders, against the order of material nature, which has been given by Krishna or Vishnu, then what will happen? Then we will not be allowed to live peacefully. We will have to. There will be various reactions that will come. We may increase our comforts, our conveniences. We may invent many, many material, scientific inventions we may create, to give us more comforts, to give us more sense gratification. But what will happen? Material nature will make the situation worse and worse. Even though our conveniences, material conveniences increase, what will happen? Material nature will also keep increasing the pressure on us, by which we will not be able to happily enjoy those conveniences. Some pandemic will come, some cyclone will come, some flood will come, some tsunami will come. Actually, if you see, the amount of sinful activities in this material world is growing. That's how Kali Yuga is. In Kali Yuga, the sinful activities of the people keep on increasing.

### sinful activities, human life, material nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1346
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The human life is meant for a higher purpose."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that sinful activities at individual and civilizational levels lead to a lack of peace, highlighting that human life is meant for a higher purpose, and aligning with that purpose allows material nature to support our progress, while opposing it leads to vehement opposition from nature.

So, we have to understand, that, at the civilizational level, whether at the global level, or at the national level, or at the individual level, even an individual, if he engages in sinful activities, he cannot expect to remain happy with the results of those sinful actions. So, this is, actually, this is the real fact, which people become blind to, people are blind to, they are not able to appreciate, they are not able to see this. That if I commit sinful activities, they will have to suffer. And, if as a civilization, we engage in sinful activities, thinking that we will make all the arrangements for sinful activities, and we will manage the show, no. Whether at the individual level, or at the civilizational level, it is incumbent upon us, to live our life, free of sinful activities. Otherwise, we are bound to be in a situation where, we will not be peaceful. So, what we have to understand is, that, the human life, is meant to, meant for a higher purpose. And, that higher purpose, is achievable only in the human form of life. And, if we live our life, according to that goal, then, what happens is, the whole material world, the material nature, will align itself, to our activities, it will, it will, it will only, enhance our efforts, progress in that direction. It will not oppose us. When we engage in sinful activities, then material nature will oppose. It will oppose very vehemently. Whereas, if we engage in, achieving the ultimate goal of life, as described in the Vedas, then what happens, material nature will become very favorable, and it will help us, to progress in that direction. It will not oppose us.

### goal of life, materialism, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1473
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Ultimate goal of life, in today's world is, money, money, money, sweeter than honey."
**Summary:** This segment laments the modern world's ignorance of the ultimate goal of life, reducing it to materialism and fleeting pleasures, and introduces the conversation between Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Ramananda Raya about the true goal of life.

So, what is that ultimate goal of life? Unfortunately, today, in the modern day world, people don't know, what is the ultimate goal of life. Ultimate goal of life, in today's world is, money, money, money, sweeter than honey. Unfortunately, that's our, eat, drink, and be merry. You can eat and drink, but you cannot be merry. If you drink also, next day morning, when you get up, you will have such a headache. Where is merry? All gone. And when you are fully drunk, you don't know, what you are talking, what you are doing, nothing you will remember. Where is merry? So, you may say, eat, drink, and be merry, but actually, you cannot be merry. So, what is the ultimate goal of life? Actually, when Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, met Ramananda Raya, on the banks of the Godavari, so, he said, Krishna Balram, so, when Chaitanya Mahaprabhu met, Ramananda Raya, on the banks of Godavari, Ramananda Raya, Ramananda Raya, was the governor, of Vidyanagara. Vidyanagara, at that time, is today's, I think, this one, Vishakaparnam. Am I right? So, there, he happened to, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, happened to meet, Ramananda Raya. So, Ramananda Raya, because he was the governor, naturally, governor will not come, you know, alone to the Godavari, people used to go to the river, to take bath, and all that. So, Ramananda Raya, came there. And, naturally, he was, being carried in a, palanquin, and there were lot of Brahmanas, and Vedic mantras, and there were all his assistants, and all those kind of people, who had come, along with him. And so, then, there, he saw Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, had also come there, and both of them, met there. And then, but then, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, did not want to discuss, too much with him, surrounding him, and he had come, in a very official manner. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, invited, invited Ramananda Raya, you come to, I am staying in one Brahmana's house, here. So, you come and meet me, in the evening. So, Ramananda Raya, in the evening, came and met Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, all alone. Just one assistant, he brought with him, and he came all alone, and met Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. So, when Chaitanya, so, actually, who was this Ramananda Raya? Ramananda Raya, is, the niece, of, of Krishna. So, Lalitha and Vishakha, are the, are the, are the, after Radharani, Lalitha and Vishakha, are the next Gopis. So, Vishakha Gopi, had incarnated as, Ramananda Raya. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, asked Ramananda Raya, what is the ultimate goal, of life? It's a very important question, because, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu knows, in the earlier, Vedic civilization, everybody knew, goal of life means, we have to attain Mukti, we have to go back, to Godhead, that used to be, a common knowledge, but in Kali Yuga, nobody knows, what is the ultimate goal, of life. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu asked, Ramananda Raya, can you please tell me, what is the goal of life? It is not that, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu knows, but he wanted, an answer from, Ramananda Raya, for the benefit of, the general mass of people. So, Ramananda Raya, replied, that, Varna Ashramacharavata, Purushena Parapuman, Vishnu Aradhyate, Nanya Tattoshkaranam. He said, following, the Varna Ashrama system, is the way to, satisfy, Lord Vishnu, and satisfying, Lord Vishnu, is the, highest goal of life. So, if we follow, Varna Ashrama Dharma, then, we are, actually, we will be, achieving, the highest goal, of life.

---
### surrender, bhakti, goal of life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1683
**Verse References:** BG 18.66
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Give up all other kinds of Dharma, that means, this Varna Ashrama Dharma, and all these things, you give it up, and surrender unto Me."
**Summary:** This segment continues the conversation between Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Ramananda Raya, where Ramananda Raya initially suggests following Varna Ashrama Dharma as the highest goal, but Chaitanya Mahaprabhu rejects it, prompting Ramananda Raya to quote Bhagavad Gita 18.66 about surrendering to Krishna.

So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, immediately said, Eho Bahiya, Aage Kaho. He said, this is external, I reject this. Why? Because, Varna Ashrama Dharma, when you are, following, all the duties, of Varna Ashrama Dharma, are, to engage one, in material activities. The, duties, of a Brahma, of a Kshatriya, of a Vaishya, of a Shudra, of a Brahmachari, a Grihasta, a Vanaprastha, Sanyasi, these are all material. So, this is not the highest thing. Aage Kaho, you know, tell something better. So, immediately, Ramananda Raya replied, that, to give up, all these duties, to renounce, the highest goal of life. So, immediately, Ramananda Raya quoted, the, famous words, from the, 66th verse, of the 18th chapter, of the Bhagavad Gita. Krishna advised Arjuna, give up, all other kinds of Dharma, that means, this Varna Ashrama Dharma, and all these things, you give it up, and surrender unto Me. So, this is the, actually, this is the ultimate instruction, of the Bhagavad Gita. So, Ramananda Raya said, this is the ultimate, goal of life. So, what did, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu reply? Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said, Aage Kaho, Bahiya. This is external, rejected. Aage Kaho, tell something better.

---
### Jnana Misra Bhakti, glorifying Krishna, association of devotees
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1780
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To hear Krishna's glories, being described by devotees, that is the highest goal."
**Summary:** This segment continues the dialogue, with Chaitanya Mahaprabhu rejecting the idea of simply renouncing material duties, emphasizing the need for positive engagement. Ramananda Raya then suggests Jnana Misra Bhakti, but Chaitanya Mahaprabhu rejects that too, leading Ramananda Raya to quote Lord Brahma about the importance of hearing Krishna's glories in the association of devotees.

So, Ramananda Raya then replied, with another verse, from the Bhagavad Gita, Brahma Bhuta Prasannatma, Na Sochati Na Kaanshati, Sama Sarveshu Bhuteshu, Madh Bhakti Labhate Paran. So, why, why did Chaitanya Mahaprabhu reject? Because, simply renouncing the material duties, is not going to help one. It is not going to, it is not going to really, there is no positive engagement, you are just renouncing. Simply by renouncing, what is the use? There has to be some positive engagement. So, then, he said, Ramananda Raya replied that, Jnana Misra Bhakti is better, than, Karma Misra Bhakti, or, renouncing completely one's duties. So, he said, better than both of these is, Jnana Misra Bhakti, because on the platform of Jnana, you know, there is, you become qualified to, execute Bhakti. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, for this also, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said, Eho Bahiya, this is external, rejected, Aage Kaho, tell something better. So, then, Ramananda Raya, quoted a verse from, 10th canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam, which was spoken by, Lord Brahma. Jnane Prayasam, Udapasi Namantayeva, Sanmukharitam, Bhavadiyava Vartam. What is that, after that? Sthane Sthitah, Tanuang Nobher, Prayaso Ajitajitopya, Sitayis Trilokyam. So, Brahma Ji said, Jnane Prayasam, to engage in the process of Jnana, is, Prayasam, it is simply hard labor, there is no, result that comes out of it. No, real spiritual benefit that comes out of, undertaking the path of Jnana Yoga. Udapasi, so reject the process of Jnana Yoga. Jnane Prayasam, Udapasi, Namantayeva, so, to surrender to Krishna, by rejecting Jnana Yoga, and, Sanmukharitam, Bhavadiyava Vartam, to constantly hear your glories, submitting his prayers to Lord Krishna. So, to hear Krishna's glories, being described by devotees, that is the highest goal. And, by doing this, what happens is, Thane Sita, wherever you are situated, you may be a Brahmana, you may be a Kshatriya, you may be a Vaishya, you may be a, you may be, you may be a Sanyasi, you may be a Grihasta, you may be Brahmachari, Manaprasad, whatever you are, whichever situation you are, engage in glorifying Krishna, in the association of devotees, and by this, what happens is, Krishna is Ajitha, Ajitha Jithopiasi, Krishna is Ajitha, Ajitha means, nobody can conquer Krishna, that unconquerable Krishna, becomes Jitha, Ajitha Jithopiasi, he becomes conquered, simply by, glorifying Krishna, in the association of devotees. So, when Chaitanya Mahaprabhu heard this, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said, Eho Yaha, now what you are saying makes sense.

---
### Kali Yuga, association of devotees, ISKCON
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1977
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In Kali Yuga, the process of spiritual emancipation is to glorify Krishna, in the association of other devotees."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the power of glorifying Krishna in the association of devotees, especially in Kali Yuga, highlighting how the Srimad Bhagavatam reiterates this principle and how Prabhupada established ISKCON to facilitate this practice.

Why? Because, now there is some activity, spiritual activity. What is that? To glorify Krishna. So, what happens is, this is a very powerful process, especially in Kali Yuga. What is that? That, we engage in glorifying Krishna, Satamprasangat, in association of other devotees. Satamprasangat means, in the association of pure devotees, and in a group, as a group, we engage in glorifying Krishna. So, this process, is very, very powerful. So, that is why, the Bhagavatam, actually if you see, the Bhagavatam, is, in many, many places, you will find, from the beginning till the end, the Bhagavatam is pushing this, principle, that is, is exposing this principle, is again and again, reiterating this principle, that, in Kali Yuga, the process, the process of spiritual emancipation is, to glorify Krishna, in the association of other devotees. In the earlier ages, we could perform spiritual activities, all alone. Bharat Maharaj, he gave up his whole kingdom, and he went to the forest, and practiced Bhakti, all alone. And he reached the stage of Bhavabhakti. And later on, of course, he again took the birth of Jadabharata, and continued. He did not join, and, you know, in a group, and became a Bhakta, and started getting trained, and all that. You know, he went to the forest, and he practiced. Dhruva Maharaj, at the age of 5 years, he went to the forest, all alone, and practiced. And within 6 months, he achieved perfection. And, we all in Kali Yuga, you know, we come and join, he is gone 24, 26 years old, and 20 years will pass, but still, we will, you know, we will be in, you know, we will never, you know, what he achieved. But then, Dhruva Maharaj did it all alone. He went to Dhrotila, in Vrindavan, and he practiced. In 6 months, he had Darshan of Vishnu. You take the case of Narada Muni, Narada Muni also, for a few months, during Chaturma, for 4 months, he served all the pure devotees. After that, he went to the forest, and practiced on his own, all alone. Whereas, in Kali Yuga, if you try to do that, all these great personalities, they went to the forest, executed Bhakti, and overcame Maya. If in Kali Yuga, we try to do, first of all, you will not find a forest. Even if you find a forest, you go there, he will not overcome Maya, Maya will overcome us. So, it is not possible in Kali Yuga, to practice Krishna Consciousness, all alone. So, in the olden ages, it used to be possible. That's why, in the olden age, there was no ISKCON, there was no organization. It's not that, Panacharya had to create an organization, and everybody would come and practice. This is a very novel thing, which is happening only in Kali Yuga, which was started by Shri Krishna. And this is not unique only to our Sampradaya, Gaudiya Vaishnava Sampradaya. Even Madhavacharya had an institution. Even Ramadhyacharya started an institution. Because, in Kali Yuga, you have to have an institutional place, where devotees can all come together, and jointly they can practice. And that is how Prabhupada made a whole program, where we all get together.

---
### Sadhana, spiritual strength, Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=2170
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is actually building up our spiritual strength."
**Summary:** This segment describes the ISKCON Sadhana program, emphasizing how it provides a protected environment and builds spiritual strength through collective practices like chanting, hearing Bhagavatam, and Guru Puja, making it easier to progress in spiritual life and resist the forces of Maya in Kali Yuga.

In the morning we get up, all of us come together in the temple hall, assemble together, and then we sing Samsara Dhaman Leda. And then what we do, we all sit in the temple hall, and together we chant Hare Krishna Maha Mantra. And then, together we do Guru Poja. Together we all sit and hear Bhagavatam class. When, you know, in the olden ages, where was this kind of system? Dhruva Maharaj or Bharat Maharaj, they were not sitting in the forest and, you know, gathering lot of people, and together let us, you know, have Bhagavatam class daily. No, they were not having. Okay, only the sages got together in Naimisharanya, because they were conducting a special sacrifice. So, they together all got together and they heard. Because anyway it was the beginning of Kali Yuga. So, they actually set the precedence. That in Kali Yuga, all of, all the sages got together and heard Bhagavatam. Bhagavatam is there all the time. So, only in Kali Yuga, hearing Bhagavatam, process of hearing Bhagavatam is Satamprasangat. You have to do it in the association of others. Alone, we will not be able to do it. We will not be able to sustain. And that is why, Prabhupada has created a whole Sadhana program, where we all come together, and together we hear Bhagavatam, together we chant Hare Krishna Mantra, together we do Sankirtana. And this is the process for Kali Yuga, which is very, very powerful. And simply by engaging in this process, what happens is, we gain strength from each other, and we will be able to very quickly make progress in spiritual life. Otherwise, in Kali Yuga especially, it is very, very difficult to progress in spiritual life. To be able to protect ourselves from the forces of Maya is very, very tough. It is not at all easy. Maya is very powerful, and we are very weak. In the earlier ages, what used to happen, Maya used to be less powerful, and people used to be much stronger spiritually. Now the roles have reversed. So, therefore, we have to, you know, together we have to face the force of Maya. So we stay in the same ashram, we come and practice spiritual life together. So what happens is, in the temple, temple is a very protected environment, where the force of Maya is practically not there. So, this association of devotees, we have to appreciate, and we have to value that, you know, what Prabhupada has given us in the form of this institution, in this form of, in the form of the opportunity to be able to, be able to practice the principles of the Bhagavatam, in the manner it is described in the Bhagavatam. In the association, in a group, every day, day after day, we go on practicing. This is actually building up our spiritual strength. And this is what is required for Kalyuga. So, with these few words, I will stop. If there are any questions or comments, we can discuss.

## The Difference Between Material and Spiritual Activity | Sri Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### material fatigue, spiritual satisfaction, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2pjhe6-QyLg?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But because devotional service to Krishna is completely transcendental, therefore, we never get satiated."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts material activities, which lead to burnout and require breaks, with devotional service to Krishna, which is transcendental and provides constant satisfaction.

Whenever somebody is engaged in some activity, that person after a little while needs a little break. Otherwise, he'll get burnt out. That's why every week when you go to office, there is a weekend. Saturday and Sunday are off. Shut off your mind from your work and relax and take out all the stress. Go out somewhere. Nowadays, people are talking that the five-day week should be reduced to a four-day or a three-day week. Every alternate day, they should have holiday. That will increase the productivity of the people. And once in a year, some long holiday also is required. Go on vacation. In the case of a devotee, what happens is, Anisham constantly is engaged only in thinking of Krishna, speaking of Krishna, rendering service to Krishna. Nataraptaha, he never gets satiated. He feels whatever I'm doing is not enough. I want to do more. I want to do more. Even 24 hours in a day is not enough. I want more time. I want to do more. This is the characteristic of devotional service because it is not material. Anything material becomes hackneyed. You get fed up with it after a little time. But because devotional service to Krishna is completely transcendental, therefore, we never get satiated.

## The Multi-faceted Aspects of Krishna | Sri Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa | SB 6.9.21 | 23.07.2024
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### fearlessness, surrender, divine protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mw4Wpx13HUw?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, let us now worship that Supreme Lord who alone can give us full protection."
**Summary:** The speaker reads and explains a verse from Srimad Bhagavatam about demigods seeking protection from the Supreme Lord due to their fear of time.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 6, Chapter 9, entitled, The Appearance of the Demon, Prithrasura. Text No. 21: śrī-devā-ūchuhu vāivambharāgnya-kṣitayastriloka brahmādayo ye vayam udvijantaḥ harāmayasmai balimantakosau vibheti asmādharanam tato naḥ. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace Śrīla Prabhupāda: The demigods said the three worlds are created by the five elements namely ether, air, fire, water and earth, which are controlled by various demigods, beginning from Lord Brahmā. Being very much afraid that the time factor will end our existence, we offer presentations unto time by performing our work as time dictates. The time factor himself, however, is afraid of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Therefore, let us now worship that Supreme Lord who alone can give us full protection.

---
### Krishna's pastimes, motherly love, divine childhood
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mw4Wpx13HUw?t=92
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So she immediately removed Kṛṣṇa from her lap, set Him down and ran to the kitchen."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the Damodara Lila, focusing on Yasoda's interaction with baby Krishna when she leaves him to tend to boiling milk.

Purport by Śrīla Prabhupāda: When one is afraid of being killed, one must take shelter of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. He is worshipped by all the demigods, beginning from Brahmā, although they are in charge of the various elements of the material world. The words vibheti asmād indicate that all the demons, regardless of how great and powerful, fear the Supreme Personality of Godhead. The demigods, being afraid of death, took shelter of the Lord and offered Him these prayers. Although the time factor is fearful to everyone, fear personified is afraid of the Supreme Lord, who is therefore known as abhaya, fearless. Taking shelter of the Supreme Lord brings actual fearlessness and therefore the demigods decided to take shelter of the Lord. Thus in Śrīla Prabhupāda. When Kṛṣṇa had appeared 5000 years ago, He performed many wonderful pastimes, especially during His childhood. When Kṛṣṇa was about probably around one year old, once it so happened that Yasodamāyī was sitting in her house and she was churning the curd, nice freshly formed curd. She was churning to take out the butter, so that she could feed Kṛṣṇa with it. And Kṛṣṇa at that time, He was feeling hungry. He walked up to His mother and started pulling her, pestering her, that I am hungry, I want milk. Yasodamāyī stopped her churning, took Kṛṣṇa on her lap and started feeding Him her breast milk. And while Kṛṣṇa was drinking His mother's milk, suddenly Yasodamāyī noticed on the fireplace, she had placed one pot of milk to boil. And this milk was a special milk which was meant for feeding Kṛṣṇa. And that milk started boiling and overflowing. Yasodamāyī immediately, she thought, oh, if this milk boils over and it gets burnt, then what will my Kṛṣṇa drink? So she immediately removed Kṛṣṇa from her lap, set Him down and ran to the kitchen to remove the milk from the fireplace. And it would have taken some time, she had to remove the milk and then put off the fire and if some milk had been spilt over, clean up and all those kind of things. Some time it took and she came back.

---
### Krishna's pastimes, divine anger, childlike behavior
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mw4Wpx13HUw?t=265
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "How Mother Yasoda can put me down when I am hungry and I am drinking her milk?"
**Summary:** The speaker continues the Damodara Lila, describing Krishna's anger at being left by Yasoda, his breaking of the curd pot, and his attempt to escape.

Now in the meantime, our Kṛṣṇa is not someone who is going to wait for anyone. He got very angry. How Mother Yasoda can put me down when I am hungry and I am drinking her milk? How she can put me down and go away? So Kṛṣṇa became very angry. I will show her what I am. So He looked around and what to do? He looked at the pot of curd that Mother Yasoda had been churning. He took a stone and broke that pot. And even, you know, all small children also when they get angry, they will do something naughty like that. And after they do it, then they will start thinking, now what is going to be the consequence? You know, when we grow up, we first think of the consequence and then we act. Children are not like that. They first act and then think of the consequence. So Kṛṣṇa also broke the pot. After breaking the pot, He started thinking, okay, my anger, Bhadas nikal diya. I, you know, I showed my anger, broke the pot. Now Mother Yasoda will come. What is going to happen? So better I scoot from here. Better I don't stay here. So silently, He slipped away from there. So Yasoda Mai, after cleaning up the kitchen or whatever, she came back and she saw, Krishna is not there. Okay, Krishna must have gone. He must have gone to play or something. But then she saw that the pot of curd has been broken and the curd is all spilled over. So she thought, who can do this? Nobody can come into the house and do all this. Krishna only must have done this. She deduced. After all, she is a mother. She knows her son. Who can know her son better than her? So she thought, Oh, Krishna, you are doing like this. I will teach you a lesson today. She started searching. Where is Krishna? Where is Krishna? Where is Krishna? And searching, searching, she came to the storehouse where Krishna was sitting and he was taking out the butter and he was feeding all the monkeys. So silently, Yasoda Mai walked up behind him, wanting to catch him. But suddenly, Krishna turned and saw, Oh, Yasoda Mai is coming. He left everything and started running. Yasoda Mai also started. She was also not going to give up. Today I have to teach him one lesson. Determined. And she ran after him and Krishna is also running. Yasoda Mai is running. And you know how difficult it is to catch children. Although they are so small, if they start running, so difficult it is. Like some of our temples, we will be doing ICVK. Right? In the, for small children, even in Chennai also, we have started ICVK. Some, some small kids, in that three to five year age guru, they will not sit quietly. They will keep running around and, and the teacher, to control the child, it is very difficult. So, she ran after him and Krishna is also running. Yasoda Mai is running. And you know how difficult it is to catch children. To control the child, it will, you know, she will be totally fed up. She will not be able to do it. So, children are like that. So, Krishna is running. Yasoda Mai is trying to catch, but she is not able to capture him. And, the laborious, you know, running, running, running. She was panting and she was perspiring. But she was also not giving up. Somehow, I am going to catch Krishna today. I am not going to give up. So, Krishna also saw that his mother has become tired. She is perspiring. And, she is, you know, breathing heavily. So, Krishna allowed himself to be captured by Yasoda Mai. Yasoda Mai caught him. And then, when she caught him, she saw Krishna was crying out of fear. What is mother is going to do now? I broke the pot. So, already in fear of the punishment, he had started crying.

---
### Krishna's pastimes, motherly love, divine grace
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mw4Wpx13HUw?t=478
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So, she saw there was a wooden mortar there. And, she took some rope and tied Krishna to it."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the Damodara Lila, describing Yasoda's decision to tie Krishna to a mortar, and Krishna's subsequent freeing of the Yamala Arjuna trees.

Sometimes, it happens, no? We all, as children, if you remember, when we were in classroom, something we did, show your hand and took the scale. Before the teacher lifts this one itself, we will start crying. And, teacher will say, hey, what did I do? First, you show your hand. Don't cry. So, children are like that. So, Krishna also, Yasoda Mai is going to punish me today. He started crying. And, Yasoda Mai had applied nice mascara to Krishna's eyes. All that mascara is flowing down Krishna's cheeks. And, Krishna is in great fear. And, Yasoda Mai saw that Krishna is crying so badly. It's not good that he feels so afraid. My child should not be so much afraid of me. So, she saw in her hand, there was a stick. And, seeing the stick, she saw Krishna is becoming so afraid. So, she thought, okay, let me not beat him with this stick. She threw away the stick. But then, still punishment has to be there. Otherwise, Krishna will every time do something, even bigger. So, she saw there was a wooden mortar there. And, she took some rope and tied Krishna to it. So, you are being so naughty. So, I am punishing you. So, for some time, you remain there. Let us see what you do. So, she tied him to the wooden mortar. And, Krishna was hardly about a year old. So, Yasoda Mai then tied him and then went off. And then, Krishna thought, oh, Yasoda Mai has tied me. She tied me for breaking that pot of curd. Now, I will do something even bigger. Let us see what she will do. So, he thought what to do. And, he saw in the courtyard of Nanda Maharaj, there were two tall Arjuna trees, Yamala Arjuna trees. So, Krishna started crawling. Because, you know, one year old children, they will sometimes crawl. They will sometimes walk also. It is like that. So, Krishna started crawling. And, crawling, crawling, pulling the wooden mortar which got stuck between the two trees. And, what happened? The two trees fell down. And, out of that, two dazzling, effulgent personalities came out. They were, you all know who they are, right? Who are they? Nalakuvara and Manigreeva, the sons of Kubera. So, they came out and they offered their obeisances to Krishna. They offered their prayers and they got liberated. In the meantime, the Gopas Yashoda Mai, they all came running because two huge trees falling. They made a thundering sound. So, everybody came running out. What happened? What happened? And, they saw two trees have fallen down and Krishna is between them. So, they all thought, Oh, great fortune. Narayana has protected him. Otherwise, the trees could have fallen on Krishna. What would have been his state? He would have been crushed. So, Yashoda Mai also took Krishna and then she did not she did not chastise him or anything thinking, you know, like this. This is the famous Damodara Leela pastime. In a few months, we will have the Damodara month when we will sing those prayers and Damodara Ashtakam prayers remembering this pastime.

---
### Krishna's divinity, Kunti's prayers, time factor
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mw4Wpx13HUw?t=674
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Although she was the paternal daughter of Krishna, so she was in a superior position to Krishna, although she was so, she always knew that Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker transitions to Krishna's later life, describing Kunti's prayers as Krishna departs for Dwaraka, emphasizing her understanding of Krishna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

Now, this happened when Krishna was about one year old. Several decades later, Krishna grew up. He got married to 16,000 princesses. He had sons, grandsons, great-grandsons. He had built Dwaraka, ruling over Dwaraka with all the Yadavas. And then, the Pandavas had also grown up. They were Krishna's cousin-brothers and they had they had become very close associates of Krishna. And the Pandavas went through many tribulations and finally, the battlefield, the battle of Kurukshetra was fought and the Pandavas got back the kingdom. By this time, Krishna had already crossed the 90 years. He was more than 90 years old. So you can see, he had, he had, you know, more than nine decades had passed. And after Yudhishthira Maharaj was enthroned as the emperor, Krishna stayed for a few months in Hastinapura to please Yudhishthira and then after a few months, then Krishna said, all the residents of Dwaraka will be waiting for me, so I have to go now. So the Pandavas assembled and Satyavati also came, although she was very old, she was also there, Shantanu's wife and the, you know, Draupadi came, Kunti Maharani came, all the Pandavas, they all came, Uttara also came, they started offering their prayers to Krishna. Great anxiety, now Krishna is going to go away. At that time, Kunti Maharani offered her prayers to Krishna. Although Kunti Maharani was the paternal daughter of Krishna, so she was in a superior position to Krishna, although she was so, she always knew that Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. She did not think of Krishna like a, my nephew, no, not like that. She, she always had the understanding that Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. So when she heard that Krishna is going to go back to Dwaraka, she came and offered her prayers to Krishna, and Krishna was departing. And those prayers of Kunti Maharani are captured in the first canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam. Beautiful prayers.

---
### Kunti's prayers, time factor, Krishna's control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mw4Wpx13HUw?t=814
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O Krishna, I know that you are Kaala, the Supreme Controller in the form of the Time Factor."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Kunti's prayer to Krishna, focusing on her recognition of Krishna as the Supreme Controller in the form of the Time Factor.

One of the things that Kunti Maharani prayed to Krishna was, Manyetvam Kaalam Eeshanam Anadi Nidhanam Vibhum Samam Charantham Sarvatra Bhutanam Yan Mitak Kalihi. She said, because she is, you know, she is aware that she is the Supreme Controller in the form of the Time Factor. Kaalam Eeshanam. O Krishna, I know that you are Kaala, the Supreme Controller in the form of the Time Factor. Kaalam Eeshanam. So you control everybody in the form of the Time Factor. The Time Factor is nothing but it is another aspect of yours. Actually, the Time Factor it is all powerful. Nobody can withstand the force of time. So, Manyetvaam Kaalam Eeshanam Anadi Nidhanam. Anadi, Adi means beginning. Nidhanam means end or destruction. So, even in Hindi, we say when somebody dies, we say, Inka Nidhan Ho Gaya. Right? So that means Nidhanam means destruction or end. The word Adi, what it becomes Anadi. That means Adi means beginning. Anadi means without a beginning. Anadi Nidhanam means without a beginning or end. So, Krishna is that Supreme Time Factor which has neither beginning nor end. Samam Charantham Sarvatra and the Time Factor is very, very impartial. Time Factor will not see that this person, he is a small child, small baby. His Ayu, his life span has got over. But what to do, poor child, no, let him grow a little more. Time Factor will not see. Time, your time has gone up, your duration of life is over. You are a baby, you are old man, young man, girl, man, woman, child. Time Factor will not consider any of those things. Your Ayu is over, your life span is over. Time Factor is very impersonal. It will never consider, it will, it shows, does not show any mercy, no favour to anyone. So, so, Kunti Maharani said, Samam Charantham Sarvatra and all the living entities are under the control of the Time Factor. And when we see, say all living entities, it's not just limited to the Earth Planet. It's not limited to the birds and bees and all that. Even human beings are also under the control of the Time Factor. Human beings, the human civilization may develop. We may become very advanced. Science and technology may advance a lot. But no matter how much advancement we make, no matter how much scientific discoveries and inventions we make, still, we are always under the control of the Time Factor. Samam Charantham Sarvatra Bhoothaanam Yanyam Mithranam Samam Charantham Sarvatra Bhoothaanam Yanyam Mithra Kalihi. So all the living entities, not only on this planet, even the Devatas are under the control of the Time Factor. Even Brahma is under the control of the Time Factor. Brahma also has a fixed lifespan. When the time comes for him to, when his lifespan gets over, after Dviparartha, two Pararthas, what happens? He has to give up his body, go on to the next body. So even Brahma, even all the Devatas, Agni, Vayu, Brahmins, Devatas, Demigods who control all the other living entities, even they are controlled by the Time Factor. Which is what the Devatas themselves are stating here. They are saying, Udvijan Taha We are afraid of the Time Factor because any moment the Time Factor may take control of us and we may die. So Kuntimharani is well aware of this. Now Kuntimharani did not stop there. He said, I am not going to stop when she glorified Krishna like this that you are the Time Factor. You are the Supreme Controller. The everybody all the Devatas everybody are afraid of you.

### Kunti's prayers, Damodara Lila, inconceivable nature of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mw4Wpx13HUw?t=1085
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And that Krishna is afraid of a simple cowherd woman, Yashodamai. When I think of this, it's very, very bewildering."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining Kunti's prayers, highlighting her astonishment at the fact that Krishna, the controller of time and death, was afraid of Yasoda.

But then she remembered see 90 years 90 years have passed by. After 90 years Kuntimharani is remembering that incident which happened when Krishna was one year old with Esodhamai in Vraja. Krishna was like a Chant in the Gopi Adade Gopi Adade Dhama Tavam Nyate Dasashru Kalilanjana Sambramaksham Vatram Niniya Bhaya Bhavana Asthithasyas Samami Bimohiti Bheerapiyat Bibheti. She said Gopi Adade Dvai Krita Agasi Krita Agasi means The Gopi, Yashodamai, took a rope in her hand to tie you to the wooden mortar. At that time, What was your state? I remember your state. What was your state? There were a flood of tears flowing down your cheeks. And all the mascara was getting washed away. And this was your state. And you are in great fear of Yashodamai. And who are you? Fear personified. Fear personified. Who is fear personified? Death. And death is another form of Kala or time. So she is saying, The Kala, the time factor, is so fearful to all the Devatas. And you are the controller of that time factor. Even Kala, even death personified. Death personified fears you. And what are you doing? You are crying in fear of Yashodamai. Samam vimohayati. When I remember this, I am bewildered. How can this happen? How is this happening?

### Damodara Lila, Sukadeva Goswami, inconceivable nature of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mw4Wpx13HUw?t=1253
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And prakritam yatha, As if Krishna is just another ordinary child, Like everybody else."
**Summary:** The speaker references Sukadeva Goswami's description of the Damodara Lila, emphasizing the inconceivable nature of Yasoda treating Krishna, the Supreme Lord, as her ordinary son.

So this, This is a completely inconceivable thing. A personality, Whom fear personified fears. Whom Kala, time factor, fears. Fearing whom death can happen. Whom Kala, time factor, fears. Whom Kala, time factor, fears. Death carries out its duties very impartially and very strictly. Why is death, why is Kala so impartial? Because Kala or death is very much afraid. If I don't do my duty properly, Krishna will punish me. So that death is afraid of Krishna. And that Krishna is afraid of a simple cowherd woman. Yashodamai. When I think of this, it's very, very bewildering. So it's inconceivable. How can such a great personality, Whom even death personified fears, Be afraid of a simple woman like Yashodamai? It's inconceivable. And this, This astonishment, And Krishna's becoming afraid, Of Yashodamai, Is not limited only to Kunti Maharani. Even Sukadeva Goswami, When he describes this, Pastime of the Damodara Leela, In the Srimad Bhagavatam, in the 10th canto, After describing this, He said, He said, Tam amanyata atmajam That Yashodamai, She thought Krishna is my son. Atmajam. But, Who is that Krishna? He is Avyaktam. He is that unmanifest Supreme Lord. Who is not visible to everybody. It's not that Krishna is an ordinary person. Anybody can see. No. He is such a great person. He is Avyaktam. Not everybody can see him. Only to whom Krishna reveals himself, They can see him. That is that personality. And, Adhokshajam. He is completely transcendental. But that Krishna, Yashodamai thought, This Krishna is my son. And thinking like this, What did she do? Gopikoolukhale, Damna babandha prakritam yatha. She, Damna with a rope, Ulukale babandha. She tied him to a wooden mortar. What is this? That Supreme Lord, Who is, Who is unmanifest, Who is not visible to everybody. Even the Devatas, If they want to see Krishna, They cannot see him. They have to go to the shore of, The Kshira Udaka, The milk ocean. And then they have to chant the Purusha Sukta prayers. And they please the Paramatma. Even then Paramatma also is not visible to them. He will only give instructions to Brahma in the heart. And they have to hear. And they have to follow. Om tad Vishnoha paramam padam, Sada pashyanti surayaha. Even the Devatas are waiting, When will we have darshan of the lotus feet of, Paramatma Vishnu. And that Vishnu is, One portion of the, Plenary portion of Krishna. So you can imagine, How much tougher it will be to see Krishna. And that Krishna, Yashodhama is thinking, That, This is my son. And prakritam yatha, As if Krishna is just another ordinary child, Like everybody else. Gopi kulukale damna babandashi, Took him and tied him to the wooden mat. What is this? This is completely unbelievable.

### Krishna's features, fear, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mw4Wpx13HUw?t=1408
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So Krishna has this facet, Of being very frightening. At the same time, Krishna, Has this feature, Of himself being afraid, Of his own devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the multifaceted nature of Krishna, contrasting his frightening aspect as the controller of death with his fear of his own devotees.

So you see, Krishna's feature, On one hand, You have Krishna's feature, Of being, Fear personified, So much so that, Even fear personified fears him. So there is a, There is a facet of Krishna, Which is very frightening, To even death personified. Death frightens everyone. Right? Death terrorizes everyone. Anybody, When the point of death comes, They will all become completely afraid. So, That death who frightens everyone else, Including all the Devatas, demons, everybody, That death, Is afraid of Krishna. So Krishna has this facet, Of being very frightening. At the same time, Krishna, Has this feature, Of himself being afraid, Of his own devotees. Devotees are all completely surrendered to Krishna. They have taken shelter of the lotus feet of Krishna. To those people who have taken shelter of him, To whom he is giving protection, Krishna is afraid of them. So how is this? How do you understand this? Actually, This shows, The, The, The, Multifaceted personality that Krishna is. Actually, This facet of Krishna, Of being a very, A person whom we all have to fear, Is there in every religion. And that is why, If somebody is very religious, What do we say? He is a God-fearing person. He is afraid of God. Isn't it? That's what we say. Like the, Devatas are also stating here, Udvijantaha, We are afraid of Krishna. Especially in his, Form as Kala. So that is one facet of Krishna. And normally in all other scriptures, Religious scriptures, Only this facet of the Supreme Lord, Is shown to the people. Oh, God is very powerful. If you don't follow his rules, He will punish you. He will put you into hell. You will have to undergo so much misery. So, With great fear, Follow whatever he has told. So this fearful aspect of Krishna, Is there in every scripture. Even amongst tribal people, Aborigines, Those who are not very civilized people, Even amongst them, You can see, They will worship some lightning, Or some thunder, Or some big mountain, With great fear. So you will see, This facet of Krishna, Is there, Very commonly you will find it.

But it's only in the Srimad Bhagavatam, Where Krishna is shown to have, Shown to be, A multi-faceted personality. And this fact, Sukadeva Goswami, Brings out in the tenth canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam. When Krishna, When he was about ten years and six months old, Krishna left, Vrindavan, Nandagav, And he came to Mathura, To attend that Mela, That great festival, Which had been organized by Kamsa. When Krishna came there, Kamsa had made a big plan, In the name of a Mela, He had called everybody, Invited everyone, He had called all his allies, Some friendly kings, And then he had called some yogis, Some ascetics, Some rishis, All those kind of people, And he had also invited all the, Village people, Here, Like that he sent an invitation to, Vraja also, To Nanda Maharaj, That we are organizing a big festival, All of you also come, Like that. So, All these people had come to Mathura, And there was a big stadium, Which had been erected, And everybody was seated, The kings were given one position, The common folk of Mathura, Were given another position, Invitees like Nanda Maharaj, The Vrajavasis, Who had come from other villages, They were given another position, The rishis, The sages, They were given another separate area, Like this, All these people were seated, In that big stadium, And then, What Tamsa did, He called all his close associates, He had some associates, Who were big wrestlers, Chanura, Mushtika, Toshala, He called all of them, See, The person who is supposed to kill me, I am, I am doing some trick, And getting him to Mathura, And that person is Krishna, Krishna and Balaram, Two brothers are there, So they are only, Ten and a half years old, But, They are very powerful, So many asuras, I had sent earlier here, They have killed all of them, So now, I want, That when they come, You should invite them, For a wrestling match, And, You should kill them, And Chanura, Mushtika, They all said, Ah, Ten year old boy, Why are you bothered, King Kamsa, Some fluke, He must have killed all the other asuras, Don't worry, We are all there, And they started flexing their biceps, And all that, You know, They were wrestlers, Strongly built, Chappan inch, And all those kind of things, You know, Very powerfully built, And, Very strong, And professional wrestlers, Not ordinary people, Even today, You will see, Wrestlers will be, You know, Very strongly built, So, In this big stadium, In the midst of that, There is the wrestling arena, And there is lot of music, And dance, And so many other things going on, And then, Krishna, And Balaram, With a few of his, Covert boy friends, They entered the stadium, And, When they entered the stadium, Sukadeva Goswami says,

## The Supreme Position of Lord Ramachandra | Sri Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### glories of Lord Ramachandra, comparison with historical figures
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m-JB2-c4bFo?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Although Lord Ramachandra appeared such a long long time ago, still his glories are spread everywhere."
**Summary:** The speaker establishes the lasting impact of Lord Ramachandra compared to other historical figures like Alexander the Great.

Although Lord Ramachandra appeared such a long long time ago, still his glories are spread everywhere. The activities of the greatest of the human beings, be they emperors or world conquerors or big big philosophers, are forgotten after a few hundred years. During his time, Alexander must have been world famous, but today we know very little of him. In a few more decades, he may even be forgotten from the minds of the people.

---
### Lord Ramachandra as God, Vedic authority, incarnations of God
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m-JB2-c4bFo?t=36
**Verse References:** SB 1.3
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The basis for determining who is God or who is an incarnation of God and who is not, is not just on the basis of popularity. The basis are the Vedas."
**Summary:** The speaker asserts Lord Ramachandra's divinity based on Vedic declarations, specifically referencing the Srimad Bhagavatam's description of incarnations, including Lord Rama.

However, Lord Ramachandra continues to rule the hearts of people even after millions of years. Why? Because he is not an ordinary man. He is God. How do we claim that Lord Ramachandra is God and not an ordinary person? The basis for determining who is God or who is an incarnation of God and who is not, is not just on the basis of popularity. The basis are the Vedas. The Vedas must declare that this person is Bhagavan or God. In the first canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam, there is a description of the different incarnations or avatara of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. One of them is mentioned as below: Naradevatvam Aapan Naha Surakarya Chikirsaya Samudra Nigraha Dini Chakrae Veeryaaniyata Param. In the 18th incarnation, this is what this verse of the Bhagavatam is saying. In the 18th incarnation, the Supreme Lord appeared as King Rama. In order to perform some pleasing work for the demigods, for the devatas, he exhibited superhuman powers by controlling the Indian Ocean and then killing the Atheist King Ravana who was on the other side of the sea. This is what the Srimad Bhagavatam says.

---
### Lord Ramachandra as avatara, Srimad Bhagavatam, Padma Purana, Vishnu Dharmotara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m-JB2-c4bFo?t=126
**Verse References:** SB 9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We can see here clearly that Lord Rama is declared to be the incarnation of the supreme personality of God and unequivocally in the Srimad Bhagavatam."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to establish Lord Ramachandra as an incarnation of God, referencing the Srimad Bhagavatam, Padma Purana, and Vishnu Dharmotara.

So Bhagavatam is saying Lord Ramachandra is an incarnation, avatara. Again in the 9th canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam it is mentioned, Being prayed for by the devatas, by the demigods, the supreme personality of God and the absolute truth himself, directly appeared with his expansion and expansions of the expansion. Their holy names were Rama, Lakshmana, Bharata and Satrughna. These celebrated incarnations thus appeared in four forms as the sons of Maharaj Dasaratha. This is what the Srimad Bhagavatam, the 9th canto says. We can see here clearly that Lord Rama is declared to be the incarnation of the supreme personality of God and unequivocally in the Srimad Bhagavatam. The Padma Purana declares that Lord Ramachandra is Narayana himself, while the Vishnu Dharmotara declares that Lord Ramachandra is an incarnation of Narayana. So Lord Ramachandra is not an ordinary person.

---
### Lord Ramachandra as expansion of God, Brahma Samhita, Vedic declarations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/m-JB2-c4bFo?t=230
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Thus in multiple places, apart from the Ramayana, Lord Rama has been declared to be an incarnation of the supreme personality of Godhead in the Vedas, and hence we accept him as such."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating Lord Ramachandra's status as an incarnation of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, citing the Brahma Samhita and emphasizing the Vedic basis for this understanding.

But he is an expansion or incarnation, the avatara of the supreme personality of Godhead. In various parts, apart from the Ramayana, we can see how Lord Ramachandra is declared to be the supreme Lord himself. The Brahma Samhita also states, I worship the supreme personality of Godhead, Govinda, Krishna, who is always situated in various incarnations such as Rama, Narasimha and many sub-incarnations as well, but who is the original personality of Godhead, known as Krishna, and who incarnates personally also. So even the Brahma Samhita also declares that Lord Ramachandra is an incarnation. Thus in multiple places, apart from the Ramayana, Lord Rama has been declared to be an incarnation of the supreme personality of Godhead in the Vedas, and hence we accept him as such.

### Vedic Cosmology, Time Calculation, Sankalpa Mantra

Lord Ramachandra appeared 17.28 million years ago. Where are we right now on that time scale? Adya Brahmanah Dvitiya Pararthi. What this mantra essentially does, we are in the first day of the 51st year of Brahma. Whenever a temple observes a festival, the important events of such festivals are marked with chanting of various mantras. One of the mantras chanted at the beginning of every event is known as the Sankalpa Mantra, which goes on to say, something like this.

### Sankalpa Mantra, Vedic Geography, Time Location
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vpe776XCgDc?t=45
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "What this mantra essentially does is, to locate the space and time of the event."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning and purpose of the Sankalpa Mantra, emphasizing its role in locating events in space and time within the Vedic cosmological framework.

Jambudvipa Bhara Tavarshae Bharatakhande Mero Dakshanae Parshvae Asmin Vartamane Vyavaharike Prabhavadi Shashti Samvatsaraana Madhyay Sri Subhakrit Nama Samvatsare. And like this it goes on to mention the month, the masa, the vara, the tithi, etc. What this mantra essentially does is to locate the space and time of the event. When we say Jambudvipa, Bharatavarsha, Bharatakhanda, Merohodakshinaparshva, what is happening? It is locating our current position within the universe.

---
### Vedic Astrology, Samvatsaras, Time Cycles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vpe776XCgDc?t=123
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We are currently in the 36th Samvatsara by the name Shubhakrit."
**Summary:** The speaker delves into the concept of Samvatsaras in Vedic astrology, explaining the cycle of 60 years and the current year, Shubhakrit.

And as far as time is concerned, this is how we are located. We are right now in the Shubhakrit Samvatsara. What does this mean? In Vedic astrology, the Samvatsaras are years which are calculated in terms of the time Brihaspati or Jupiter takes to traverse the 12 Rasis. This comes to about 360 days to traverse one Rasi. One cycle of Brihaspati to cover all the 12 Rasis comes to 12 Samvatsaras. One Chakra or cycle of Samvatsaras is 5 cycles or 60 Samvatsaras. Each of these 60 Samvatsaras is given a particular name. And the first Samvatsara is called Prabhava. We are currently in the 36th Samvatsara by the name Shubhakrit. Hence, in the Sankalpa Mantra, we say Prabhavadi Shashti Samvatsarana Madhe Shubhakrit Nama Samvatsare. Shashti means 60. Samvatsarana Madhe means in the middle of the 60 Samvatsaras. Prabhavadi means beginning with the Samvatsara named Prabhava. Shubhakrit Nama Samvatsare means in the Samvatsara by the name Shubhakrit. The 36th Samvatsara in the middle of the 60 Samvatsara cycle is called Shubhakrit.

---
### Kali Yuga, Mahayuga, Manvantara, Brahma's Day
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vpe776XCgDc?t=216
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We are now in Vaivasvata Manvantara, we are in the Manvantara of Vaivasvata Manu."
**Summary:** The speaker expands on the larger time scales, explaining the concepts of Kali Yuga, Mahayuga, Manvantara, and a day of Brahma, placing our current time within these cycles.

Now, going up higher in the time scale, from the cycle of 60 Samvatsaras, we are now in the Ashtavimshatitame Kali Yuge Prathamapade. That is, we are in the Prathamapada or first quarter of Kali Yuga. Which Kali Yuga? The Ashtavimshatitame Kali Yuge in the 28th Kali Yuga. The Kali Yuga is one of the four Yugas which constitute a Mahayuga. The Mahayuga is the cycle of the Satya Yuga, Dwapara Yuga, Treta Yuga and Kali Yuga. Out of that, we have right now passed a little more than 5100 years. One Mahayuga constitutes a cycle of Satya Yuga, Dwapara Yuga, Treta Yuga and Kali Yuga. After the present Kali Yuga ends, which will take about another 427000 years, once again Satya Yuga will start, and the Yugas will go on in the same cycle. So, we are now in the 28th Kali Yuga. One Mahayuga or a cycle of Satya, Dwapara, Treta and Kali Yugas lasts for 43.2 lakhs or 4.32 million years. There are 71 such Mahayugas in the lifespan of one Manu. Now we are in Vaivasvata Manvantara, we are in the Manvantara of Vaivasvata Manu. So, we are in the 28th Mahayuga or the 28th Kali Yuga, out of 71 Mahayugas of Vaivasvata Manu. And Vaivasvata Manu is the 7th out of the 14 Manus in a day of Brahma. That means it is right now mid-day for Lord Brahma because we are in the 7th Manu out of 14 Manus. In one day of Brahma, there are 14 Manus or 1000 Mahayugas or 4.32 billion years. And similar is Brahma's night. So, 24 hours of Brahma's night is actually, for us, 8.64 billion years.

---
### Brahma's Lifespan, Shwetavaraha Kalpa, Krishna's Appearance, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's Appearance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vpe776XCgDc?t=344
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Krishna appeared in this 28th Mahayuga, in the same Mahayuga where we are all currently in."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to describe Brahma's lifespan and then connects it to the appearances of Lord Krishna and Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu within the current Mahayuga.

360 such days constitute one year of Brahma or it comes to 3.11 trillion years. And one Pararth of Brahma is 50 such years which comes to 155.5 trillion years and Brahma lives for 2 such Pararth's which comes to a total of 311 trillion years. Where are we right now on that time scale? Adya Brahmanah Dvitiya Parardhe meaning we are right now in the second Parardha of Lord Brahma and in that we are in Shwetavaraha Kalpe. We are in the Shwetavaraha Kalpa or Shwetavaraha Day of Brahma. Shwetavaraha Day is the first day of Brahma of the Parardha. That means we are in the first day of the 51st year of Brahma and so we can all say happy birthday to Lord Brahma. And in that we are at midday of Brahma. It is about noon time for Brahma now in the 7th Manu within which we are in the 28th Mahayuga of the 7th Manu which is Vaivasvata Manu. Lord Krishna appeared in this 28th Mahayuga, in the same Mahayuga where we are all currently in. Lord Krishna appears only once in a day of Lord Brahma and in the Mahayuga where Lord Krishna appears a unique thing happens. The Dwapara Yuga comes after Treta Yuga. Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu also appears in the Kali Yuga in the same Mahayuga where Krishna appears in a day of Brahma. That means Chaitanya Mahaprabhu also appears only once in a day of Brahma.

### Lord Rama's Appearance, Vedic Scriptures, Time Calculation, Lagubhagavata Amrita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vpe776XCgDc?t=442
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Ramachandra appeared four Mahayugas before our current Mahayuga, where we are all right now."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses misconceptions about the timing of Lord Rama's appearance and clarifies, based on Srila Rupakaswami's Lagubhagavata Amrita, that Lord Rama appeared in the Treta Yuga of the 24th Mahayuga.

So in this mega time scale of the Universe when does Lord Ramachandra appears? You would all have heard many things from many sources. Some people say Lord Rama appears in the 7th Mahayuga. It is not true. The Lord Ramachandra appears in the 7th Mahayuga. It is not the same. The Lord Rama appeared 6,000 years ago, or 7,000 years ago, some say 20 or 30,000 years ago, and so on. We should not go by hearsay, but we should go by what the Acharyas and the Shastras, the Vedic scriptures have stated. Srila Rupakaswami, in the Lagubhagavata Amrita, he mentions like this, Kausalyaayaam Dasarathaan, Navadurva Jaladyutihi, Tretaayaam Aavirbhavat, Chaturvimshe Chaturyuge, Bharatena Sumitraya, Nandana Abhyamcha Samyutaha. Srila Rupakaswami is actually describing the appearance of Lord Ramachandra. He says, Chaturvimshe Chaturyuge Tretaayaam, meaning in the Tretaayuga of the 24th Mahabharata, Lord Ramachandra appears as the son of Dasaratha and Kausalya, along with Bharatha and the two sons of Sumitra, who are Lakshmana and Shatrughna. In other words, Lord Ramachandra appeared four Mahayugas before our current Mahayuga, where we are all right now. That means Lord Ramachandra appeared about 172.8 lakh years, or 17.28 million years, ago. This is the actual time scale at which Sri Ramachandra appeared on this earth.
