## WAS RTVIK SYSTEM TEMPORARY? | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### Ritvik system, Deeksha, Parampara, ISKCON Law
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/B2AxofMdAyU?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Ritvikism directly goes against the principle of parampara itself of successive Deeksha and Sikhsha Gurus which sustains the pure teachings and practices of Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic of the Ritvik system of initiations, Srila Prabhupada's July 9th directive, and the GBC's opposition to it through ISKCON law 6.4.7.2, which labels Ritvikism as a dangerous philosophical deviation.

Hare Krishna! Vishwaguru Srila Prabhupada ki Jai! Srila Prabhupada very carefully formulated a system of initiations via Ritviks to ensure that the movement and the parampara continue smoothly even after his disappearance from this world. And Srila Prabhupada did this through the July 9th, 1977 directive. This July 9th, 1977 directive is one of the most important directions for the institution because in Gaudiya Vaishnava Siddhanta, Deeksha is the foremost step in a devotee's spiritual life which eternally binds the devotee to his spiritual master and sets him on the path home, back home, back to Godhead. The ISKCON Mumbai Group's GBC has chosen to disobey this direct order of Srila Prabhupada in the matter of initiations or Deeksha. Not only has the GBC decided to disobey Srila Prabhupada's July 9th order, they have also decided that in ISKCON, nobody else should be allowed to follow this order of Srila Prabhupada. And so, they have formulated ISKCON law 6.4.7.2 outlawed and banned practices which states the doctrine of the ISKCON, doctrine that Srila Prabhupada desired to continue to act as Deeksha Guru after his departure from this world and did not desire any of his disciples to give Deeksha in succession after him is a dangerous philosophical deviation. Ritvikism directly goes against the principle of parampara itself of successive Deeksha and Sikhsha Gurus which sustains the pure teachings and practices of Krishna consciousness. This principle has been established by Krishna and is upheld by all Vaishnava Acharyas. Indeed, it is accepted by all followers of Vedic culture. Ritvikism is thus an extreme deviation. It is utterly erroneous to espouse it, deluding and misguiding to teach it and blasphemous to attribute it to Srila Prabhupada. No one who espouses, teaches, or practices Ritvikism can be a member in good standing of ISKCON. So, in this ISKCON law, according to the GBC, Ritvikism is a doctrine that Srila Prabhupada desired to continue to act as Deeksha Guru in succession after him and it is a dangerous philosophical deviation.

---
### Order vs. Doctrine, Chanting, 16 Rounds, Principle
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/B2AxofMdAyU?t=168
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To chant 16 rounds of Hare Krishna Mahamantra is not any doctrine. You won't find any mention of such a thing in any Shastra."
**Summary:** The speaker argues that the July 9th directive is an order, not a doctrine, and uses the analogy of chanting 16 rounds to illustrate the difference between a principle found in Shastra and its specific application by an Acharya.

What we need to understand is that the July 9th directive of Srila Prabhupada, it's an order, not a doctrine. To understand the difference between order and doctrine, let me give you a simple example. Suppose I ask you, how many rounds of Hare Krishna Mahamantra do you chant daily? You will respond that you chant 16 rounds. Now, if I ask you, why 16 rounds? Why are you chanting 16 rounds? You will answer, because Srila Prabhupada ordered all devotees in ISKCON to chant 16 rounds. That's why. Now, suppose I respond that, oh, so you espouse the doctrine of 16-roundism, which is a doctrine that Srila Prabhupada desired that every devotee in ISKCON should continue to chant 16 rounds after his departure and it is a dangerous philosophical deviation. Does it make any sense? Obviously not. Why? Because to chant 16 rounds of Hare Krishna Mahamantra is not any doctrine. You won't find any mention of such a thing in any Shastra. Nowhere in Shastra will you find it prescribed that you have to chant 16 rounds. You won't find any precedence of it in our Sampradaya or any other Sampradaya. The fact is that to chant a fixed number of times daily as per a vow is the principle. Sankhya Purvaka Nama. Nama, to chant the holy name a fixed number of times daily is the principle. Now, how many rounds to chant? That is left to the Acharya to determine based on various considerations like time, place, circumstance, etc. Hence, Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur ordered to chant 64 rounds in Gaudiya Matha and Srila Prabhupada ordered to chant 16 rounds in ISKCON. Whether 64 or 16 rounds, both don't have any precedence or Shastric reference. That is because the principle only is there in the Shastra. And what is the principle? One must chant as per a vrata or vow.

---
### Deeksha, Guru, Bhajana Kriya, Application of Principle
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/B2AxofMdAyU?t=335
**Verse References:** SB 11.2.40
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is clear that the principle is that one must take Deeksha from a spiritual master to progress in devotional life to the next stage of Anarthanivritti and then further on till one reaches the stage of Krishna Prema."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the principle of taking Deeksha from a spiritual master, referencing Srimad Bhagavatam and Chaitanya Charitamrita, and argues that the Ritvik system is an application of this principle given by Srila Prabhupada in his July 9th order.

In the Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 11, Chapter 2, Verse number 40, it is said, Evam vratah svapriya nama kirtiya. Evam vratah svapriya nama kirtiya. By chanting Krishna's holy name, a fixed number of times daily as a vow, one will develop love for Krishna. That is the principle. Now, when applying this principle, Srila Prabhupada has decided that the number of times should be 16 rounds. Similarly, the principle as per Shastra is Adau Guruva Shayam. One's spiritual life begins when one takes Deeksha or initiation from the spiritual master. Adau Shraddha Tata Sadhu Sangha Tato Bhajana Kriya. This is the famous verse from the Nectar of Devotion, Bhakti Rasamrita Sindhu. So, at the Bhajana Kriya stage, one decides to take formal Deeksha. In the Chaitanya Charitamrita, Madhyalira, Chapter 23, Verses 14 and 15, give this Adau Shraddha verse. And it states, Srila Prabhupada, in the synonyms, word-to-word synonyms of the Bhajana Kriya verse, he mentions the Bhajana Kriya, he has translated it as, Performance of Devotion. He is giving formal service to Krishna, surrendering to the spiritual master and being encouraged by the association of devotees, so that initiation takes place. In the translation to these verses also, Srila Prabhupada in the Chaitanya Charitamrita says, In the beginning, there must be faith. That is Adau Shraddha. Then one becomes interested in associating with pure devotees. This is Sadhu Sangha. Thereafter, one is initiated by the spiritual master and executes the regulative principles under his order. So, it is clear that the principle is that one must take Deeksha from a spiritual master to progress in devotional life to the next stage of Anarthanivritti and then further on till one reaches the stage of Krishna Prema. That is the principle. Now, how to get the formal Deeksha? That is an application of the principle that one must take Deeksha at the stage of Bhajana Kriya from a pure devotee. And Srila Prabhupada has given the application, the application of the principle in his July 9th order. So, therefore, it is not necessary that we will find either chanting 16 rounds or 64 rounds or the Rithvik system of initiations in Shastra. So, the GBC calling the Rithvik system of initiations as a doctrine is wrong. Just like saying 16 roundism is a doctrine. So, like that, Rithvikism is a doctrine is also wrong. The Rithvik system of initiations is an order of Srila Prabhupada as to how initiations will be conducted in ISKCON, particularly after his disappearance.

---
### July 9th Directive, Authority, Vani vs. Vapu, Philosophical Deviation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/B2AxofMdAyU?t=476
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The July 9th directive gets its authority because of Srila Prabhupada's vani, his order. It is his order. Therefore, it has authenticity and potency."
**Summary:** The speaker presents Amayatma Prabhu's argument that the July 9th directive's authority comes from Srila Prabhupada's vani (order), not his vapu (body), and therefore remains valid even after his physical disappearance.

Another flaw in this ISKCON law which is brought up by His Grace Amayatma Prabhu is worth considering. So, I will put Amayatma Prabhu's argument in my own words. As per the GBC, Srila Prabhupada's July 9th order appointing Rithviks was only temporary to be followed until Srila Prabhupada was physically present. That means that the GBC accepts that at least between July 9th, 1977, if not earlier, and till November 14th, 1977, the Rithvik system of initiations was valid and that the July 9th order was authentic and potent, at least for that period during Srila Prabhupada's physical presence. That raises the question, as to what forms the basis, what is the foundation of authenticity or what lends power to the July 9th order? Does the July 9th order get its authority or authenticity or potency from Srila Prabhupada's vapu or his vani? Is the July 9th order getting its power from Srila Prabhupada's body? You don't need to be an Einstein to get the answer right, the July 9th directive gets its authority because of Srila Prabhupada's vani, his order. It is his order. Therefore, it has authenticity and potency. Now, if the July 9th directive is getting its power and potency because of Srila Prabhupada's order, not his body, then Srila Prabhupada's physical presence or absence should not make any difference. Why? Because Srila Prabhupada carries the same potency, authority and authenticity even without his physical presence.

Isn't the GBC's idea to tie Srila Prabhupada's order or his vani to his physical presence by coining a new term called pastimus Ritvik initiation? Isn't that a dangerous philosophical deviation? Isn't the GBC's assertion that Srila Prabhupada's orders must be tied to his body and once he disappears, his orders are no longer valid? Isn't that a dangerous philosophical deviation? **Summary:** The speaker continues to elaborate on Srila Prabhupada's reasons for forming the GBC, despite the existing management structure in ISKCON's centers, emphasizing the need for further improvement and standardization.

So, you see, Srila Prabhupada is saying here that he wants to instruct his disciples when if it is necessary I think that he wants the disciple how they should manage the institution after Prabhupada's disappearance. That's why he formed the GBC. They are already managing individual centers represented by one president, one secretary, and one treasurer. And in my opinion, they are doing nice. But we want still more improvement in the standard of temple management, propaganda for Krishna consciousness, distribution of books and literatures, opening of new centers, and educating devotees to the right standard. Therefore, I have decided to adopt the following principles and I hope my beloved disciples will kindly accept them. So, we can see what was running in Srila Prabhupada's mind in 1970. ISKCON had already grown to 34 centers. They had their temple president, secretary, etc. And in Srila Prabhupada's own words, they were doing nice. Then what was the need for a GBC?

### GBC, ISKCON, Authority, Power Struggle
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N7Sp-7Etfco?t=419
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The primary reason, as Srila Prabhupada said at that time was, I am getting old, 75 years old. Therefore, at any time, I may be out of the scene. Therefore, I think it is necessary to give instruction to my disciples how they shall manage the whole institution."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates Srila Prabhupada's primary reason for establishing the GBC, emphasizing his desire to ensure the GBC's authority would be accepted and to prevent power struggles after his disappearance.

The primary reason, as Srila Prabhupada said at that time was, I am getting old, 75 years old. Therefore, at any time, I may be out of the scene. Therefore, I think it is necessary to give instruction to my disciples how they shall manage the whole institution. That means the GBC was meant to manage ISKCON after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance. By forming the GBC before his disappearance, Srila Prabhupada ensured that the authority of the GBC would be accepted by all his disciples and no one would question its authority after his disappearance. By giving managing power in the hands of the GBC in his physical presence, he preempted any ugly power struggle or dissension upon his disappearance. That would also mitigate the risk of ambitious disappearance.

### GBC, Diksha Gurus, ISKCON, Gaudiya Mutt
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N7Sp-7Etfco?t=483
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The GBC may have been an ideal for many disciples, artificially declaring themselves as diksha gurus and acharyas unauthorisedly after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance, leading to the splitting of the movement like Gaudiya Mathu."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how the GBC was intended to prevent ambitious disciples from declaring themselves as Diksha Gurus and Acharyas, potentially leading to a split in the movement, similar to what happened in Gaudiya Mathu.

The GBC may have been an ideal for many disciples, artificially declaring themselves as diksha gurus and acharyas unauthorisedly after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance, leading to the splitting of the movement like Gaudiya Mathu. But unfortunately, big gradually, one would say that after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance, despite Srila Prabhupada's explicit warning in his 1974 letter to not repeat the mistake of Gaudiya Mathu, the ambitious devotees in ISKCON did exactly that. that. They declared themselves as Diksha Gurus in March 1978. Maybe, probably because Shaila Prabhupada had set up the GBC and run it for 7 straight years, the authority of the GBC was ingrained in the minds of all the rank and file devotees. And so, therefore, the 11 Diksha Gurus could not dismantle the GBC. And that's why probably unlike the Gaudiamant which split into many, many factions after Shaila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur's disappearance with no GBC to keep it together. Unlike that, ISKCON did not split up. Although, instead of 2 in Gaudiamant, in ISKCON, there were 11 Acharyas and still the ISKCON did not go on to split up because the GBC was there. So, you see Prabhupada's system of the GBC worked to some extent in ISKCON. But unfortunately, it did not preempt the ambitious Diksha Gurus. Devotees from declaring themselves as Diksha Gurus.

So, they said the 11 were selected by Prabhupada. This they said in 1978. Later in 1999, the GBC in the resolution number 403 accepted that they had lied when they resolved in resolution 403. Unfortunately, shortly after Shaila Prabhupada's disappearance, the GBC allowed for an unauthorized zonal Acharya system to evolve in ISKCON, elevating 11 exclusive successor Diksha Gurus.

Let us hear from Srila Prabhupada himself about their performance in governing ISKCON between 1970 and 1977. I set up the GBC with hope that I shall get relief from administration of the mission, but on the contrary, I have become the center of receiving so many complaints. So, it is not a relief for me. ### GBC, ISKCON, Authority, Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N7Sp-7Etfco?t=774
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But in the meantime, they have decided some appointments without consulting me."
**Summary:** The speaker presents evidence from 1972, where Srila Prabhupada expressed strong disapproval of the GBC's actions, particularly their appointments made without his consultation, highlighting a lack of respect for his authority.

But then they were just one year old. So, we can forgive them for their shortcomings. Maybe with time, they could do better. But look at what happened in 1972. The next year, on 8th of April 1972, Srila Prabhupada sent this memo to all the ISKCON temple presidents. Please accept my blessings. I beg to inform you that recently, some of the governing body commission members held a meeting at New York on 25th through 28th March 1972. And they have sent me a big, big minutes, duplicated, for my consideration and approval. But in the meantime, they have decided some appointments without consulting me. One of the items which struck me very much is as follows. Open quotes, Atre Rishi Das was selected to be the secretary for GBC and receive all correspondence including monthly reports. Close quotes. I never appointed Atre Rishi member of the GBC and I do not know how he can be appointed secretary to GBC without my sanction. Once again, open quotes, he was also appointed to be on the management committee with Karandhara for the purpose of supervising ISKCON business and implementing the decisions reached by the GBC. Close quotes. This has very much disturbed me. Sriman Atre Rishi Das may be very expert. But without my say, he has been given so much power and this has upset my brain. I also understand that immediate actions are going to take place even prior to my permission and that also without divulging to the devotees. I do not follow exactly what is the motive of the so-called GBC meeting.

But on the contrary, complaints and counter complaints are coming to me. Then how my brain can be peaceful? So, things definitely hadn't improved from 1971 to 1975. What Shaila Prabhupada said in 1971 that the GBC was not giving him relief was the same thing Shaila Prabhupada repeated in 1974 as well as in 1975. About a month later, Shaila Prabhupada wrote this letter to Alal Nath on 10th of November 1975. Now has the GBC become more than Guru Maharaj? Today, we will look at what is Deeksha or initiation and how the Rithvik system is not violating any of the principles of initiation or Deeksha. Before we dive into that question, let us take an overview of how Srila Prabhupada built up the institution of ISKCON and how the system of initiations evolved as the institution grew from 1966 to 1977. Srila Prabhupada built up from scratch a worldwide organization in a relatively short span of 12 years. From 1965 to 2077.

Especially with Srila Prabhupada's frail health in 1967. It was actually after 1968 that the movement really took off. In a lecture in 1971, Srila Prabhupada said, in this lecture given on 15th February 1971, So, practically we began work from 1968. 66 I started, but in 67 I became very much sick. ### Srila Prabhupada, Leadership, ISKCON growth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yMdIaIRgz-g?t=123
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

During this time between 1965 to 1977, Srila Prabhupada wrote books prolifically while he guided his spiritually naive young disciples in the nuances of detail of community worship, Vaishnava culture, hygiene standards, personal attire and the like. Simultaneously, Srila Prabhupada stitched together the disparate but sincere attempts of his disciples into a coherent force resoundingly heard across countries. The world sat up and took notice. Not because of the genius marketing strategies, masterminded by some devotee, but by the transcendental vision and potency of the pure devotee, Srila Prabhupada. Srila Prabhupada was like the lens which concentrates the divergent rays of the sun into a searing focal point. What otherwise appeared like random attempts by the devotees to spread the moment was coordinated by Srila Prabhupada into a symphony which rose elegantly in time.

### Srila Prabhupada, Leadership, ISKCON growth

Srila Prabhupada was the expert conductor of this orchestra. Without Srila Prabhupada, it would have been nothing but cacophony. Everything was happening at a frenetic pace. Books were being published in an unending stream. Devotees were joining in droves. And temples were being opened in every nook and cranny of the world. The world was proving to be too small for the devotees. Unsurprisingly, although everything was expanding at a scorching pace between 1965 and 1977, nothing was haphazard or mindless. There was a method to the madness. And this was unsurprising given how Srila Prabhupada knew exactly what needed to be done, when and how in all matters. Srila Prabhupada was the sutradhara, the puppeteer, deftly coordinating the whole show into one smooth integrated growth in unison without any discord.

Although, such appointments appeared to be a result of random adjustments to address practical difficulties and challenges as they arose. In reality, those circumstances were only instrumental in the implementation of an already existing, well thought out system in Srila Prabhupada's vision for initiations in the moment, which could run for eternity. Srila Prabhupada as always knew precisely what he was doing and why, being the sharp transcendental visionary that he was. But this was not obvious to all. ### Diksha, Initiation, Transcendental Knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yMdIaIRgz-g?t=533
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Diksha is the process by which one can awaken his transcendental knowledge and vanquish all reactions caused by sinful activity."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to define Diksha or initiation according to Srila Prabhupada and Jeeva Goswami, emphasizing the awakening of transcendental knowledge and the vanquishing of sinful reactions as the essence of initiation.

how does Srila Prabhupada define Diksha or initiation? Srila Prabhupada, in his purport to verse number 331 of chapter 24 of the Madhya Leela of the Chaitanya Charitamrita, mentions, as far as Diksha is concerned, one should consult Madhya Leela, chapter 15, verse 108. And if we go to Chaitanya Charitamrita, Madhya Leela, chapter 15, verse 108 purport, there Srila Prabhupada says, Srila Jeeva Goswami explains Diksha in his Bhakti Sandarbha, verse 283. Divyam Jnanam Yatho Dadyat Kuryat Papasya Sankshayam Tasma Dikshe Tisa Prokta Desikais Tattva Kovidaihi Diksha is the process by which one can awaken his transcendental knowledge and vanquish all reactions caused by sinful activity. A person expert in the study of the revealed scriptures knows this process as Diksha. So, essentially, as per the definition given by Srila Jeeva Goswami, Diksha or initiation constitutes two fundamental things. One is awakening transcendental knowledge and the second is vanquishing all sinful reactions. This is the real essence of Diksha or initiation.

---
### Diksha, Initiation, Spiritual Master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yMdIaIRgz-g?t=634
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Diksha Guru takes responsibility to take the disciple back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker lists commonly understood components of initiation, including instructions, inquiry, service, surrender, fire yajna, new name, sanctified beads, destruction of karma, revelation of transcendental knowledge, and the Guru's responsibility to take the disciple back to Godhead.

Now, let us list down some of the commonly understood components of initiation or Diksha. Some of them are like this. 1. The spiritual master gives instructions to the disciple about his service. 2. The disciple inquires about his spiritual doubts from the spiritual master, from the Diksha Guru. 3. The disciple serves the spiritual master by carrying out his instructions. 4. The disciple surrenders to the Diksha Guru. 5. A fire yajna is conducted to formalize the acceptance of the spiritual master by the disciple. 6. After the yajna, a new spiritual name is given to the disciple. 7. Sanctified beads are handed over to the disciple who verbs to chant the prescribed number of rounds of Hare Krishna Maha Mantra on those beads. All previous karmic reactions of the disciple are destroyed. This is point number 8. And point number 9. Transcendental knowledge is revealed in the heart of the disciple by the Diksha Guru. Number 10. The Diksha Guru takes responsibility to take the disciple back to Godhead. So, these are some of the commonly understood components of initiation or Diksha.

---
### Diksha, Initiation, Spiritual Master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yMdIaIRgz-g?t=732
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All these components of Diksha or initiation may be summarized in a table as you can see on the screen now."
**Summary:** The speaker summarizes the components of Diksha into a table with three columns: physical/personal aspects, ceremonial aspects, and transcendental aspects, categorizing various activities under each.

All these components of Diksha or initiation may be summarized in a table as you can see on the screen now. Under the first column of physical or personal aspects are listed the activities of Guru instructs on service of the disciple. Disciple carries out the instructions of the Diksha Guru. Diksha Guru clears the spiritual doubts and corrects the disciple's mistakes. These activities require a physically present person to instruct, to correct, to clear specific doubts, etc. Hence, we have included them under the physical or personal aspect. Now, under the second column of ceremonial aspects are listed the activities of formal surrender by the disciple to the Diksha Guru, formal acceptance by the Diksha Guru of the disciple, initiation yajna, Diksha yajna, initiation sacrifice, sanctified beads being handed over to the disciple, and new spiritual name being selected for the disciple. These are generally related to the initiation ceremony or the Diksha ceremony. Therefore, we have called this the ceremonial aspects of Diksha. Under the third column of transcendental aspects are listed the activities of disciple accepting the Diksha Guru in his heart and vice versa, the Guru accepting the disciple. Then the Guru reveals transcendental knowledge in the heart of the disciple. Then the Diksha Guru destroys previous karma of the disciple and the Diksha Guru takes the disciple back to Godhead.

Here is what Srila Prabhupada said on this matter on various occasions. Similarly, the GBC member means they will see that in every temple, these books are very thoroughly being read and discussed and understood and applied in practical life. The GBC can supervise the things that are going on. The first management is that each and every member in the temple is chanting extremely long things and following the regulations. The representative of all five members must see that the members are chanting. So all GBC members must see that in every temple everyone is engaged. So the purpose of having in the society is to show the devotees how they can always be in 24 hours engaged according to your... Yes, that is helping one another. If I am deficient, by seeing your example, I shall correct myself. This is the idea. Not that a fool's paradise. All fools join together. Not like that. There should be ideal life. At least the leaders, the president, the GBC, they will show the example and they will follow. Srila Prabhupada, there is also a question of reciprocation. You have so many disciples, thousands. And one devotee was asking me yesterday, I want to please Srila Prabhupada. How does he know my progress and my service when I am so far away from him and if I don't write him? So there is a representative there, the president, the GBC. They will see. The representative may be there, but what is my personal relationship? To obey your spiritual master. Whatever he has said, you follow strictly. Follow the regulative principle, chant sixteen now. That's all.

What happens when that inevitable time comes when a successor is needed? He is asking about the future. Who will guide the movement in the future? They will guide. I am training them. Will there be one spiritual leader though? I am training GBC 18 all over the world. His personal secretaries. I see. To see that the original teachings that Prabhupada has given are not in any way changed. In addition to whatever you just heard, in the Chaitanya Charitamrita, Adi Leela, chapter 1, verse 35 purport, Srila Prabhupada says, A devotee must have only one initiating spiritual master. Because in the scriptures, acceptance of more than one is always forbidden. There is no limit, however, to the number of instructing spiritual masters one may accept. Generally, a spiritual master who constantly instructs a disciple in spiritual science becomes his initiating spiritual master later on.

### Diksha, Initiation, Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yMdIaIRgz-g?t=1070

From this, from whatever you have heard until now, we can see clearly that Srila Prabhupada delegated the aspect of initiation or Deeksha which requires physical presence. Srila Prabhupada delegated that to the temple presidents and to the GBCs. The temple presidents and the GBCs are supposed to be Siksha Gurus taking care of correcting, clearing doubts and assigning services to the devotees. Srila Prabhupada through his books delivers spiritual instructions to the disciples which we have classified as revealing transcendental knowledge within the heart under the column transcendental aspects. But if any devotee is in doubt after reading Srila Prabhupada's books, what does he do? Whom does he go to clear his doubts? Those doubts are to be cleared by the temple president and the GBC which falls under the first column which is called the physical or personal aspects.

You have the threads chanted on by me and now I am enclosing here with copies of Gayathri Mantra, one for each of the seven disciples to be initiated. Hold a fire ceremony. Chant Gayathri Mantra to them individually. And let them hear and repeat. Then offer the threads on my behalf. The fire sacrifice should be performed as usual. Here, Srila Prabhupada authorized the temple president to chant the Gayathri Mantra into the year of the devotee being initiated. In another letter to Nithyananda written on 29th of September 1972,

Srila Prabhupada said something similar to what he wrote in his 1970 letter which we saw just now. So, in 1972 also, in this letter to Nithyananda, Srila Prabhupada said, Now hold the ceremony as per the enclosed instructions. During the fire ceremony, you can also give the sacred thread and Gayathri Mantra to Bhudhara, which I am enclosing also here with. Teach him to chant on the finger divisions and play the copy of the tape of me reciting Gayathri Mantra into his right ear. Also, I am sending the beads for Michael Lightfoot duly chanted by me under separate post. So, you may hold the fire yajna, install DTs and give initiation to these two boys. In yet another letter to Bhutatma written on 5th of January 1973, Srila Prabhupada wrote, Upon your recommendation, I am very glad to accept the six new students as my duly initiated disciples. Their letter is enclosed herewith. As for chanting on their beads, I have delegated Kirtananda Swami to chant on the new devotees beads on my behalf in your country. In Europe, Revatinandan Swami will chant on the new devotees beads on my behalf. Because it is such long distance, therefore it is very expensive to send beads air mail from here. And there are so many instances of the beads being lost in that way. And I am getting now requests up to 20 or 30 each week. So, it is practically becoming impossible for me to chant the beads when I am so far distant. Therefore, the new devotees may send their beads to Kirtananda in New Vrindavan and he will chant the beads and send back to them. Is this all right?

### Diksha, Initiation, Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yMdIaIRgz-g?t=1451

**Summary:** The speaker presents Srila Prabhupada's letter clarifying that even with senior devotees chanting on beads, the new disciples are still considered Srila Prabhupada's disciples, emphasizing the empowerment of Rithwiks to bind the disciple to Srila Prabhupada as the Diksha Guru.

Just now I have received some more requests for giving first initiation from Dhananjaya. And now I am receiving weekly not less than 10 to 15 such requests from new students. So, it is becoming very expensive to send so many sets of beads such long distance. And it has become little bothersome for me also. So, I think now you may be appointed by me to give first initiations to new disciples by chanting on their beads on my behalf. In America, Kirtananda Swami is doing that. So, now if there are two of you, that will give me great relief. Kirtananda will chant on the beads for new devotees in America, Canada, like that. You can chant on the beads for the European continent new disciples. They shall, of course, still be considered as my disciples. Not that they shall become your disciples. But you will be empowered by me to chant their beads. And that is the same effect of binding master and disciple as if I were personally chanting. So, the senior devotees who are acting as Rithwiks were empowered by Srila Prabhupada to chant on the beads. Thus, binding Srila Prabhupada as the Diksha Guru or spiritual master to the disciple. Now, it is clear that Srila Prabhupada delegated the ceremonial aspects of the process of Diksha or initiation, the Diksha Vidhana to the senior devotees.

### Diksha, Initiation, Srila Prabhupada

**Summary:** The speaker notes that Srila Prabhupada delegated name selection to Rithwiks on July 9th and then shifts focus to the transcendental aspects of initiation, quoting Srila Prabhupada's emphasis on chanting Hare Krishna as the main business and real initiation.

And on July 9th, Srila Prabhupada delegated the choosing of names also to the Rithwiks. Now, let us look at a few more quotes of Srila Prabhupada with respect to the last column, the transcendental aspects column. In a letter to Tamal Krishna written on 19th of August 1968, Srila Prabhupada wrote, The chanting Hare Krishna is our main business. That is real initiation. And as you are all following my instruction, in that matter, the initiator is already there. Now, the next initiation will be performed as a ceremony officially. Of course, that ceremony has value because the name, holy name will be delivered to the student from the disciplic succession. It has got value. But in spite of that, as you are going on chanting, please go on with this business sincerely. And Krishna willing, I may be coming to you very soon. In another letter to Jadurani written on 4th of September 1972, Srila Prabhupada wrote, The eternal bond between disciples and spiritual master begins from the first day he hears. Just like my spiritual master, in 1922, he said in our first meeting, You are educated boys. Why don't you preach this cult? That was the beginning. Now, it is coming to fact.

### Diksha, Initiation, Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yMdIaIRgz-g?t=1670
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As Srila Prabhupada says, the ceremony has its place and importance. But without the transcendental aspects, the ceremonial aspects will not bear fruit."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes Srila Prabhupada's focus on the transcendental aspects of initiation over ceremonial and physical aspects, noting the delegation of ceremonial aspects to Rithwiks and temple presidents, culminating in the directive on July 9th, 1977.

From these quotes of Srila Prabhupada, we can see how Srila Prabhupada placed more emphasis on the transcendental aspects rather than on the ceremonial aspects or the personal or physical aspects of the process of initiation to act. As Srila Prabhupada says, the ceremony has its place and importance. But without the transcendental aspects, the ceremonial aspects will not bear fruit. Srila Prabhupada delegated the ceremonial aspect of initiation to the rithwiks. Namely, chanting on the beads, selecting the spiritual names, etc. And he delegated to the temple presidents the conducting of the fire yajna, handing over the beads and announcing the name. So like this, Srila Prabhupada delegated the ceremonial aspects to the rithwiks and to the temple presidents. And Srila Prabhupada started delegating this aspect to the temple presidents and GBCs from as early as 1970. Finally, when Srila Prabhupada had personally overseen the successful implementation of this delegation of the ceremonial aspects as well as the personal or physical aspects over the next seven years from 1970 till 1977, Srila Prabhupada gave it a solid stamp of finality via the directive on July 9th, 1977.

### Diksha, Initiation, Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yMdIaIRgz-g?t=1770

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Srila Prabhupada retained the transcendental aspect of initiation, quoting Chaitanya Charitamrita on Jeeva Goswami's definition of Diksha and the Srimad Bhagavatam's view on Deity worship.

Now what Srila Prabhupada did not delegate to his representatives, the temple presidents, the GBCs and the rithwiks is the transcendental aspect of initiation. Let us look at a few more quotes of Srila Prabhupada. In the purport to the Chaitanya Charitamrita Madhyalila, Chapter 15, verse number 108, purport, Srila Prabhupada says, Srila Jeeva Goswami explains deeksha in his Bhaktisandharbha, verse 283, Divyam Jnanam Yathodadhyat Kuryat Papasya Sankshayam Tasma Deeksheti Saa Prokta Deshikai Statva Kovidaihi Deeksha is the process by which one can awaken his transcendental knowledge and vanquish all reactions caused by sinful activity. A person expert in the study of the revealed scriptures knows this process as deeksha. Further, in the same purport, Srila Prabhupada says, It is Srimad Bhagavatam's opinion that the process of Deity worship is not actually necessary. Just as the specific prescriptions of the Pancharatra and other scriptures do not have to be followed, the Bhagavatam enjoins that even without practising Deity worship, one can achieve the complete success of human life by any of the other devotional processes, such as simply offering oneself at the Lord's feet for His protection.

## What Is The Role Of Diksa Guru In ISKCON? | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### ISKCON history, Guru system, Zonal Acharyas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In 1978, after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance, the GBC of Vizcon decided to implement an unauthorized guru system by elevating 11 devotees as zonal acharyas and diksha gurus, declaring that they had overnight become pure devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic by discussing the implementation of an unauthorized guru system in ISKCON after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance in 1978, and the subsequent failures and changes made by the GBC.

Hare Krishna! Vishwaguru Srila Prabhupada ki Jai! In 1978, after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance, the GBC of Vizcon decided to implement an unauthorized guru system by elevating 11 devotees as zonal acharyas and diksha gurus, declaring that they had overnight become pure devotees. Over the years, this unauthorized guru system has failed the devotees, with almost 75 gurus falling from grace till date.

---
### GBC Resolutions, Guru Puja, Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=39
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is considered an essential devotional practice for all ISKCON devotees to observe or perform Srila Prabhupada's guru puja daily, either in an ISKCON temple or, if not possible, in one's home."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses GBC Resolution No. 405, passed in 1999, which mandates daily guru puja for Srila Prabhupada for all ISKCON devotees, regardless of their diksha guru.

In reaction to the constant guru fall-downs, the GBC has been chopping and changing the unauthorized guru system as well as their understanding of the functions, qualifications and characteristics. In the year 1999, the GBC passed Resolution No. 405, titled Standards of Guru Worship in ISKCON, and the GBC made it into a law in ISKCON. It has become an ISKCON law. As part of this ISKCON law, the GBC resolved, under subsection C of this Resolution No. 405, in order to offer appropriate respect to Srila Prabhupada as founder acharya and pre-eminent spiritual master of ISKCON. It is considered an essential devotional practice for all ISKCON devotees to observe or perform Srila Prabhupada's guru puja daily, either in an ISKCON temple or, if not possible, in one's home.

---
### Guru Puja, Shiksha Guru, Diksha Guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=130
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "With an aim to focus more fully on Srila Prabhupada, and every devotee's special relationship with him and to correct any imbalance in application between the respect offered to shiksha gurus, diksha gurus or other senior Vaisnavas contributing to a devotee's spiritual progress, an ISKCON shiksha usingacao or diksha guru may public Guru Puja, Aarati and or foot bathing in person once a year on ISKCON property as a Vyasa Puja."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that while daily guru puja is mandated for Srila Prabhupada, guru puja for diksha gurus is optional and limited to once a year. The GBC also instructs that all disciples should consider Srila Prabhupada as their pre-eminent Shiksha Guru.

While the GBC mandated daily guru puja for Srila Prabhupada, in subsection D of the same resolution of the same ISKCON law, the GBC resolved this about their unauthorized diksha guru puja. the GBC resolved this about their unauthorized diksha guru puja. With an aim to focus more fully on Srila Prabhupada, and every devotee's special relationship with him and to correct any imbalance in application between the respect offered to shiksha gurus, diksha gurus or other senior Vaisnavas contributing to a devotee's spiritual progress, an ISKCON shiksha usingacao or diksha guru may public Guru Puja, Aarati and or foot bathing in person once a year on ISKCON property as a Vyasa Puja. So, the disciples of the unauthorized Gurus may offer, may offer means it's not mandatory, it is optional. The disciples may offer Guru Puja to their Gurus in person only once a year in ISKCON. Further, in subsection J, the GBC law states, at the time of diksha initiation, all disciples will be further instructed by their initiating guru that Shaila Prabhupad is their pre-eminent Siksha Guru as ISKCON's founder Acharya, ever present in His books and teachings. Thus, they have a recognized Siksha Guru.

---
### Shiksha Guru, Diksha Guru, Guru Vandana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=230
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That means, receiving the mercy of Guru or Shri Guru Charanapadma happens through initiation. In other words, Shri Guru Charanapadma in the Guru Vandana refers to the Deeksha Guru, not just to some preeminent Deeksha Guru."
**Summary:** The speaker analyzes the Sri Guru Vandana song, which is sung daily for Srila Prabhupada, and argues that the song's content implies that Srila Prabhupada is acting as the Diksha Guru for all devotees, despite the GBC's designation of him as a pre-eminent Shiksha Guru.

So, the disciples of the Unauthorized Gurus may offer Guru Puja to their Gurus in person link to Shaila Prabhupada. That means, in ISKCON, every devotee is supposed to consider Shaila Prabhupada as his pre-eminent Shiksha Guru, except, of course, for those initiated before 1978. So, for their pre-eminent Shiksha Guru, Shaila Prabhupada, the devotees during the Guru Puja, sing the Shri Guru Vandana song penned by Shri Launarothamadas Thakur daily, every day, mandatorily. In this song, that every devotee in ISKCON must compulsorily sing for Shaila Prabhupada every single day of his life, it begins with Shri Guru Charanapadma Kevala Bhakati Sadma, meaning, Shaila Prabhupada's lotus feet are the shelter of Shri Guru. In the Chaitanya Charitamrita, Madhya Leela, Chapter 15, verse 108 purport, Shaila Prabhupada quotes Shri Launarothamadas Thakur from his Bhakti Sandarbha, where Shri Launarothamadas Thakur says, Deeksha Vidhanena Shri Guru Charana Sampaditam. Shri Launarothamadas Thakur translates this as, Receiving the Grace of a bona fide spiritual master through initiation. That means, receiving the mercy of Guru or Shri Guru Charanapadma happens through initiation. In other words, Shri Guru Charanapadma in the Guru Vandana refers to the Deeksha Guru, not just to some preeminent Deeksha Guru. And then, the devotees, have to sing, Vandohmui Savadahana Mate, meaning, I bow down to the lotus feet of Shaila Prabhupada with great awe and reverence. So, every devotee in ISKCON has to take shelter of the lotus feet of Shaila Prabhupada. What is the meaning of taking shelter of the lotus feet of Shaila Prabhupada? We will come to that a bit later. towards the end of the Guru Vandana. Please bear with us till then.

---
### Guru Vandana, Diksha Guru, Material Existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=374
**Verse References:** CC Madhya 9.61, NOI 7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So clearly, it is the responsibility of the Diksha Guru to deliver the disciple from the material existence."
**Summary:** The speaker continues analyzing the Sri Guru Vandana, focusing on the lines about crossing over material existence by Srila Prabhupada's grace. He questions why devotees are mandated to sing about Srila Prabhupada delivering them from material existence, when scriptures state that it is the Diksha Guru's responsibility.

For now, let us go on to the next line. And what is that next line? Meaning, by Srila Prabhupada's grace, because we are singing this song for Prabhupada, this is an offering to Prabhupada, so by Srila Prabhupada's grace, we can cross over the material existence and we can attain Krishna. That means, every devotee in ISKCON must sing every single day of his life that he can cross over the material existence and attain Krishna by Srila Prabhupada's grace. Now, the question arises that who takes the responsibility to deliver the physical life of the human being? Of course, the one who takes the responsibility to deliver the devotee across the material existence to the lotus feet of Krishna? Is it the responsibility of the Shiksha Guru or is it the responsibility of the Diksha Guru? Let us see what Srila Prabhupada says in the Chaitanya Charitamrita, Madhya Leela, Chapter 9, verse 61, purport. There, Srila Prabhupada says, it is the spiritual master who delivers the disciple from the clutches of Maya. How? By initiating him into the chanting of the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra. So clearly, it is the responsibility of the Diksha Guru to deliver the disciple from the material existence. In the Nectar of Instruction, purport to verse 7, Srila Prabhupada says, if one is sincere, he is initiated and this stage is called Bhajanak Kriya. One then actually engages in the service of the Lord by regularly chanting the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra 16 rounds daily and refraining from illicit sex, intoxicants, meat-eating and gambling. By Bhajanak Kriya, one attains freedom from the contamination of materialistic life. So if the Diksha Guru delivers one from material contamination, why are the devotees in Eskan mandated to sing every day that it is Srila Prabhupada who delivers them from material existence? Instead of their Diksha Guru, they can sing about the Diksha Guru only once a day in a year. Whereas, they are singing every day to Srila Prabhupada 365 days a year. That it is Srila Prabhupada who delivers them from material existence. It is Prabhupada who delivers them from there, from the material existence. So how is it that the Shiksha Guru is delivering the disciple from the clutches of material existence whereas the Shiksha Guru is not doing it? While you ponder over that point, the whole situation becomes more complicated when the devotee sings the next line, Jai Prasad-e-Pure Sarva Asha, meaning that by Srila Prabhupada's song, all our desires for spiritual perfection will be fulfilled.

### Mercy, Diksha Guru, Krishna's Representative
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=770
**Verse References:** CC Antya 7.12, SB 4.21.31

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker continues to analyze the Sri Guru Vandana, focusing on the line that Srila Prabhupada is the ocean of mercy (Karuna Sindhu). He argues that this refers to the Diksha Guru, who is the mercy incarnation of Krishna, and questions why the GBC mandates this glorification for Srila Prabhupada instead of the unauthorized gurus.

As if that was not enough, the devotees have to further sing to Srila Prabhupada. Srila Prabhupada says, Krishnadekar, Krishnadekar, Krishnadekar, Krishnadekar. Guru Karunasindhu that Srila Prabhupada is the ocean of mercy. Again, in the Sri Chaitanya Charitamrita, Antalila, chapter 7, verse 12 purport, Srila Prabhupada says, the true Acharya, the spiritual master of the entire world, must be considered an incarnation of Krishna's mercy. So, the spiritual master is the mercy incarnation of Krishna. Which spiritual master? Preeminent Shikshaguru or initiating Dikshaguru, initiating spiritual master? Which spiritual master is this referring to? Srila Prabhupada explains in Srimad Bhagavatam, canto 4, chapter 21, verse 31 purport, as Krishna takes away all the sinful reactions of a person, immediately, upon his surrender unto him. Similarly, the external manifestation of Krishna, the representative of Krishna, who acts as the mercy of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, takes all the resultant actions of the sinful life of the disciple, immediately after the disciple's initiation. So, it is the spiritual master who gives initiation to the disciple. So, it is the spiritual master who gives initiation to the disciple. The Dikshaguru, who is the mercy incarnation of Krishna. So, why has the GBC mandated to all the devotees in ISKCON to glorify Srila Prabhupada daily, that Srila Prabhupada is the ocean of mercy, the Karuna Sindhu, meaning the manifestation of mercy of Krishna, or in other words, the Dikshaguru, that Srila Prabhupada is the Dikshaguru, and not one of the unauthorized Gurus, indicated no objection by the GBC.

**Summary:** The speaker analyzes the final lines of the Sri Guru Vandana, where devotees seek shelter at the lotus feet of Srila Prabhupada. He argues that taking shelter means taking initiation, implying that Srila Prabhupada is acting as the Diksha Guru for all ISKCON devotees.

Haha Prabhu Karo Daya Deho More Paadachchaya, meaning, Srila Prabhupada, our spiritual master, please give us the shelter of your lotus feet. Like the Sri Guru Vandana began with Vandomui Savadhana Mate, I bow down at your lotus feet with great reverence. In the Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 4, Chapter 29, Verse 55, purport, Srila Prabhupada explains this. Any person who is seriously desirous of achieving real happiness must seek out a bona fide spiritual master and take shelter of him by initiation. Not only that, in the Nectar of Devotion, Chapter 6, Srila Prabhupada says, all together, Srila Rupa Goswami mentions twenty items and all of them are very important. Out of the twenty, the first three, namely accepting the shelter of a bona fide spiritual master, taking initiation from him and serving him with respect and reverence are the most important. So, taking shelter of the lotus feet of Srila Prabhupada, haha means to take initiation from Srila Prabhupada. ### Diksha Guru, Functions, Characteristics
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=1136
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So actually, by the GBC's own rules and laws, Srila Prabhupada is acting as the diksha guru for all the devotees in ISKCON. And they are affirming that by singing Sri Guru Vandana for Srila Prabhupada day in and day out."
**Summary:** The speaker outlines the functions and characteristics of a Diksha Guru and argues that the GBC's own rules and laws, by mandating the Sri Guru Vandana for Srila Prabhupada, affirm that Srila Prabhupada is acting as the Diksha Guru for all ISKCON devotees. He questions the role and functions of the unauthorized diksha gurus.

Isn't this incongruous? Why this confusion? Actually, if we see the functions and characteristics of a diksha guru, what are they? The functions and characteristics of a diksha guru are, number one, he is a shiksha guru. Number two, he is a shiksha guru. Number three, he is a pure devotee. Number two, to give shelter to the disciple by initiating him or her. Number three, to remove all the sinful reactions of the disciple, kuryatpapasesankshayam. Number four, to reveal transcendental knowledge within the heart of the disciple, divyam jnanam yathodadyat. And then, number five, to be the spiritual master for the disciple, life after life, eternally. And number six, to be the spiritual master for the disciple, life after life, eternally. And number six, to be the spiritual master for the disciple, to act as the manifestation of the mercy of Krishna. All these are functions of the diksha guru. And what does the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai group do? They have mandated that all the initiated devotees in ISKCON, irrespective of who their diksha guru is, they should sing daily to Srila Prabhupada all these aspects, which are meant actually for the diksha guru. And simultaneously, they have prohibited the disciples from singing the same thing to their so-called diksha gurus, except for one day in a year. And that too, optionally, not mandated. So actually, by the GBC's own rules and laws, Srila Prabhupada is acting as the diksha guru for all the devotees in ISKCON. And they are affirming that by singing Sri Guru Vandana for Srila Prabhupada day in and day out. Well, if Srila Prabhupada is performing the diksha guru for all the devotees in ISKCON, they are performing the all these functions of a diksha guru. Then, what are the unauthorized diksha gurus doing in ISKCON? What are they doing? What are their functions towards their disciples? What are their characteristics? Do they really do anything for their disciples at all? Or do they simply give a mala and a name in a ceremony and that's it? But that is what a Rithvik does, not what a diksha guru does. So, do the unauthorized gurus perform all these functions of a diksha guru?

---

## Who Initiated Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur? | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### guru, initiation, official guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=0

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

Who initiated Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur and who initiated Srila Gaukishor Das Babaji Maharaj? In the Vedic culture, the father, the sanyasi, and the brahmana are automatically given the respect and position of a spiritual master. But when such a natural spiritual master turns out to be an atheist like Hiranyakashipu, then he is nothing more than an official guru or a pseudo guru. The real deeksha or initiation involves a bona fide guru and surrender from the disciple.

### natural guru, official guru, atheist, Hiranyakashipu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=155
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When such a natural spiritual master turns out to be an atheist like Hiranyakashipu, then he is nothing more than an official guru or a judo guru."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept of the natural spiritual master, explaining that if such a figure becomes an atheist, they are reduced to an official or pseudo guru, using Hiranyakashipu as an example.

This means that in the Vedic culture, the father, the sanyasi, and the brahmana are automatically given the respect and position of a spiritual master because they are all expected to play that role of a spiritual master. It is expected that they are qualified as spiritual masters. But when such a natural spiritual master turns out to be an atheist like Hiranyakashipu, then he is nothing more than an official guru or a judo guru.

### guru, demon, jnata-guru, loki guru, paramarthi guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=189
**Verse References:** SB 7.9.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We have accepted somebody as guru, but he's a demon. You should be rejected immediately."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada on rejecting a guru who is a demon and discusses the system of jnata-guru (hereditary guru), contrasting it with a paramarthi guru (spiritually advanced guru) as recommended by Jiva Goswami.

In another lecture on Srimad Bhagavatam, 7th canto, 9th chapter, verse number 14, recorded on 21st of February in 1976, Srila Prabhupada says, we have accepted somebody as guru, but he's a demon. You should be rejected immediately. Immediately. Just like in India, there is a system of jnata-guru, the guru by car. By family-wise, they become guru. One family is attached to another guru family by hereditary rules, the sons after sons accepting guru, the other party, and there is regular business. So such kind of guru is not wanted. See, Jiva Goswami has recommended that loki guru, this is loki guru. Parityagela adhyatmik paramarthi guru accepted. Guru has to be accepted who is actually paramarthi, advanced in spiritual knowledge, not the formality. That is seconded by Jiva Goswami.

### official guru, laukika guru, caste guru, hereditary guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=288
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sometimes a caste guru says, You are a caste guru."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to discuss official, judo, laukika, and hereditary gurus, quoting Srila Prabhupada from the Chaitanya Charitamrita on the topic of caste gurus and the Vaishnava rejection of hereditary considerations.

So here, Srila Prabhupada is mentioning another example of an official, or a judo guru, or a laukika guru. A guru who is accepted according to hereditary rules, which are simply material rules. This type of hereditary guru is mentioned by Srila Prabhupada in the Chaitanya Charitamrita also. There in the Madhya Lila, Chapter 8, verse number 128, purport, Srila Prabhupada says, sometimes a caste guru says, You are a caste guru. That ye krsna-tattva-veitase guru-haya means that one who is not a brahmana may become a siksa-guru or a vartma-pradarsaka-guru, but not an initiator-guru. According to such caste gurus, birth and family ties are considered foremost. However, the hereditary consideration is not acceptable to Vaishnavas.

### official guru, initiation, surrender, diksha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=376
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The official initiation has no meaning. Unless one was fully surrendered to guru, there is no question of initiation."
**Summary:** The speaker summarizes Srila Prabhupada's statements on official gurus and initiations, emphasizing that real initiation requires surrender from the disciple and that official initiation is ineffective.

According to caste, etc., these are all not real spiritual masters. And initiation taken from such gurus is only official initiation and is not the real initiation. Such official initiation is not effective. Initiation or diksa may become official either if the guru is an official guru or judo guru or if the disciple is not committed to the diksa. Srila Prabhupada in a lecture on the Srimad-Bhagavatam, 1st canto, 2nd chapter, verse number 20, recorded on 24th of July, 1974, says, The official initiation has no meaning. Unless one was fully surrendered to guru, there is no question of initiation. Dibhagyana hrde prakasi. Dibhagyana means transcendental knowledge. So, to play tricks with guru, to become diplomat and introvert, to become a guru, to become a leader, these rascals will not help. You can get some other things, some material profit, but spiritual life will be spoiled.

### diksha, surrender, bona fide guru, preceptor, acharya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=456
**Verse References:** SB 1.1.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The real heart of initiation or diksa is surrender by the disciple."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates the importance of surrender in diksha and defines the real initiation as involving a bona fide guru, contrasting it with official or judo gurus. He then introduces the term 'preceptor' as used by Srila Prabhupada.

This means that the real heart of initiation or diksa is surrender by the disciple. Therefore, the real diksa or initiation involves a bona fide guru, not an official or judo guru. And without these two things, the initiation is only official initiation, which is meaningless. Now we have understood what is meant by official guru and official initiation. We have also seen that the surrender of the disciple is what really constitutes initiation. Next, let us look at one more terminology that Srila Prabhupada uses, which is preceptor. In the Srimad-Bhagavatam, purport to 1st canto, 1st chapter, verse 5, Srila Prabhupada says, Sri Vyasadeva is the original spiritual preceptor for all men, and all other preceptors are considered to be his representatives. Obviously, here Srila Prabhupada says spiritual preceptor or preceptor to be his representative. It is important to understand this principle.

### acharya, preceptor, guru parampara, disciplic succession
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=530
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.15, SB 3.19.32
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Someone who is in the Guru Parampara."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that Srila Prabhupada uses the term 'preceptor' as an equivalent to 'acharya' and emphasizes the importance of receiving the transcendental message through disciplic succession (parampara).

He says, to mean the Acharya or spiritual master like Vyasa Deva. That means someone who is in the Guru Parampara. Again, in Srimad Bhagavatam, purport to Canto 1, chapter 13, verse 15, Srila Prabhupada mentions about Vidura. He was quite competent to occupy the post of an Acharya or spiritual preceptor. Here also, Srila Prabhupada uses the word spiritual preceptor as an equivalent of Acharya. Again, in the purport to Srimad Bhagavatam, 3rd Canto, 19th chapter, 32nd verse, Srila Prabhupada again uses the word preceptor in the same sense of Acharya. So, there Srila Prabhupada says, Thus, he accepted as bona fide. The system of Parampara or receiving the transcendental message in disciplic succession. Unless received by this bona fide process of hearing from a spiritual master, the statement of an Acharya or preceptor cannot be valid. So, like this, in many places, we can see, Srila Prabhupada uses the word preceptor as an equivalent of an Acharya.

### Bhaktivinoda Thakur, Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, siksha guru, diksha guru, official
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=621
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Thakur Bhaktivinoda was not the official spiritual master of Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to address the central question of the lecture, quoting a letter from Srila Prabhupada to Dayananda, which states that Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was not the official spiritual master of Srila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, but was treated as a preceptor due to his elevated spiritual understanding.

Now, let us come to what Srila Prabhupada says on the matter of initiation of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur and Srila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. In a letter that Srila Prabhupada wrote to Dayananda, written on 1st of May, 1969, Srila Prabhupada says, So far as your second question, Thakur Bhaktivinoda was not the official spiritual master of Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj was already renounced daughter, Paramahamsa. But, Thakur Bhaktivinoda, while he was even playing the part of a householder, was treated by Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj as preceptor, on account of his highly elevated spiritual understanding. And thus, he was always treating him as his spiritual master. The spiritual master is divided into two parts, namely, Shiksha Guru and Diksha Guru. So, officially, Bhaktivinoda Thakur was like Shiksha Guru. He was like the Shiksha Guru of Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. So here, Srila Prabhupada is clearly mentioning that Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was not the official, please underline, official spiritual master of Shila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. But, officially, again underline, officially, he was like his Shiksha Guru. Like his Shiksha Guru means, he was not actually Shiksha Guru.

---
### Bhaktivinoda Thakur, Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, siksha guru, diksha guru, preceptor, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=734
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj treated Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur as his Diksha Guru."
**Summary:** The speaker interprets Srila Prabhupada's statement, arguing that since Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was not really the siksha guru of Srila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, and Srila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj treated him as a preceptor, then Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur must have been his diksha guru.

From official point of view, that means in material point of view or from the point of view of custom or tradition that was prevalent in the society at that time, it appeared like Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was the Shiksha Guru of Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. This was only on the official front. But Prabhupada says Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj treated Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur as his preceptor, which means spiritual master or acharya, as we have seen how Shaila Prabhupada uses the word preceptor elsewhere, which means a Diksha Guru in the parampara or acharya. In other words, Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji. Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj treated Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur as his Diksha Guru. So, Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj surrendered to Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur, accepting him as his Diksha Guru, treating him as his Diksha Guru. Not only that, Shaila Prabhupada here mentions that spiritual master are of two kinds, Diksha Guru and Shiksha Guru. So, if Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was not really the Shiksha Guru of Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, then, because Prabhupada says officially he was like the Shiksha Guru, that means he was not a Shiksha Guru, but Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj accepted him as his preceptor. Then, what kind of spiritual master was Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur? Obviously, he was not the Shiksha Guru. So, then the only alternative left is that of Diksha Guru. So, Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was the Diksha Guru of Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, because Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj treated him like that. He accepted him like that. He surrendered to him like that.

---
### Bhaktivinoda Thakur, Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj, initiation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=851
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur initiated Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada from the Chaitanya Charitamrita and Back to Godhead, stating that Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur initiated Srila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, and that Srila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj initiated Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur.

In the Chaitanya Charita Amrita, Adi Lila, Chapter 1 Summary, Shaila Prabhupada says this, Shaila Vishwanatha Chakravarti Thakur accepted Shaila Jagannath Das Babaji, who initiated Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur, who in turn initiated Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji. So, here, Shaila Prabhupada clearly mentions, unequivocally, that Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur initiated Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. In the same vein, Shaila Prabhupada here says that Shaila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj also initiated Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur. And this is confirmed by Shaila Prabhupada in the March 20, 1960 edition of Back to Godhead, Volume 3, Part 16, where Prabhupada says, Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was initiated and Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji, the spiritual master of Om Vishnupad, Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Prabhupada, the divine spiritual master of our humble self. Again, here also, Shaila Prabhupada says that Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was initiated by Shaila Jagannath Das Babaji.

---
### diksha guru, official, spiritual consideration, Jiva Goswami
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=938
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Such official Diksha has no relevance, no meaning in spiritual life and therefore such official Diksha Gurus find no mention in the teachings of Srila Prabhupada."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes that from a spiritual perspective, Srila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj was indeed the diksha guru of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur, and Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was indeed the diksha guru of Srila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, emphasizing that official diksha based on material considerations is irrelevant and should be rejected, quoting Srila Jiva Goswami.

So, from all of this, it is clear that officially or from material point of view, in terms of maybe social custom or tradition or hereditary practices or caste or whatever practices were prevalent in the society at that time, Shaila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj might not have appeared to be the diksha guru of Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur. And Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur... In turn, might not have appeared to be the diksha guru of Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj in the eyes of the material people. But from spiritual consideration, Shaila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj was indeed the diksha guru of Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur and Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was indeed the diksha guru of Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. This... is the conclusion we can draw. The official Diksha Guru, the Diksha Guru accepted according to hereditary customs or traditions or whatever. The official Diksha Guru of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur, who may have been accepted in terms of social customs, tradition, hereditary practices, caste or any other material reason, whatever it is. It might have been someone else, not Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj. But for all practical purposes, Srila Prabhupada quotes Srila Jeev Goswami to emphasize that such official Diksha has no relevance, no meaning in spiritual life and therefore such official Diksha Gurus find no mention in the teachings of Srila Prabhupada about who were the official Gurus of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur or of Srila Gaur Keshavar Das Babaji Maharaj. Because they are simply not relevant and they have no meaning. So, in fact, Prabhupada says such official Diksha Gurus are to be rejected.

### ISKCON Mumbai, GBC, official diksha, Srila Prabhupada, sole diksha guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=1062
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy

As Srila Prabhupada quotes Srila Jeev Goswami, which brings us to the current unauthorized Guru system in the ISKCON Mumbai group. Their GBC has based their self-appointed Guru system on Srila Prabhupada. The Bhaktivinoda Thakur's official acceptance of Diksha from someone who was not an Acharya in our Parampara or who was not a pure devotee. So, the GBC quotes this to justify their stand that the Diksha Gurus in ISKCON need not be pure devotees. But as we have seen until now, such official Diksha Gurus are accepted. The Bhaktivinoda Thakur's official acceptance of Diksha Gurus from someone who was not an Acharya in our Parampara or who was not a pure devotee. So, the GBC quotes this to justify God by Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was Laukiki. It was only official and had no spiritual significance, no spiritual meaning. And Srila Prabhupada quoted Srila Jeevakaswami to say that such Laukika Guru is to be rejected. Parityagena. And a Paramarthika Adhyatmika Guru has to be accepted for Diksha. So this means that all of ISKCON Mumbai group's Diksha Gurus who are indicated no objection by the GBC are based on the official or Laukika Diksha Guru example of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur which means as per Srila Jeevakaswami, they are all to be rejected. Parityagena. All the Diksha Gurus in ISKCON are only official or Laukika Gurus. Then who is the Paramarthika Guru? Who is the Paramarthika Guru to be accepted as Diksha Guru in ISKCON? The answer is that is only His Divine Grace Vishwaguru Abhay Charanarvinda Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. He is the sole Diksha Guru of ISKCON as declared by Srila Prabhupada himself in his July 9th 1977 Directive which is followed by ISKCON Bangalore group. Vishwaguru Srila Prabhupada Ki Jai. Hare Krishna.
